In addition, once per scene, after making a casting roll, you may reroll one Bonus Die that failed to Burst and must keep the second result.

Fate is never free. Each time you use the reroll benefit, the GM gains one Fate Thread. At any later point in the session, the GM may spend that Fate Thread to impose 1 Challenge on one of your rolls or introduce a small but inconvenient twist of timing, coincidence, or omen.

Divine Agent. A minor herald, saintly attendant, archon-servitor, or emissary of some divine power has taken notice of you. It intercedes in moments of need, but it expects reverence, gratitude, and eventual alignment with its sacred will.

A number of times per day equal to your Soul Bonus Dice, the Divine Agent may perform one Basic Miracle on your behalf without requiring you to know that Miracle yourself. The Miracle must target you, an ally within 30 feet, or a creature you can clearly perceive within 30 feet. Use your Occult or appropriate casting roll as normal, and the effect still consumes its normal action unless otherwise stated, but it does not cost Mana or Grace.
If you consistently reject, mock, or act directly against the power it serves, the agent may withhold this gift until you make amends in a manner acceptable to the GM.

The unseen have taught you how to move where the world thins. While you are Destined, once per turn as an action, you may step through the spirit boundary and reappear anywhere within your movement distance that you can see. This movement cannot create an Opening and will bypass challenging terrain. Until the beginning of your next turn, you gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense or Resistance against spirits, summons, and spirit based effects.
You gain 1 additional Action on your turn.

As an action on your turn, you may attempt to draw upon Forbidden Mystery not for power, but for protection, add your Corruption Score to your Ward until the beginning of your next turn, rather than powering your arcane forces. You must still pay the price for this power.

You have opened yourself to something foul, ancient, or ravenous, and it has answered. Whether by pact, blasphemous study, inherited stain, or simple desperation, corruption now has a recognizable shape within you. Choose one of the following Vile Powers. Once you make this selection, it is permanent and cannot be changed.
Grave Hunger. Something sepulchral and starving now peers through your flesh. It does not eat food. It eats endings, pain, and the weakness of the living.

Once per turn, when you cast a spell, or use an occult ability that deals Umbral or Entropic damage, you may add +1 Bonus Die to its damage or effect. If the target is already Wounded, you instead add +2 Bonus Dice.

Whenever you use this benefit, you lose 1d6 Durability. If you have no Durability remaining, the loss comes from Health instead. Minor physical signs of deathly blight begin to gather around you: cold breath, dimmed eyes, grave-smell, still shadows, or similar manifestations.

Cursetongue. You have learned to speak the oldest of curses, the foul words of unmaking and corruption from the depths beyond the outer gates. When you inflict a curse, hex, fear effect, confusion, enthrallment, or any occult penalty that imposes Challenges, increase that penalty by 1 additional Challenge or increase its duration by 1 round, your choice.

However, spite seeks a mouth. If you go a full day without using a hostile spell, curse, hex, or spiritually coercive ability on another living creature, you suffer 1 Challenge on all Occult rolls until you do.

Profane Spark. A shard of destructive blasphemy burns within you like black lightning. It does not reason. It erupts. Choose one damage type when you take this talent: Fire, Lightning, Umbral, Entropic, or Corrosive. Once per turn, when you cast a spell or occult ability that deals damage, you may change its damage type to the chosen type. If it already dealt that type, add 1 Charge to its damage Bonus Dice instead.

Each time you use this benefit, roll 1d6. On a 1, the spark lashes out: you, one adjacent ally, or one unattended object within 10 feet suffers damage equal to one die of the spell’s damage rank. The GM chooses the target if the situation is unclear.

Blasphemous Insight. You have stared too long into truths that were not meant to be understood whole. Some part of you broke. Some part of you woke. You gain +1 Bonus Die to all Occult checks involving spirits, pacts, curses, corruption, infernal beings, the dead, forbidden rituals, or eldritch symbols. In addition, as an action on your turn you may consume your Destined state after seeing a spell, miracle, curse, summon, or occult phenomenon used within 60 feet, and may ask the GM one of the following and receive a truthful answer:

items: What is its weakest point?
What sustains it?
What is it truly calling upon?
How could it be broken, resisted, or turned?

blocks.html: You have achieved the final understanding of mysteries. Choose one of the following:

items: Perfect a Mystery: Upgrade a Major Mystery you possess to its Perfect tier. The Mystery's passive and active effects reach their fullest expression.
Deepen a Mystery: Upgrade a Minor Mystery you possess by to the Major tier.
Acquire Final Mystery: Choose a new Mystery from the catalog and gain it at Minor tier.

blocks.html: Your spirit companion has learned to bear the burden of power in your stead. When you cast a spell that has a duration of concentration, your Spirit Focus may hold that spell for you for a number of turns equal to your Soul Bonus Dice. Your Spirit Focus can only maintain one such magic at any given time. If it is dismissed, suppressed, or moved beyond the range required by your Spirit Focus, the effect ends immediately.

You have learned to loosen your odic form from your flesh and move as a spirit apart from your mortal shell. With one minute of mediation to project your Spiritual Body from your physical form for a number of minutes equal to your Soul Bonus Dice.
While projected:

items: your body is unconscious, helpless, and unaware,
your Spiritual Body may fly at your normal movement speed,
you may pass through mundane barriers no thicker than 5 feet,
you may perceive spirits, curses, wards, invisible magical effects, and incorporeal beings as though plainly visible,
and you may not make weapon attacks or manipulate mundane objects.

blocks.html: You may cast spells and use occult abilities while projected, but all such effects suffer 1 Challenge unless they specifically target spirits, wards, curses, summoned beings, or other spiritual phenomena. If your Spiritual Body is forcibly returned, disrupted, or destroyed, you immediately return to your body and suffer 1 rank of Fatigue.

Corruption has worked so deeply into your being that certain forms of ruin no longer find easy purchase in you. You gain Heroic Durability against one of the following damage types, chosen when you take this talent: Toxic, Corrosive, or Entropic.

Only Epic or better damage of the chosen type can damage you normally. Lesser damage of that type is ignored entirely. In addition, whenever you would suffer a condition caused primarily by your chosen damage type, you gain +2 Bonus Dice on the Resistance roll to resist or end that condition.
However, your flesh and spirit now unmistakably bear the signs of profane adaptation. Holy, sanctified, or life-aligned beings can sense that something in you has gone badly wrong.

You have learned the darkest economy of power: life rendered into occult force. By ritually sacrificing a living creature that is helpless, willing, bound, or otherwise wholly in your power, you may invoke Forbidden Mystery without paying its normal Durability, Health, or Vigor costs. Instead, the sacrificed creature is slain and you immediately gain the following benefits:

items: you receive the full chosen boon of Forbidden Mystery,
you recover Mana equal to 5 times the creature’s Soul Bonus Dice, minimum 5,
and you recover Vigor equal to 5 times the creature’s Stamina Bonus Dice, minimum 5.

blocks.html: You cannot exceed your maximum Mana or Vigor through this recovery.

Each Mystery is presented at its three tiers — Minor, Major, and Perfect. The Spiritualist gains a Mystery at Minor tier when first selected; subsequent talent purchases may upgrade the Mystery to Major or Perfect. A Spiritualist may carry multiple Mysteries simultaneously, but most upgrade their First Mystery as far as possible before branching into new ones.
Animals are not what most people think they are. They have memories, preferences, and grievances, and they will share them if asked properly. The asking is the work.

Minor. You may speak with animals — communicate simple ideas, requests, and observations in either direction. Animals understand you; you understand their general dispositions and recent experiences. You gain +1 BD to Beast Handling checks.

Major. Your communication with beasts is now nuanced; animals can convey complex memories, locations, and identifying features of people they have encountered. Once per scene, you may beseech an animal of Basic tier or lower to perform a single complex task on your behalf — scout an area, deliver a message, distract an enemy. You also gain a Basic-tier Animal Companion (a hawk, dog, small cat, or similar) who travels with you and follows your direction.

Perfect. You may speak with all natural creatures — beasts, birds, fish, reptiles — at the depth of complex conversation. Your Animal Companion advances to Novice tier and gains a small magical attribute (faster movement, modest aura sense, mild resistance to one element). Once per day, you may call on the local animal life to aid you: every natural creature within 1 mile becomes aware of your need and may, at the GM's discretion, contribute information, presence, or limited action toward what you require.
Names are not what they appear. The name on the deed is not the name on the soul. The Bound-Name Spiritualist has learned which names matter, and how to use them.

Minor. You perceive the true names of bound spirits, contracted entities, and similar ethereal beings within 60 feet. You gain +1 BD to Social checks when dealing with summoned, bound, or contracted creatures, and you may not be deceived about the identity of such entities.

Major. Once per scene, you may invoke a creature's True Name to compel them to answer one question truthfully or refrain from a single action for 1 round. The target may resist with a Resistance (Soul) check at 1 Challenge. Bound, contracted, summoned, or pacted creatures suffer 2 Challenges on the resistance instead.

Perfect. Once per long rest, you may invoke the Sealed Name — a deeper command directed at a creature whose True Name you possess. The target must obey a single specific instruction (one sentence's worth, GM-adjudicated) without resistance, regardless of their tier or alignment. Using the Sealed Name marks you to that creature's notice forever; they will know who spoke their name, and they will remember.
The dead and the recently departed are not so far from you as they seem. Your eyes have learned the trick of looking past the moment of death, and what looks back is not always silent.

Minor. You see ghosts, lingering souls, and ethereal beings within 30 feet as if solid. You may attempt to communicate with the recently dead (within 24 hours of death) by touching their remains; they answer briefly and as they are able.

Major. Ghost-sight extends to 100 feet. You may communicate freely with cooperative ghosts and lingering souls without ritual. Once per scene, you may compel a hostile ghost or lingering soul to answer a single true question, on a successful Soul-vs-Soul check.

Perfect. Once per long rest, you may perform a 10-minute ritual to summon the spirit of a named deceased person, provided their remains are accessible or their name is genuinely known. The summoned spirit is bound to honest answer for 1 hour, after which it returns to its rest. Hostile or unwilling spirits resist; you make a Soul check at 2 Challenges to compel them. While ritually summoned, the spirit may, at its discretion, manifest physically and act as a Novice-tier ally for the duration.
Devotion to a sworn divine power awakens the radiant Grace within you. The Mana of mortal cultivation is replaced with a different current entirely — one that comes from above, not within.

Minor. Your Mana is replaced with Grace, which functions identically to Mana except that it recovers through prayer, devotion, or sacred rest (not ordinary rest), and may be used to cast Divine Magic. You may not maintain Mana while you have Grace. You learn 2 Basic-rank Divine Spells of your choice (typical examples: Heal, Bless, Lux, Sanctuary, Cleanse).

Major. You gain access to Novice-rank Divine Spells. Your maximum Grace increases by ½ your Soul Score (round down). Once per long rest, you may invoke a Greater Blessing on an ally: until your next long rest, the blessed creature gains +1 BD to one chosen skill or ability score check.

Perfect. You gain access to Expert-rank Divine Spells. Once per scene, you may perform an Intercession: when an ally within 30 feet would suffer a Wound or be reduced to 0 Health, you may spend 20 Grace, and they instead remain at 1 Health and the Wound's severity is reduced by one rank. Your maximum Grace increases by an additional ½ your Soul Score (round down).

Some Spiritualists do not heal, do not bless, do not commune. They mark. The Hexer's craft is the laying of small bindings of misfortune on those who have crossed them, deepened by practice into curses that follow people across stories.

Minor. As a Bonus Action, mark a target you can see within 60 feet (Hex). Until the end of your next turn, attacks against the Hexed target gain +1 BD on the first attack roll, and the target suffers 1 Challenge on their next Resistance check. You may have one Hexed target at a time. Hexes consume nothing — they are spoken or thought into being.

Major. Hexes now last for the entire scene. The +1 BD applies to all attacks against the Hexed target, not just the first. A Hexed target also suffers 1 Challenge on Defense reactions while marked. You may have two Hexed targets at a time.

Perfect. Hexes you lay become genuine curses. Once per scene when you Hex a target, you may also impose one of the following lingering effects on them for the duration of the scene: Misfortune (they automatically fail their next critical roll), Slowing (their movement is halved), Whispered Doubts (they suffer 2 Challenges on social and Awareness checks), or Marked for Severance (severing the Hex requires an active counter-magic working). You may have three Hexed targets at a time. Once per long rest, you may make a Hex permanent until specifically removed by Severance, blessing, or sanctification — this Hex follows the target between sessions and gnaws at them.
Some questions answer themselves if asked properly. The Open Eye Spiritualist has learned to ask.

Minor. Once per day, ask the GM a single question about a person, place, event, or object within the campaign world. The GM provides a vision — cryptic or clear, as appropriate to the subject and the GM's judgment — that gives you useful but typically incomplete information.

Major. Open Eye may be invoked twice per day. Visions become clearer and more detailed; the GM is encouraged to provide specific names, locations, or sensory details rather than purely metaphorical guidance. Additionally, once per scene you may ask the GM whether a specific course of action you are considering would lead to a known catastrophic consequence; the GM answers honestly to the best of the world's knowledge.

Perfect. Open Eye may be invoked three times per day. Once per long rest, you may petition for a True Vision — a half-hour meditative trance in which you see a specific event from the past, present, or near future of a chosen subject. The GM provides a substantive scene-length vision that should genuinely inform your decisions.
You see what is there, even when others do not. The cultivated, the magical, the warded, the bound. The architecture of power is not hidden from you.

Minor. You perceive Aura Stars, Mana Circles, active spells, wards, and similar cultivation markers in others within 60 feet. Gain +1 BD to Awareness checks regarding magical states, cultivation tier, and active workings.

Major. Your perception extends to invisible and ethereal beings within 30 feet. You may identify the specific spell or technique another creature is casting at the moment of casting, and gain +1 BD to Counterspell-equivalent reactions. Once per scene, you may immediately know whether a creature, object, or location is currently affected by hostile magic.

Perfect. Your perception of the unseen is comprehensive. You see all magic, all cultivation, all bindings, all corruption, all wards, and all spirits within 60 feet without check. Once per long rest, you may perform a 1-minute ritual to read the recent magical history of a place, person, or object — what spells, curses, or bindings have touched them in the last 30 days, and by whom.

Some Spiritualists carry the Wyrd in their bones — a small thread of prescience that lets them see the choices about to be made before the dice are rolled. The Wyrd does not speak in clear sentences; it speaks in nudges, in pulled threads, in the sudden conviction that something must be done now.

Minor. Once per scene, after any creature within 30 feet rolls an Action check or Defense reaction but before the result is determined, you may declare the roll Wyrd-Touched: roll 1d4 and add it as a Bonus Die to the result.

Major. Wyrd Sight may be invoked twice per scene. The Bonus Die is now 1d6. You also gain the passive ability to sense major fate-points approaching: once per session, the GM tells you when a significant decision or event is imminent before you must commit to action.

Perfect. Wyrd Sight may be invoked three times per scene. The Bonus Die is now 1d8. Once per long rest, you may declare a single roll (yours or another's within 30 feet) Bound by the Threads — they may roll the dice twice and choose the better result, before adding any Bonus Dice. You also see fate-points approaching with greater clarity, and the GM may share specific warnings about the next 24 hours of in-fiction events.

Where Ordinary classes establish the cultivator's chassis, Heroic classes are the apex of specialization within a single craft. A Heroic class takes one mechanic — one signature, one defining truth — and develops it past the point where any Ordinary practitioner could follow. Heroic classes are reached not through narrative arc alone, but through the achievement of specific cultivation, skill, and ability thresholds: a Heroic is earned, never granted.

Heroic classes have engine-and-skill prerequisites only. They are entirely class-agnostic: anyone who meets the requirements may take the Heroic class regardless of which Ordinary class they originated from. A Spiritualist who cultivated their Aura through Vyana Awakening can take Knight if they also have the necessary martial training. A Rogue who deepened their Awakened Edge may take Guardian if they have the right defensive grounding. The Heroic class does not ask where you came from; it asks only what you have built.
The cultivator of the cut. The bearer of the radiant edge. The one whose weapon is not held but inhabited.

Knights are the masters of the Aura Blade — the technique that compresses cultivated Aura into the length of a held weapon and turns the implement into a conduit for the wielder's full martial power. Where Ordinary martials learn to ignite their Aura through their bodies, Knights learn to project it through steel. A Knight's signature weapon is not merely a tool; it is the focused expression of their cultivated self, and a fully manifested Aura Blade can cleave through wards, sever spells, and cut what other weapons could not touch.

The Knight's craft demands both martial discipline and the spiritual cultivation that makes Aura usable. Knights are recognized by sight: a manifested Aura Blade glows visibly along the weapon's edge, and a trained eye can read the Knight's cultivation tier from the intensity, color, and stability of the projection. Veteran Knights are known to dispute over the architecture of their Aura Blades the way scholars dispute over text: there are schools, lineages, and signature styles that pass from master to student across generations.
To enter the Knight Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Strength Score of 15 or higher.
Stamina Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Light Weapons or Heavy Weapons.
Novice rank in Defense.
At least 2 Aura Stars from any source (Vyana counts as Aura Stars for this requirement).

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Aura Blade.

blocks.text: Knight Elite Tree: The Manifest Edge

blocks.html: To inhabit the weapon is the first lesson of the Knight. Every Star you ignite must travel through the steel and emerge as the cut. The Knight who keeps their Aura in their body is half a Knight; the Knight whose Aura lives in the blade has begun the true work.

Often considered the prerequisite of knighthood, the Aura Blade is the method of compressing and surrounding your weapon with cultivated Aura. As an Action, you may spend 10 Mana plus 5 Mana per Star you wish to activate (minimum 15 Mana), to surround a held weapon or your fists with Aura. The effect lasts 1 minute (4 turns) per Aura Star you possess.

For the duration, weapon attacks and weapon techniques performed with the empowered weapon gain +1 Bonus Die of damage per Aura Star applied to the Blade. The Blade is visible to all who can see the weapon — a luminous coronal sheath that follows the line of the steel — and remains active even if you sheathe or release the weapon (provided you reclaim it before the duration ends).
If your Aura Blade is dispelled, your weapon broken, or you are reduced to 0 Mana, the Blade ends immediately. You may dismiss your Aura Blade as a Free Action on your turn.

Your Aura Blade has matured beyond the bound of the weapon's edge. An additional Star ignites within your Aura Core, and your Aura Blade extends past the steel — Aura projects from the point or edge of the weapon as a near-tangible second length.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Many Knight techniques and abilities scale with the size of your Aura Core.

While your Aura Blade is active, the empowered weapon's effective reach increases by 5 feet per 2 Aura Stars applied to the Blade (round down, minimum +5 feet). This extended reach is genuine — you may strike, parry, or threaten enemies within the extended distance as if the weapon physically reached them. The reach effect ends when the Aura Blade ends.
Choose from the following three talents. These may be purchased in any order as Skill Points allow.

Your Aura Blade has learned to carry through. When you make a successful weapon attack with your Aura Blade and the attack reduces the target to 0 Health or breaks through their Ward, you may immediately make a second weapon attack against an adjacent enemy as part of the same Action. The second attack inherits all benefits of your Aura Blade and any active stance or technique.

Additionally, when one or more of your Bonus Dice Burst on a basic attack with the empowered weapon, you may declare the strike a Cleaving Cut: the attack ignores 1 point of Armor and inflicts the Bleeding condition for 2 rounds on a hit.

Your Aura Blade has learned to defend as readily as it strikes. While your Aura Blade is active, you may use the empowered weapon to Deflect attacks from any direction within the weapon's extended reach — including ranged attacks, spell projectiles, and area-effect strikes that originate from a point you can see. Each Aura Star applied to your Blade grants +1 Bonus Die to Deflect reactions made with the empowered weapon.

Once per round when you successfully Deflect an attack with your Aura Blade, you may immediately make a basic weapon attack against the deflecting opponent at no Action cost, provided they are within your weapon's reach.

Your Aura Blade has learned to endure. While your Aura Blade is active, you may spend 5 Mana at the start of each of your turns to extend its duration by an additional turn beyond its normal end. The extension does not consume an Action and may continue as long as you have Mana to spend.

Additionally, when your Aura Blade is dispelled by hostile magic or environmental effect, you may spend 10 Mana as a Reaction to immediately reignite it at its current Star investment, without paying the full Mana cost again.
Choose from the following three talents. The middle talent grants an additional Aura Star.

Your Aura Blade has learned to undo what magic and craft have made. When you make a successful weapon attack with your Aura Blade against a target sustaining a magical effect, ward, Aura technique, or active enchantment, you may spend 1 Charge of weapon damage to attempt to Sunder one such effect.

On a successful attack, make an opposed roll using your Aura Stars against the effect's caster level or the rank of the effect. On success, the targeted effect is suppressed for 1 round; on a Critical success, it is destroyed entirely. Wards and barriers Sundered by this attack do not regenerate within the scene. Aura Blades, Aura Shields, and similar cultivation manifestations are valid Sundering targets.
Through deepening cultivation and the seasoning of battle, another Star ignites within your Aura Core.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Add +1 Bonus Die to the damage or effects of aura-empowered techniques. Your Aura Blade's duration extends by an additional minute (4 turns) per Star at this and all prior tiers, and its damage scaling improves accordingly.

Your Aura Blade has learned to answer. While your Aura Blade is active and you are wielding a weapon with the Defending or Parrying feature, when you successfully Deflect an attack, the deflecting strike returns to the attacker as an immediate Reaction attack — using your Aura Blade as the weapon, applying all current Aura Star bonuses, but rolling against the original attacker's Defense rather than the original target's.
The Riposte attack costs no Action and may be made once per round. If the original attack was a critical hit and you successfully Deflected it, the Riposte attack deals +2 Bonus Dice of damage.
Choose from the following three transformative capstones. Each fundamentally changes how your Aura Blade operates.

Your Aura Blade is no longer merely an extension of your weapon — it is your weapon's true self, with the steel as scaffold. Once per long rest, as a Bonus Action, you may declare Aura Form: for 1 minute per Aura Star, your weapon's physical form is replaced entirely with concentrated Aura, holding the shape of the weapon as a manifest expression of your cultivated power.

While in Aura Form, your weapon cannot be sundered, disarmed, or broken by any means short of equivalent Aura craft. You may dismiss and re-summon the weapon as a Free Action, with the weapon reappearing in your hand from any distance. The weapon's damage type is treated as Force (in addition to any normal damage type), and the weapon is treated as having Heroic-rank inherent magical properties for the duration.

When Aura Form ends, your weapon resumes its physical form unharmed (or, if previously destroyed, is restored to its full physical state at this moment). This capstone may be the only known way for a Knight to wield a weapon they no longer physically possess.

Your Aura Blade has reached the apex of its sundering work. Once per long rest, you may declare your Aura Blade a Dispel Edge: for 1 minute per Aura Star, every successful hit with the empowered weapon automatically Sunders one magical effect, ward, Aura technique, or active enchantment on the target, without requiring a roll. You choose which effect is Sundered on each hit; effects of equivalent or lower tier than your Aura Blade are destroyed outright, while higher-tier effects are suppressed for 1 round.

Additionally, while this capstone is active, your Aura Blade itself cannot be Sundered, Dispelled, or Counterspelled by any means. The Dispel Edge persists through 0 Mana — once activated, the duration runs to completion regardless of your subsequent resource state.

Your Aura Blade has learned to share itself. Once per long rest, you may declare Aura Resonance: for 1 minute per Aura Star, every successful weapon hit with your Aura Blade produces a resonant echo — a second strike that automatically targets the nearest enemy within 10 feet of the original target, dealing damage equal to half the original strike's damage (round down).

Aura Resonance treats the echo as a weapon attack made with your Aura Blade for all purposes (damage type, Aura Star bonuses, Sundering interactions, etc.), but the echo costs no Action and creates no Opening. If multiple enemies are within range, the echo strikes the nearest; if multiple are equidistant, you choose. The echo may also bounce: if the second strike kills its target, it produces a tertiary echo against the next-nearest enemy within 10 feet, and so on, until no valid target remains. This chain ends when the original attack's duration ends, the resonating Aura Blade ends, or you dismiss the effect.
The cultivator of the wall. The bearer of the radiant shelter. The one whose body is the line that holds.

Guardians are the masters of the Aura Shield — the technique that projects cultivated Aura outward as a manifest defensive barrier. Where Knights compress their Aura into the weapon, Guardians release it outward into the air, where it absorbs incoming force and dissipates harm. A Guardian's signature Aura Shield is not the small kite of a Warrior's basic technique; it is a wall, sometimes a fortress, occasionally extended across an entire battlefield to shelter allies who could not stand without it.

The Guardian's craft is the inverse of the Knight's. Both work in cultivated Aura, but the Knight's discipline is offensive concentration while the Guardian's is defensive expansion. A Guardian's success is measured not in damage dealt but in damage prevented — in allies still standing, in ground held, in enemies broken against the patient wall of their cultivated Aura. Veteran Guardians develop reputations as immovable presences; battlefields are named for them, and sieges are decided by their absence.
To enter the Guardian Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Stamina Score of 15 or higher.
Resolve Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Defense.
Novice rank in Heavy Weapons or Novice rank in Unarmed.
At least 2 Aura Stars from any source (Vyana counts as Aura Stars for this requirement).

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Aura Shield.

blocks.text: Guardian Elite Tree: The Manifest Wall

blocks.html: To stand between is the first lesson of the Guardian. The Shield is not the thing you hold; it is the air that holds you, the ground that supports you, the Aura that makes the space around you a place worth defending. A Guardian who keeps their Aura in their body is a soldier; a Guardian whose Aura fills the air is a wall.

The signature work of the Guardian, the Aura Shield is the projection of cultivated Aura outward as a manifest defensive barrier. As a Reaction (when targeted by an attack or area effect) or as an Action (in preparation), you may spend 10 Mana plus 5 Mana per Star you wish to activate (minimum 15 Mana), to raise an Aura Shield around yourself.

The Shield absorbs incoming damage equal to 10 × the number of Aura Stars applied to its activation. A 3-Star Guardian's Aura Shield absorbs 30 damage before dissipating; a 5-Star Guardian's Shield absorbs 50. The Shield persists until its capacity is fully consumed or until end of combat, whichever comes first.

While an Aura Shield is active, you may as a Reaction interpose yourself for an adjacent ally targeted by an attack: take the attack yourself against the Shield's remaining capacity. Multiple Shields may not be active simultaneously — raising a new Shield dismisses any prior active Shield. You may not raise an Aura Shield while another Guardian's Shield is actively protecting you, or while you are within an Aegis (see capstones below).

Your Aura Shield has matured into a stance you may hold continuously, projecting outward to shelter those who stand near you. An additional Star ignites within your Aura Core, and your Aura Shield gains a sustained mode.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Many Guardian techniques and abilities scale with the size of your Aura Core.

Your Aura Shield gains a Bulwark Stance — a sustained mode you may activate as an Action while the Shield is raised, costing an additional 5 Mana per turn maintained. While in Bulwark Stance, your Aura Shield extends its protection to allies within 10 feet, granting them a portion of the Shield's absorption capacity. Each turn maintained, the Shield absorbs damage from any source within the protected radius, deducting from its total capacity rather than allowing it to strike the warded creature.

Bulwark Stance ends when its capacity is consumed, when you choose to stop maintaining it (Free Action), when you move more than your Speed in a single turn, or when you take any attack Action of your own.

Your Aura Shield has learned to return what strikes it. Once per round when an attack is absorbed by your Aura Shield, you may spend 5 Mana to redirect a portion of the absorbed energy back at the attacker as a Reaction. The reflected strike deals damage equal to half the damage absorbed (round down) and uses the original attack's damage type and properties (a reflected spell remains a spell; a reflected weapon strike remains a weapon strike).

The reflected attack uses the attacker's own Defense rating or Resistance for the resisting roll, not yours. If the attacker would normally be unable to be hit by their own attack (e.g., spell self-immunity, weapon resistance), the reflection deals half its normal damage instead of being negated.

Your Aura Shield has learned to leave you. Once per round as a Free Action, you may project your Aura Shield to a point within 60 feet that you can see, raising it around a chosen ally or unattended object at that location. The projected Shield functions exactly as if you had raised it yourself, with all current Star investment, except that you do not gain the personal-defense benefits while the Shield is projected.

You may recall your projected Shield as a Free Action; it returns to you at the start of your next turn, having absorbed whatever damage it intercepted while away. While the Shield is projected, you cannot raise a separate Shield around yourself, and the projected Shield's capacity does not increase from Mana spent on other Shield activations.

Your Aura Shield has learned to root itself. When you raise an Aura Shield while standing on solid ground, you may declare it a Pillar: the Shield becomes immobile, persisting at your current location even if you move. While the Pillar is active, you may move freely without dismissing it, and it continues to absorb damage from any attack passing through its space.

The Pillar's capacity is doubled (a 3-Star Pillar absorbs 60 damage) but its duration is limited to 1 minute per Aura Star at activation. The Pillar is visible as a coronal pillar of cultivated Aura rising from the ground, blocking line-of-sight and physical movement through its space for enemies (but not for allies). Multiple Guardians may not raise overlapping Pillars; each Pillar requires a unique location at least 10 feet from any other.

Your Aura Shield has learned to discharge. Once per round when you have an active Aura Shield, you may spend an Action to release the Shield's remaining capacity as an offensive blast — a burst of cultivated Aura that strikes all enemies within 15 feet of you. Each affected enemy takes damage equal to the Shield's remaining capacity, divided by the number of affected enemies (round down). The Shield is dismissed by this action.

Enemies struck by Sundering Shield make a Resistance check at 1 Challenge per Aura Star originally applied to the Shield. On a failed Resistance, the affected enemy is also knocked prone and pushed 5 feet away from you. On a critical failure, they suffer the Stunned condition until the end of their next turn.
Through deepening cultivation and the seasoning of battle, another Star ignites within your Aura Core.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Add +1 Bonus Die to the damage or effects of aura-empowered techniques. Your Aura Shield's capacity scales accordingly, and Bulwark Stance's protected radius extends to 15 feet.

Your Aura Shield has learned to be there always. Once per scene, as a Free Action at the start of your turn, you may declare your Shield Sustained: for 1 minute per Aura Star, your Aura Shield reforms at the start of each of your turns regardless of whether its capacity was consumed in the previous turn.

While Sustained Aegis is active, the Shield's reformation costs no Action and no Mana — your cultivated Aura supplies the energy directly from your reserves at no additional cost. When the duration ends, you suffer 1 rank of fatigue and may not raise an Aura Shield for 1 minute (4 turns) as your cultivated Aura recovers.
Choose from the following three transformative capstones.

Your Aura Shield has reached its apex expression — a sustained mobile barrier protecting all allies within a radius of you. Once per long rest, you may declare an Aegis: for 1 minute per Aura Star, every ally within 20 feet of you is protected by an extension of your Aura Shield, absorbing damage from any source as if the damage had struck your own Shield's capacity. The Aegis's total capacity is 20 × the number of Aura Stars active at declaration.

While the Aegis persists, you may not raise additional Shields, may not use Sundering Shield, and may not project Ranged Shields. You may, however, move freely; the Aegis follows you, and any ally who steps outside the 20-foot radius temporarily leaves the Aegis's protection until they re-enter. Enemies within the Aegis's radius cannot benefit from its protection but are not directly hindered.

When the Aegis ends, you suffer 1 rank of fatigue. The Aegis's protected allies remember the experience; in the moment of true peril, they have stood within a wall made of cultivated soul, and they know who built it.

Your Aura Shield has learned to break what would break it. While you have an active Aura Shield, when an attack that would deal damage equal to or greater than the Shield's full capacity strikes the Shield, you may spend a Reaction to reverse the attack's force entirely: the attacker suffers the full damage of their own strike, the Shield absorbs only the difference (or nothing if the attack exactly matches capacity), and the attack itself is dismissed before reaching its target.

This capstone may be used once per round, and the dismissed attack cannot be re-aimed at the same target this turn. Magical attacks dismissed in this way are unmade — their components are spent without effect, and the attacker is briefly aware that something turned their working back. Repeated Sundering Aegis against the same attacker over multiple rounds will produce in the attacker a recognizable mark: the small dread of having had their best work returned to them.

Your Aura Shield has rooted itself permanently. Once per long rest, you may declare yourself a Pillar: your Aura Shield activates at full capacity and extends 30 feet in all directions, persisting until you dismiss it (Free Action) or until you take an attack Action of your own. While in Pillar form, you cannot move (your feet have become part of the cultivated pillar of Aura), and your Aura Shield's capacity does not deplete — every absorbed attack is consumed by the Pillar's surrounding Aura without reducing the central Shield.

Within the Pillar's 30-foot radius, all allies treat the ground as Difficult Terrain only for enemies, and gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense reactions from sustained Pillar-resonance. Enemies entering the radius suffer 1 Challenge to attack rolls against you and against any ally within the Pillar's radius. The Pillar is treated as a magical structure of Heroic-rank inherent power for sundering and dispel purposes.

When you choose to step out of Pillar form, the Aura collapses into your body in a single radiant flash, and you regain 30 Mana from the unspent cultivated reserve. You may not raise another Aura Shield, Pillar, or Aegis for 1 minute (4 turns) afterward.
The cultivator of the distance. The bearer of the arrow that has not yet been fired. The one whose patience is the bow.

Striders are the masters of the Aura Bow — the technique that manifests cultivated Aura as a bow of pure radiant force, capable of firing arrows at distances and accuracies no physical archer can match. Where Knights compress their Aura into a held weapon and Guardians project it outward as a barrier, Striders shape their Aura into an instrument of distance: the arrow that finds the throat at four hundred yards, the strike that turns a battlefield without the Strider stepping onto it.

The Strider's craft is older than most martial traditions — a discipline that draws on hunting work, on patience cultivation, on the long apprenticeships of the wild places. Striders are recognized at distance: their drawn Aura Bow is visible as a coronal arc of cultivated light, and the arrows they fire trail short radiant tails that fade behind the shot. Veteran Striders have reputations that precede them, often by miles.
To enter the Strider Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Agility Score of 15 or higher.
Speed Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Ranged Weapons.
Novice rank in Awareness.
At least 2 Aura Stars from any source (Vyana counts as Aura Stars for this requirement).

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Aura Bow.

blocks.text: Strider Elite Tree: The Manifest Arrow

blocks.html: To find the mark is the first lesson of the Strider. The bow is not what fires the arrow; the bow is the shape your Aura takes when it has decided to travel. The arrow is not what reaches the target; the arrow is the mark of attention paid to a thing that did not yet know it was being watched.

The signature work of the Strider, the Aura Bow is the manifestation of cultivated Aura as a bow of pure radiant force. As an Action, you may spend 10 Mana plus 5 Mana per Star you wish to activate (minimum 15 Mana), to manifest an Aura Bow in your hands. The Bow lasts 1 minute (4 turns) per Aura Star applied.

While the Aura Bow is active, you may fire Aura Arrows as a basic ranged attack using Ranged Weapons skill. Each Arrow costs 2 Mana to manifest and may be fired as a Free Action (one Arrow per Attack Action). Arrows deal weapon damage equivalent to a bow of your Aura Star count's tier (1-Star: short bow; 2-Star: long bow; 3-Star: composite bow; 4+-Star: greatbow) plus +1 Bonus Die per Aura Star applied to the Bow. Arrow range is 60 feet per Aura Star applied, with no range penalty until the maximum distance is exceeded.

If your Aura Bow is dispelled or your Mana is reduced to 0, the Bow ends immediately. You may dismiss your Aura Bow as a Free Action on your turn. Aura Arrows are made of cultivated light and dissipate on impact or at end of the Bow's duration; they cannot be recovered as physical ammunition.
Your Aura Bow has matured into a tool of pursuit. An additional Star ignites within your Aura Core, and your Aura Bow gains the ability to mark a target as the focus of your attention.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Many Strider techniques and abilities scale with the size of your Aura Core.

As a Bonus Action on your turn, you may Mark a target you can see within your Aura Bow's range. The Mark persists until you Mark a different target, until the target is reduced to 0 Health, or until you choose to release the Mark (Free Action). While Marked, the target appears slightly luminous to your Aura-sight, and you gain the following benefits against them: +1 Bonus Die to ranged attack rolls; +1 Bonus Die to Awareness checks regarding the target's location, condition, and movement; and the target cannot benefit from cover or concealment when you fire at them with your Aura Bow.

Once per scene, when you Mark a target, you may invoke True Sight on them: the Mark reveals their current Durability, Health, and Mana totals, all active magical effects on them, and any specific vulnerabilities or resistances they possess.

Your Aura Bow has learned the discipline of the single shot. While your Aura Bow is active, you may spend 2 Action to take a Precision Shot at a Marked target: a single fired Arrow that automatically critical-hits on a successful attack roll (against the target's standard Defense), and which ignores armor and 1 rank of Ward.

Additionally, Precision Form lets you take aim against any target — Marked or not — by spending 1 Action to Aim before firing. An Aimed Shot gains +2 Bonus Dice to the attack roll and treats the target's cover as one rank reduced (full cover becomes half cover; half cover becomes none).

Your Aura Bow has learned the speed of repetition. While your Aura Bow is active, when you take an Attack Action, you may fire two Aura Arrows as part of the same Action, targeting the same or different enemies. Each Arrow rolls independently for hit and damage. The second Arrow costs an additional 2 Mana to manifest, and you may not benefit from Aim or Precision Form on either Arrow during a Volley turn.
Once per round, when one or more of your Bonus Dice Burst on a Volley attack, you may fire a third Arrow against any visible enemy within range as a Free Action, also using the empowered Aura Bow.

Your Aura Bow has learned to read the world for prey. While your Aura Bow is active, you gain a passive sense of all creatures within twice your normal Awareness range (typically 200 feet). You know their general direction, approximate health state, and whether they are aware of you. This awareness is genuine — fog, walls, magical concealment, and most environmental obstructions do not block it.

Additionally, you may track a Marked target across any distance for up to 24 hours after the Mark was placed, even after the Mark has been released. The tracking is automatic and reveals the target's general direction; with concentration (1 minute of focused stillness), you may pinpoint their exact location to within 60 feet.

Your Aura Bow has learned to lie about where you are. Once per round, when you fire an Aura Arrow at a Marked target, you may declare the shot a Phantom: the arrow does not appear to come from your position. The target and any creatures observing the strike perceive it as originating from any point within 30 feet of the target that you choose. You leave no visible trace; the arrow appears to emerge from empty space.

On a successful hit, the target makes an Awareness check at 2 Challenges to identify your true position. On a critical hit, the Mark on the target is preserved through the shot, and you may fire a second Phantom Shot against the same Marked target on your next turn as a Bonus Action.
Through deepening cultivation and the seasoning of battle, another Star ignites within your Aura Core.

You gain an additional Aura Star. Increase your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Add +1 Bonus Die to the damage or effects of aura-empowered techniques. Your Aura Bow's range and arrow damage scale accordingly; Marked targets gain the additional Star's worth of benefit to your shots against them.

Your Aura Bow has learned to follow what flees. Once per round when you fire an Aura Arrow at a Marked target who attempts to break line of sight or otherwise escape the shot, you may spend 5 Mana to declare it a Tracking Arrow: the Arrow follows the target through cover, around corners, and through any non-magical environmental obstruction up to a distance of 60 feet beyond the original shot's range.

The Tracking Arrow uses the original attack roll. If the target attempts to take an Action that would normally break line of sight (sprinting around a wall, ducking behind cover, teleporting up to short range), the Tracking Arrow makes a final attack roll at +2 Bonus Dice to interrupt the action as it completes. On a successful hit, the target is interrupted and any Action they were taking is wasted; on a Critical, the target is also Slowed for 1 round.
Choose from the following three transformative capstones.

Your Aura Bow has reached the apex of distance. Once per long rest, you may declare Killing Range: for 1 minute per Aura Star, your Aura Bow's effective range extends to any target you can perceive in any way — by direct sight, by Hunter's Sense, by Marked tracking, by spiritual perception, or by reliable secondhand description from someone who has seen the target within the last hour.

Aura Arrows fired during Killing Range travel along the path of cultivated Aura rather than through physical space — they take no time in transit, ignore all cover and physical obstruction (though they may still be Counterspelled or magically deflected), and strike the target the instant the attack roll succeeds. You may fire a number of Killing Range arrows per Killing Range invocation equal to your Aura Star count; each costs Mana as normal.

This capstone is the working of legend — the assassination that crossed three valleys, the shot that ended a battle without the Strider having been seen. Targets struck by a Killing Range arrow may not know who fired the shot or from where, even after death.

Your Aura Bow has learned to release everything at once. Once per long rest, you may declare a Storm: for 1 minute per Aura Star, every Attack Action with your Aura Bow releases a volley of Aura Arrows equal to your current Aura Star count, each independently targeting any enemy you can see within range. You may target the same enemy with multiple arrows, or distribute them across the battlefield. Each arrow rolls independently; each costs 2 Mana to manifest.

During Storm of Arrows, your Aura Bow does not require a discrete Aim or Volley action — every basic Attack is a Storm Volley. You may not, however, Mark new targets during the Storm; the working is too saturated with cultivated arrows to permit the focused attention a Mark requires.

Storm of Arrows may consume Mana quickly. A 5-Star Strider releasing one Storm Volley per turn over a 5-Star, 5-minute Storm fires up to 100 arrows — a sustained working that empties most reserves. The visual is unmistakable: the air around the Strider becomes a coronal rain of cultivated light, each Arrow trailing a fading tail.

Your Aura Bow has learned to strike past the body. Once per long rest, you may declare a Soul Shot: the next Aura Arrow you fire bypasses physical defenses entirely, striking the target's spirit directly. The Arrow ignores all Armor, all Ward, all natural and magical physical defenses, and all damage resistance other than direct Spirit-tier or Soul-tier protection. Damage is dealt to the target's Health pool, bypassing Durability.

Soul Shot deals damage equal to a basic Aura Bow shot plus your Aura Star count in Bonus Dice. On a successful hit, the target also suffers 1 Challenge on Resistance checks against your subsequent attacks for the remainder of the scene, as the Soul Shot has left a lingering wound in their spirit.

Targets that lack a soul — pure constructs, certain elementals, recently-arisen undead of certain tiers — are immune to Soul Shot's special effects but take normal Aura Bow damage from the strike. Spirits, undead with intact souls, and divine beings are particularly vulnerable: Soul Shot deals +2 Bonus Dice against such targets, and may unmake their spiritual existence on a critical hit (GM adjudication required for major beings).
The cultivator of the inner radiance. The bearer of the golden body. The one whose temple is the flesh, and whose flesh has become the prayer.

Ascetics are the masters of Vyana — the cultivated hybrid of Aura and spiritual essence that lights the body from within rather than from without. Where Knights project their Aura through a weapon and Guardians shape it into a barrier, Ascetics cultivate it inward, until the body itself is suffused with golden radiance and every breath is an act of cultivated power. The Ascetic's fists are weapons; their stance is a Shield; their meditation is a teaching others can feel from across the room.

The Ascetic's craft is the oldest of the inner cultivations, predating most formal martial schools. It is the path of monks, of wandering ascetics, of those who have learned that the body is itself the instrument and that no external weapon can match the cultivated self. Ascetics are recognized by the faint golden nimbus that hangs about them when their cultivation is active — a glow that intensifies in meditation, in combat, and in moments of profound emotional clarity. Veteran Ascetics walk visibly haloed, and their footsteps in deep snow leave no print.
To enter the Ascetic Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Stamina Score of 15 or higher.
Soul Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Unarmed.
Novice rank in Resistance.
At least 1 Vyana from any source.

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Vyana Awakening.

blocks.text: Ascetic Elite Tree: The Golden Body

blocks.html: To turn the cultivation inward is the first lesson of the Ascetic. The body is the temple, and the body is also the practice. Every breath cultivates; every step refines. The Ascetic whose Vyana lives only in moments of effort is half-cultivated; the Ascetic whose Vyana suffuses the body always has begun the true work.

The signature work of the Ascetic, Vyana Awakening is the cultivation of the body into a vessel for inner radiance. You ignite your first full Vyana — the hybrid cultivation of Aura and spiritual essence that makes your body itself a manifest expression of cultivated power.

Each Vyana you possess increases your maximum Mana by ½ your Soul Score (round down) and your Vigor by ½ your Stamina Score (round down). Vyana counts as Aura Stars for the purposes of all Aura Star prerequisites, Aura technique prerequisites, and cross-class qualification (with the exception that Vyana does not directly grant Aura Blade, Aura Shield, or Aura Bow — those are weapon-projection techniques learned through other paths).

Your unarmed strikes count as cultivated martial weapons, dealing 1d8 damage at Basic rank (rising with Vyana count: 1d10 at 3 Vyana, 1d12 at 5 Vyana). Unarmed strikes empowered by Vyana ignore non-magical armor by 1 point per Vyana you possess, and may target ethereal, spiritual, and incorporeal beings as if they were physical.

Your Vyana has matured into a continuous radiance about your body. An additional Vyana ignites within you, and your cultivated essence now expresses itself as a visible halo of golden light that wreathes your skin during combat, intense focus, or meditative work.
You gain an additional Vyana. The benefits stack with your first Vyana (additional Mana, Vigor, and unarmed damage scaling). Many Ascetic techniques and abilities scale with your Vyana count.

While at least one Vyana is active (which is, in practice, always for a cultivated Ascetic), your skin gains the consistency of cultivated radiance: you gain natural Armor 2 against physical damage, +1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks against cold, exhaustion, and toxin, and you no longer suffer the effects of severe weather or environmental temperature extremes. The radiance is visible at all times during combat or stress; you may suppress it through meditative effort (1 minute of focused concentration), but it returns whenever your cultivation is invoked.

Your Vyana has learned to express itself through the strike. When you make an unarmed attack, you may spend 5 Mana to declare it a Vyana Strike: the strike gains +1 Bonus Die of damage per Vyana you possess, and the damage is treated as Force damage (in addition to physical) for the purposes of damage resistance and ward interactions.

Vyana Strike may be used a number of times per scene equal to your Vyana count + your Soul Bonus Dice. Each Vyana Strike against the same target in the same scene reduces the target's Resistance to subsequent Vyana Strikes by 1 (cumulative). Spirits, undead, and creatures vulnerable to radiant or holy damage take +1 additional Bonus Die from each Vyana Strike beyond the first.

Your Vyana has learned the discipline of restraint. As an Action, you may enter a state of Quiet Hand: for 1 minute per Vyana, you gain +1 Bonus Die to all Defense reactions, your unarmed strikes inflict the Slowed condition on the target until end of your next turn (rather than dealing damage), and you cannot be the target of an opportunity attack regardless of your movement.

Quiet Hand may be used once per scene. While in Quiet Hand, you may not make Vyana Strikes or any other damage-dealing actions; the discipline requires complete attention to defense and deflection. The state ends when its duration ends, when you take an attack Action of your own, or when you choose to dismiss it (Free Action).

Your Vyana has refined your perception of the body's quiet truths. You gain +1 Bonus Die to Awareness checks and Resistance checks involving deception, manipulation, and concealed intent — your cultivated body reads physical micro-cues and aura-tremors that others miss.

Additionally, you may perceive the general health, exhaustion, fear, and emotional state of any creature within 30 feet of you, regardless of their attempts to conceal it. Specifically, you can identify when a creature is wounded, when they are about to act, and when they are deceiving you. This is a passive sense and requires no Action to engage.

Your Vyana has learned to heal what would harm. As an Action, you may enter Inner Cultivation: for 1 minute per Vyana, at the start of each of your turns, you recover 1d6 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Durability, and you reduce all conditions on you by 1 rank (Severe to Moderate, Moderate to Light, Light to none) at the end of each turn.

Inner Cultivation may be used once per scene. While in Inner Cultivation, you may not make attacks or spend Vigor on damaging actions; the discipline requires the full attention of your cultivated essence on the inward work. Allies within 10 feet of you also benefit from a subtle radiant aura: they recover 1d4 Vigor at the start of each of your turns, as if drawing strength from your sustained meditation.
Through deepening cultivation, an additional Vyana ignites within you.

You gain an additional Vyana. The benefits stack with your existing Vyana (additional Mana, Vigor, and unarmed damage scaling). Add +1 Bonus Die to the damage or effects of Vyana-empowered techniques.

Your Vyana has learned to extend outward as a visible blessing. As an Action, you may enter Outer Cultivation: for 1 minute per Vyana, every ally within 15 feet of you gains +1 Bonus Die to attack rolls, +1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks, and recovers 1 Vigor at the start of each of their turns. The Outer Cultivation aura is visible as a soft golden expansion of your body's radiance.

Outer Cultivation may be used once per scene. While in Outer Cultivation, you may make attacks and act normally, but the aura suppresses your own Inner Cultivation and Quiet Hand (you cannot benefit from those states simultaneously). The aura ends when its duration ends, when you are reduced to 0 Health, or when you choose to dismiss it (Free Action).
Choose from the following three transformative capstones.

Your Vyana has reached the apex of its outward expression. Once per long rest, you may declare Saint's Strike: your next unarmed strike or Vyana Strike deals damage to both the target's Health pool and their spiritual self simultaneously. The strike bypasses all physical defenses, all magical wards, all damage resistance and damage immunity — it strikes the soul, and the body follows.

Saint's Strike deals damage equal to a Vyana Strike at maximum Vyana investment plus 3 additional Bonus Dice. On a successful hit, the target also suffers a permanent Wound to their cultivation: until the end of the scene, the target's Aura Stars, Mana Circles, Mysteries, or other engines function at one rank lower (3 Stars become 2, 3 Circles become 2, etc.). On a critical hit, the rank reduction lasts until they receive specific spiritual healing or sanctification.

Saint's Strike is the working of legend. It is the strike a Buddha is said to have used to remove the obstruction from the seeker's soul. It is the strike that sometimes ends wars without further fighting. The Ascetic who has mastered it is sometimes feared by their own teachers.

Your Vyana has learned to disarm without striking. Once per long rest, you may invoke Saint's Witness: for 1 minute per Vyana, your cultivated radiance is visibly manifest as a holy nimbus around your entire body, perceptible to anyone with even rudimentary spiritual sight. While the Witness is active, every creature within 30 feet of you that intends violence toward you or your allies must make a Resistance check at 1 Challenge per Vyana you possess at the start of each of their turns.

On a failed Resistance, the creature is unable to take hostile action against you or your allies for that turn; they may take any other action, but cannot attack, cast harmful spells, or otherwise commit violence in your direction. On a critical failure, the creature suffers 1 rank of fatigue and is also rendered Frightened of you for 1 round.

Saint's Witness affects all hostile creatures within range without specific targeting. The Ascetic stands silent and radiant; the violence around them simply stops, as if the surrounding combatants have remembered what they actually meant to do today and chosen otherwise. Allies and neutral observers are unaffected by the Witness, though they often find the experience deeply moving.

Your Vyana has reached the apex of its inward expression. Your body has become more radiant than physical — flesh has been replaced by cultivated essence held in human form by will alone. This is the apex of the Ascetic's path, and the threshold of what some call the Mortal Saint.

Once per long rest, as a Free Action, you may declare Saint's Body: for 1 minute per Vyana, your physical form is replaced entirely with manifest Vyana, your body becoming a sustained expression of cultivated soul. While in Saint's Body, you gain the following: Heroic Resistance to all physical damage; full immunity to disease, poison, age, exhaustion, hunger, thirst, suffocation, and environmental extreme; +1 Bonus Die to all attack rolls, Defense reactions, and Resistance checks; the ability to fly at your normal Speed; and the ability to pass through mundane physical obstacles no thicker than 5 feet, as your body becomes briefly intangible at the moment of passage.

When Saint's Body ends, you suffer 1 rank of fatigue and your body resumes its physical form. If you took damage during Saint's Body equal to or greater than your Health pool (which is difficult to achieve given the Heroic Resistance), your physical form does not resume — your cultivation has been spent, and you must be returned to physical form through specific Ritual work performed by a Heroic-or-higher tier Ascetic, Priest, or other suitably empowered practitioner. This is the apex risk of the path: to walk too far into the radiance, and not return.

The cultivator of small graces. The bearer of the luck that others called luck because they had nothing else to call it. The one whose pockets carry someone else's coin and whose footsteps leave no mark.

Thieves are the masters of Fortune — the cultivated luck that bends ordinary chance away from probability and toward the Thief's particular needs. Where the Ordinary Rogue spends Edge Dice on planned work, the Heroic Thief spends Fortune Dice on the moments where the plan fails — the lock that should not have opened, the guard who should have seen the shadow, the leap that should not have landed. Fortune is not the Thief's only craft, but it is the one that separates them from the rest of the underworld.

The Thief's craft is the craft of survival in places where the normal rules do not protect you. It is the craft of the back alley, the rooftop, the unlit corridor, and the moment when something is about to go wrong and you have one round to make sure it goes wrong for someone else. Thieves are recognized by what they have walked away from: the manor that was burgled and no one knew how, the captured smuggler who escaped a sealed cell, the assassination attempt that the target slept through. Their reputations precede them in whispered form, and most Thieves prefer to keep it that way.
To enter the Thief Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Agility Score of 15 or higher.
Speed Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Stealth.
The Rank 4 Legerdemain Talent Steal

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Lucky Break.

blocks.text: Thief Elite Tree: The Cultivated Luck

blocks.html: The Thief's first lesson is that the world does not actually care about probability. It cares about attention, and where attention is paid, results follow. The cultivated Thief has learned to pay attention to the places where others stop paying attention. That is what they call Luck.

Luck is also a skill, and the Thief has cultivated it. You gain 2 Fortune Dice (sometimes called Luck Bonus Dice) of d4 quality (Feeble). Fortune Dice are a separate resource pool from Edge Dice; they are recovered after each long rest.

Once per round, before or after rolling but before the result is determined, you may spend one or more Fortune Dice and add them as Bonus Dice to any single Action Roll or Reaction Roll you make. Fortune Dice ignore all Charges and Challenges that apply to the roll — they are pure additions, unaffected by penalties of any kind.

Additionally, you gain the Appraise ability: with one full turn of inspection, you can get a general idea of the value of an item that is sized medium or smaller and within 10 feet. You can guess the item's overall Quality and approximate market value. Magical artifacts and relics may require an Awareness (Investigate) check VS 25 to fully grasp the value of.
Your Fortune has matured beyond the small graces of the apprentice. Increase your Fortune Dice quality from d4 (Feeble) to d6 (Poor). Increase your total Fortune Dice per long rest to 3.

Additionally, you gain Rogue's Panache: choose either the Basic Melee technique of Feint or the Basic Ranged technique of Body Shot as a learned technique. Increase your maximum Vigor by 1d4×10. For each Heroic level after this, increase your Vigor by an additional 1d4.

Your accumulated luck and your maturing reflexes have begun to make you a noticeable presence in the underworld. Other Thieves recognize your style; smaller operators step aside when you walk past them; the more established practitioners begin to notice you for either employment or competition.
Choose from the following three talents. The Specialty talent may be purchased multiple times; the others are unique.

You have learned one of the cultivated subspecialties that distinguish a journeyman Thief from a beginner. Choose one of the following Specialties at the time of purchase. You may purchase Thief's Specialty multiple times (at this rank and at Rank 4), each time selecting a different Specialty:

Swift Stealth. You may enter stealth as an Action even during combat. Opponents are entitled to an Awareness (Notice) check VS your Stealth check to see you hiding. If you successfully hide, you may flee without creating an Opening, or attempt a Sneak Attack on your next turn.

Evade and Escape. The first rule of winning is living through losing. While you are Wounded, you suffer no Challenges to Defense reactions. When you take an Action to flee, you do not create an Opening.

Fence and Market. There is always someone willing to buy and sell goods acquired through illicit means. In any medium or larger settlement, you can locate appropriate fences and underground markets with a Social (Charm) check VS 25 and about a half hour of careful inquiry.

Lucky Knife. When you successfully employ Sneak Attack with a melee or ranged weapon, you may spend one or more of your Fortune Dice and add them as Bonus Dice to the attack's damage roll. Your attack must succeed first.

Fortune's Luck. You have a knack for finding treasures. With 1 minute of careful searching and an Awareness (Investigate) check VS 30, you may roll one of your Fortune Dice and discover this many gold coins (or roughly equivalent valuables) on the searched person, in the searched container, or in the searched room. Once per location.

Tinker. Your skill with small mechanisms extends well beyond locks. When you identify a booby trap or similar mechanism, you may attempt to disable the device with a Legerdemain (Lockbreak) check VS 30 rather than triggering or avoiding it.
Your reflexes have reached the threshold of professional speed. You gain 1 additional Action on your turn.

Through hard practice and the kind of bone-deep instinct that comes from being knocked down many, many times, you have learned to recover from compromised positions before your opponent can capitalize. When you are knocked prone, as a Reaction you may spend 5 Vigor to roll away and come up standing adjacent to your original position, without creating an Opening.

Additionally, once per scene, when you suffer a condition that would impede your movement (Restrained, Grappled, Slowed, or similar), you may immediately make a Resistance check at 1 Challenge to shake off the condition without spending an Action.

Your accumulated craft has reached the point where good fortune amplifies its own returns. When you deal damage with a ranged or melee weapon attack or technique that includes Bonus Dice, and any of your Bonus Dice to damage Burst, you recover 4 Vigor per Bursting Bonus Die. This recovery happens automatically with no action cost.

Additionally, when one or more of your Fortune Dice Burst on any roll, you may immediately roll a second Fortune Die (at no Fortune cost) and add it to the same roll. The second Fortune Die may also Burst, in which case a third Fortune Die rolls, continuing as long as Fortune continues to favor you. The visible signature of a chain-Bursting Fortune Die working is unmistakable: small bright sparks at the edge of your vision, a momentary still in the air, the sense that the world has briefly held its breath.
Your Fortune has matured to the threshold of consistent operation. Increase your Fortune Dice quality from d6 (Poor) to d8 (Ordinary). Increase your total Fortune Dice per long rest to 4.

Many Thief abilities scale with your Fortune Dice pool and quality. At Ordinary Luck, your Fortune-influenced rolls become reliably advantageous — the kind of reliability that other practitioners recognize as not actually luck at all, but cultivated skill operating at a layer below conscious thought.

You learn an additional Thief's Specialty from the list at Rank 3 (Swift Stealth, Evade and Escape, Fence and Market, Lucky Knife, Fortune's Luck, or Tinker). You may not select a Specialty you have already learned. The Thief who has accumulated three or four Specialties is genuinely versatile — the seasoned underworld operator capable of working any room, any rooftop, any market or vault.
Choose from the following three transformative capstones.
Your Fortune has reached the apex of Ordinary-tier cultivation. Increase your Fortune Dice quality from d8 (Ordinary) to d10 (Heroic). Increase your total Fortune Dice per long rest to 5.

Once per long rest, when you spend Fortune Dice on a single roll, you may declare the spend a Cascade: every die in your Fortune Dice pool is added to the roll, all at once. The dice ignore Charges and Challenges as normal. After a Cascade, your Fortune Dice pool is empty until your next long rest. The Cascade is the working of the legend — the heist that ended a war, the escape that emptied a fortress's coffers, the moment when a Thief decided that they would not, in fact, be caught today. Whatever you were trying to do, you almost certainly do it.

Your skill at taking what you should not have has reached the apex of practiced refinement. Once per long rest, you may declare a Perfect Theft: while concentrating on a single object you can physically reach or perceive within 60 feet, you may, with a Legerdemain (Steal) check, take the object from its location to your own possession in a single Action — regardless of intervening barriers, alarms, magical wards (up to Heroic tier), or the presence of observers.

The check is rolled at +3 Bonus Dice from your accumulated mastery, and Fortune Dice may be spent freely on the check. On a successful Theft, the object appears in your possession at the start of your next turn. No alarm sounds; no ward triggers; no observer perceives the moment of transit. If the object is being held or worn by a creature, that creature makes an Awareness check at 2 Challenges to perceive that anything has happened at all.

A Perfect Theft cannot take a creature, cannot take an object whose mass exceeds twice your own, cannot take an object actively being defended by a Heroic-or-higher tier protector, and cannot take an object inscribed with a Master-tier or Forbidden enchantment. Within those limits, the working is genuinely impossible to defend against. Most owners who have suffered a Perfect Theft never realize the object is gone for hours, days, or — in some cases — years.

Your accumulated reputation has reached the threshold where the underworld and the broader populace alike know your name and tell stories about you. Choose one specific reputation you have cultivated: Honorable Thief (you steal only from those who deserve it; the poor and the common will hide you), Master of Shadows (no one knows your face, but everyone knows your work; you are a name and a rumor only), or Robin's Heir (your wealth flows back into the communities you draw from; you are protected by gratitude rather than fear).

Your chosen reputation grants concrete benefits: +2 Bonus Dice to Social checks in your reputation's domain (commoners and the poor for Honorable Thief; underworld figures and informants for Master of Shadows; the formerly downtrodden and politically dispossessed for Robin's Heir). Once per session, you may invoke your reputation to gain immediate refuge, an introduction to a relevant contact, free passage through a controlled area, or the cooperation of a stranger who has heard of your work. Your reputation has costs as well: enemies of your reputation's framing (corrupt nobles, rival underworld lords, oppressive governments) may take particular interest in finding you.

The Folk Hero capstone is the Thief who has, despite everything, become a story. Children sing songs about them. Authorities pretend they do not exist. Other Thieves either look up to them or, occasionally, try to inherit the name by killing the bearer. The reputation is a power and a target both — but the Thief who reaches this tier has decided that being known is, on balance, worth more than the alternative.
The cultivator of attention. The bearer of the spoken word as cultivated power. The one whose voice carries weight beyond hearing.

Orators are the masters of Glamours — Performance-driven enchantments embedded in spoken word, in song, in oratorical structure, and in the cultivated discipline of attracting and holding an audience's attention. Where Mages cast through inscribed Circles and Knights through manifested Aura, Orators cast through *people listening* — a Glamour does not work if no one hears it, and a Glamour cast to a thousand listeners works a thousand times harder than one cast to one. The Orator is the only Heroic class whose cultivation engine is the audience itself.

The Orator's craft is old. It descends from the storytellers of the early peoples, from the war-poets whose verses could rally a routed army or break a hostile siege, from the priest-orators whose sermons changed the convictions of kingdoms. Modern Orators carry this lineage into a more formal mechanical structure — Glamours are now categorized, codified, and trainable — but the underlying truth remains: an Orator who does not have an audience is not an Orator. They are, at most, a singer practicing alone in the dark. The full power of the craft requires people to be listening.
To enter the Orator Heroic class, you must meet all of the following:

items: Empathy Score of 15 or higher.
Soul Score of 15 or higher.
Novice rank in Entertain.
Novice rank in Social.
At least 3 Edge Dice from any source (Rogue Edge Dice qualify; alternate sources at GM discretion).

blocks.html: When you enter this class you gain the following Talent:

items: Glamours.

blocks.text: Orator Elite Tree: The Cultivated Voice

blocks.html: The Orator's first lesson is that the world is held together by spoken agreements, and that spoken agreements can be unwoven, rewoven, or quietly rewritten by those who have practiced the work. The Glamour is not a spell. The Glamour is the moment when the audience decides what is true.

You have completed the cultivation of the Orator's foundational craft. Glamours are Performance-driven enchantments — magical effects produced through spoken word, song, oratorical structure, or related cultivated arts. A Glamour requires you to perform (Entertain skill check) and to have at least one observer who can perceive your Performance. Glamours cost Mana to invoke (10 Mana per use, scaling with Glamour type) and consume one Action on your turn.
At the time of purchase, you learn three Glamour types of your choice from the following list:

items: Inspire. Choose one ally who hears your Performance. Until the end of their next turn, they gain +1 Bonus Die to one chosen roll type (attack, defense, skill, or resistance). Additional listeners may benefit at +1 Mana per listener.

Distract. Choose one enemy who hears your Performance. Until the end of their next turn, they suffer 1 Challenge to one chosen roll type (attack, defense, awareness, or resistance). Multiple targets may be affected at +5 Mana per additional target.

Compel. Choose one creature who hears your Performance. Make an Entertain check VS their Soul-based Resistance. On success, the target performs one simple action of your choosing on their next turn (drop a held item, take one step in a direction, look at a specific person, etc.).
