You can substitute usable parts for core components, with 1 rank reduction in quality, and you can substitute core components for usable parts of the same quality. You can disassemble machines and devices into 1d4 usable parts and 1 core component of the same quality as the original device.

You maintain a compact laboratory kit and know how to improvise a full research station from spare equipment, allowing you to conduct high-level analysis and experimentation in the field. In game terms, while using your Laboratory you gain +1 Bonus dice on Science and Technology checks made to investigate or synthesize materials.
Your expertise deepens. Add +1 Bonus Dice to all Science & Technology rolls (for a total of +2). You may learn Expert Blueprints.
You carry an array of specialized tools. When you assemble a toolkit, you receive +1 bonus dice on Science & Technology checks to build or repair equipment when using your custom-built toolkit.
You have developed a mind for technical problems. Add 1d6 to both your Reason and Memory Ability Scores.
You specialize in a scientific discipline and have the knowledge and communication skills to turn discoveries into actionable tools for yourself and your allies. Choose one Scientific Field:
Material Sciences: Compounds, chemicals, explosives, acids, and materials you synthesize are always of +1 rank higher quality.
Physical Sciences: When solving engineering or force-related problems, you receive 1 free Charge to Science & Technology checks, provided you have time to run the numbers.
Life Sciences: You receive 1 free Charge on Medicine checks, so long as you have proper tools or pharmaceuticals.
Pharmacology: You create pharmaceutical compounds at +1 rank higher quality.

In addition, whenever you succeed on a Science & Technology check involving your chosen field, you may generate a Research Brief. This can be shared with others (or referenced by yourself later) to grant +1 Bonus Die to a single related action roll made by referencing that work.

You’re a master of code, computation, and machine logic. Programs, operating systems, simulations, and AI constructs you design are of +1 rank higher quality. When performing a Science & Technology check involving encryption, code-breaking, simulations, algorithm optimization, or synthetic intelligence, you receive 1 free Charge, provided you have access to your terminal or rig.

In addition, you may create a Digital Protocol after succeeding on a relevant Science & Technology check. A Digital Protocol is a reusable subroutine or template that, when referenced, grants +1 Bonus Die on a related digital or computational task to you or an ally. Only one such protocol can be used per action.

You specialize in building and repairing physical systems; engines, hydraulics, gears, and powerframes. Machines you create are 1 rank higher quality be default. Machines, vehicles, and devices you repair regain double the Durability or Integrity.

You specialize in circuits, power flow, sensors, and logic-driven hardware. Electronics you create are 1 rank higher quality be default. You may reroll any 1s you roll on Bonus Dice when repairing or interfacing with electrical or arcane circuitry.
Your mind is a wellspring of invention. Add +1 Bonus Dice to all Science & Technology rolls (for a total of +3). You may learn Master Innovation techniques.

You are a master of abstract systems, synthetic minds, and information itself. You can manipulate algorithms with an elegance that rivals divine logic. Up to once per full rest, you may instantly resolve a digital task (bypassing the need for a check) if the VS would be equal to your Reason Score or lower and you have access to a connected interface or toolset. You may maintain 2 active Digital Protocols at once. Both may be used in the same action if relevant, granting a combined +2 Bonus Dice.

While attempting to create or analyze with a Science & Technology check while using your Laboratory, you can take 2 Chances to trigger a Breakthrough. A Breakthrough allows you to bypass a normally insurmountable limitation (e.g., unstable materials, power source requirements, unknown component) at GM discretion.

You push machines past their limits, using raw parts and reckless genius. It takes at least 2d6 minutes to perform this work and a Science & Technology Check VS 40, and requires you to consume one core component. For the next 2 minutes your reckless upgrades effectively increase the machine’s quality by 1 rank. This will increase speed, damage, range and other factors, depending on the machine. After use, the machine takes 5d8 Durability damage.

You can seize control of any electronic or arcane logic system. With 1d6 minutes of work you can attempt a Science & Technology Check VS 40 to spend 1 Usable Part to bypass a machine’s native programming and issue a new command (e.g., open a sealed door, shut down a turret, seize drone control). You may also trigger self-destruction on controlled systems or hostile constructs, causing an explosive effect equal to their power source or damage die (GM discretion).

This very broad skill tree encompasses general and specific knowledge regarding any number of subjects, such as history, bureaucracy, literature, and philosophy. You might be called upon to make a Studies check to recall a bit of history or lore, or to understand a cultural reference. Studies skills are each branches of academic knowledge, such as lore and trivia, modern history, or regulation and law.
You can take the following Chances with Studies action rolls:
Scholarly Insight. Increase the rank of your academic understanding, providing deeper knowledge.
Efficient Study. Decrease the time required for study and research by 20%.
Analytical Precision. Improve the accuracy of your analysis, providing 1 additional piece of information.
Add +1 Bonus Die to all Studies action rolls (historical, cultural, legal, or scholarly analysis).

You are a recognized authority in one academic discipline, trained to transform scholarly knowledge into practical tools in the field. Your expertise lets you draw direct, actionable benefits from your studies, turning theory into tangible advantages. Choose one:
History: This includes areas of ancient and modern history, dead civilizations, or even modern affairs. Gain +1 Charge when using historical precedents to justify an action or design a plan.

Law: Including legal, bureaucratic, and organizational studies and governance. You can “draft” or “cite” a legal precedent in downtime that grants +1 Bonus Die on related Social or similar negotiations during the next day.
Culture: Arts, humanities, philosophy and anthropology. You can identify and leverage a group’s unspoken customs to reduce the VS on Social checks against them by up to 15.

Commerce: You have extensively studies goods. Economics, and trade. You can successfully appraise the general value of an object with a Studies check against a VS of 20 +5 per Quality Rank of the item.
Whenever you succeed on a Studies check involving your chosen specialty, you may create a Research Brief: a concise, authoritative record of your findings.
A Research Brief can be shared with an ally (or referenced by you later) to grant +1 Bonus Die on a single related action roll.
Briefs are considered unique to the specific subject, location, or group studied and cannot be reused for unrelated situations.

You maintain a personal collection of research materials. In game terms, while using your archive (portable or permanent), you gain +1 Bonus Die to Studies checks made to analyze documents, decode scripts, or cross-reference facts.
Your analytical processes are unmatched. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Studies rolls (for a total +2). You can identify forgeries or falsified records with a VS 25 check.

You compile precise observational notes. Once you’ve studied a culture or site for at least one day, allies may reference your notes to gain +1 Bonus Die on any related Social or Investigation roll in that location or group.
Your scholarly pursuits have trained your logical understanding. Increase your Reason Score by 1d10.
Your mind stores detail with uncanny fidelity. Increase your Memory Score by 1d10.
You read people and customs with ease. Increase your Empathy score by 1d10
Your practice of applying knowledge under stress has steeled your will. Increase your Resolve Score by 1d10.
When attempting to memorize a new blue print, formula, spell or technique, you may add your Studious Bonus Dice to your roll.

Your scholarship carries undeniable weight in formal or intellectual arenas. You may roll Studies instead of Social in any official, academic, or bureaucratic setting where your specialty is relevant. Once per scene, you may add +1 Bonus Die to any ally’s roll when your name, credentials, or reputation is invoked.
You are a master of academic pursuit. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Studies rolls (for a total +3).

ou are a master of connecting disparate information sources. When cross-referencing at least two different archives, you receive +1 Charge on your Studies roll. On success, you may also identify a hidden or secondary use for the information.

When observing a live scene or environment, you may spot a momentary advantage. With 5 minutes of field observation, you may make a Studies check VS 40 on your turn as an action. On a Success you device a field strategy for you and your comrades; Calling out to your allies you can gain one of the following benefits:
Create an opening against a single creature you were observing.
Give all allies listening +1 Charge on one specific skill check related to your observation.
Add +1 Bonus Dice on Attack Roles against one group of enemies.

When you are required to make a Resistance roll against a mental, memetic, or psionic assault, you may use your Reaction to invoke scholarship and bolster that Resistance with your Studies skill, adding your Studies Bonus dice to the roll at the cost of 5 additional Mana.

The principles of medicine are one of the oldest forms of natural philosophy and science. Modern medicine requires extensive training in anatomy, biology, chemistry, and ethics. Medical skills include medical treatment and surgery, as well as medical specialties and pharmacology.
You can take the following Chances with Medicine action rolls:
Healing Touch. Increase the rank of your first aid healing dice by 1, even though they are not bonus dice.
Rapid Treatment. Decrease the amount of time necessary for first aid to 1 action.
Comprehensive Care. Decrease the time required for medical procedures by 20%.

Your skill with Medicine is advanced. Add +1 Bonus Dice to Medicine checks to treat illness and injury with modern remedies and pharmaceuticals, stiches, sutures, and antibiotics. Under your care, patients will recover +2d4 Health each day after a night’s sleep. With a Medicine (treatment) check VS 20 you can spend 2 actions to perform first aid which will recover 1d10 Durability to an injured character by cleaning and dressing wounds.
You have become an expert in one area of medicine and treatment, such as surgery or internal medicine. Select one of the specialties below.

Modern Surgery. You are an expert surgeon. With a successful Medicine (treatment) check, you can perform complex, life-saving surgeries. In practical terms, a successful surgery check can stabilize a patient that is dying or has died very recently from injury and help injuries recover in 1d4 weeks. During a break, you can restore 1d10 Durability to a creature or 1d8 Health once per day per patient.

Genetics. You are an expert in genetics and epigenetic research. With a successful Medicine (treatment) check, you can craft specific gene therapies to treat disease and potentially augment a patient if the technology exists. In practical terms, provided a sufficient laboratory you can cure or reverse some genetic conditions and diseases.

Internal Medicine. You specialize in internal medicine and toxicology. When you treat a creature with a disease or poisoning, they receive an additional +1 bonus dice to Resistance checks made to resist any ailments or diseases while under your care.
You excel at diagnosing, treating, and preventing injury or disease. You add +1 Bonus Dice your Medicine checks, for a total of +2.

You’re built for high-pressure saves in hostile environments. As a first responder to injury or illness, you know how to treat wounds, poisonings, and potentially fatal episodes. As an action you can make a Medicine check VS 20 to perform first aid to treat an adjacent creature, you restore 2d10 Durability by dressing wounds or provide a +1 Bonus Dice to overcome illness or poisoning with Resistance.

You excel in sustained, restorative healing. Under your care, a patience recovers an additional +2d4 Health with a nights rest, as well as gain +1 Bonus Dice on Resistance checks against progressive illness or poison made during that rest.

You can keep someone going past their limits. When you use an action to perform first aid on a wounded target, you can neutralize the effects of the Wounded condition for 1d4 turns and restore 1d4 Health.
You can handle complex, multi-system injuries and illnesses. During Downtime, you may treat two different conditions or two separate patients in the time normally required for one.
Your insights have expanded your deductive ability. Increase your Reason Ability Score by 1d10.
You are a master of medical techniques. Add +1 Bonus Dice to Medicine checks (for a total of +3).
As a master of medicine, you may deliver a treatment that vastly accelerates recovery. Under your care patience recover +4d4 Health each day after a night’s sleep and 2d10 Durability.

You can save creatures from certain death. When an ally adjacent to you would be killed by an attack or physical assault, you may make a Medicine check VS 40 as a Reaction. On success, they remain active at 1 Health. As an action on your turn, you may make a Medicine check VS 50 and you can treat a creature that as been killed within the last 4 rounds (one minute) from an attack or assault, stabilizing them at 0 health. However, this requires some sacrifice. The saved creature suffers some permanent injury or scar. The GM may dictate, or simply reduce one physical or mental ability score by 1d4, or one resource (Durability, Health, Mana, Vigor) by 2d4.

Attempting to build, repair, or manufacture something calls for a Crafts check. Crafts skills are branches of crafts such as metalworking, woodworking, or lapidary. Crafts can also include visual arts, such as painting or assembling simple machines from parts.
You can take the following Chances with Crafts action rolls:
Masterwork. Increase the quality of the crafted item by 1 Rank.
Efficient Use. Use 20% fewer materials or resources without compromising quality.
Swift Construction. Decrease the time needed for crafting by 20%.
Add +1 Bonus Die to Crafting checks. You may create Poor-quality items (never Feeble) from basic materials. You may also learn Novice Drafts.

You can restore tools and gear with ease. When repairing armor or weapons, restore +1d8 Durability in addition to normal results. One your turn, you may use 1 action, 1 usable parts, and 15 Vigor to perform emergency field repair, restoring 3d6 Durability.

You can instantly appraise material quality and hidden properties. With a successful Crafting check VS 15 +5 per quality rank, you can determine the quality rank and any special traits (e.g., flexible, brittle, conductive) of raw materials usable parts, and core components.

Choose one of the following craftsmanship specialties: metalworking, woodworking, leatherworking and tailoring, construction, glass & lapidary, or another relevant craft. Items you make of that specific type are by default Ordinary quality.
You can craft functional weapon and armor modifications. You may create weapon grip and head mods, and armor mods provided you learn the drafts for them.
Your expertise grows. You add +1 Bonus Dice to Crafting checks (for a total of +2). You may learn Expert Drafts.

You have built a masterwork toolkit attuned to your craft. When using your bonded tools, gain 1 Charge on your Crafting Bonus Dice on Crafting checks. If lost, your bonded toolkit can be remade during Downtime.
You can strip and reconfigure weapon mods on the fly. Using 2 actions on your turn you may swap out one modification on a weapon or armor. You may also remove mods safely without damaging them.
Your skill extends into enchanted and arcane tools. You may safely swap or alter modifications on magically enchanted weapons or armor.

It is easier for you to achieve superb quality in your craftsmanship. When you take a chance on a Crafting check to increase the quality of your product, you only increase your Target VS by +10, not 15. This also adjusts the construction time and cost as well.
Your specialty in forging weapons shines. Provided you have materials and usable parts of the appropriate quality to use, weapons you create in your specialty are always +1 quality rank higher.

Your skill at protective gear is unmatched. If you have the time and high-quality materials, Armor you craft in your specialty is always +1 quality rank higher. When repairing armor you crafted, restore double Durability.

You have developed the skill to deeply customize your personal equipment. You may apply one additional modification to a personal weapon beyond its normal limit. Only you can wield the weapon effectively; in another’s hands, its mods go inert.

You refine weapons into perfect extensions of their form. You may sacrifice the Head/Blade mod slot on a weapon to permanently increase its quality rank by +1. This reforging requires 1 core component of equal or greater quality and a Craft Check VS 40.
Your craft is legendary. Add +1 Bonus Dice to Crafting checks (for a total of +3). You may learn Master Drafts. Crafted works can be created 1 rank higher quality than the base material.

You can bind charms into your crafted weapons. Weapons you craft gain an integral Charm Mod slot, in addition to their normal slots. Only one such talisman may be bound per weapon, and once you select this charm, it is the only charm you may add in this manner. This Charm becomes your signature as a weaponsmith.

Your armor resists all forms of punishment. Armor you craft may gain one permanent resilience trait: Hardened (physical), Padded (impact), Flak (explosive), Insulated (fire), or Grounded (electricity). This counts as an additional characteristic, not a mod slot.

Song, instruments, dance, and performing arts, you’ll make an Entertain check when you wish to engage in some manner of performance. Entertain skills include musical instruments, song, storytelling, and distraction.
You can take the following Chances with Entertain action rolls:
Captivating Performance. Attempts to resist your performance are made with 1 Challenge.
Inspirational Act. Boost the morale of your audience, giving them a Charge on their next action with Encourage.
Crushing Insults. Add an additional Challenge with Demoralize.
Add +1 Bonus Die on Entertain checks. You know all basic Performances and may learn Novice Performances.
You are a specialist in one form of entertainment selected from the list below.
Music. Instruments, vocals, rhythm. When you use a Performance Technique, you may affect one additional target within range if they can hear you.
Oratory. Rhetoric, poetry, storytelling. When you succeed on an Entertain check, you may also grant yourself +1 Bonus Die to your next Social or Studies action made on the same turn.
Dance. Choreography, physical expression. When you use a Performance Technique, you may shift up to 10 feet as a component of the performance without breaking the performance.

Drama. Masks, mimicry, roleplay, presence. With at least 3 minutes of preparation, you may use a performance to disguise yourself or another, granting +1 Bonus Die on Social checks to deceive or beguile.
Add +1 Bonus Dice to Entertain checks (for a total of +2). You may learn Expert Performances.
Your buff performances are heightened. Whenever an ally succeeds on an action roll while under your performance, they regain 1d6 Vigor or Mana.
Your debuff performances linger. When you successfully impose a negative effect with a performance, it lasts +1 additional rounds.
You may sustain a performance across multiple rounds by paying its Mana cost as upkeep. You do not need to re-roll your performance check.
Your unique performance identity shines. Choose one Novice or Expert Performance you know. When performing this, gain +1 Bonus Die to Entertain checks and reduce Mana costs by 3 (minimum 1).
Your words and acts cut deeper than expected. When you succeed on a debuff performance, the target also loses 1 action on their next turn.
Add +1 Bonus Dice to Entertain checks (total of +3). You may learn Master Performances.
Your Maestro specialty evolves into world-class genius:
Music: Affected allies also regain 1d6 Vigor the first time they succeed on an action roll during your performance.
Oratory: Affected allies gain +1 Bonus Die to their next two Social/Studies rolls, not just one.
Dance: You may move up to half your Speed each turn while maintaining a performance.
Drama: Your disguises are flawless; they hold up under scrutiny unless exposed with hard evidence.
Withering
Your debuff performances are soul-crushing. When you succeed, the target loses 2d6 Mana immediately, plus an additional 1d4 Mana per round they remain under your performance.

The art of fellowship, charm, diplomacy, and deceit. You might roll a Social check to convince another creature of your good intentions or sway them to your side. Social also includes psychology, which you might use to understand or treat a creature with mental illness. Social skills include Charm, Deceit, and Manipulation.
You can take the following Chances with Social action rolls:
Charismatic Presence. Enhance the impact of your charisma, making interactions more favorable.
Compelling Allure. Attempts to Resist your charm or seduction are made with 1 Challenge.
Malicious Deceit. Attempts to resist your deceit and manipulation are made with 1 Challenge.
You are skilled at communicating and interfacing with others. Add +1 Bonus Die on Social checks.

You are attuned to others. When interacting socially, you may ask the GM to reveal one hidden emotional state of a creature you’re addressing. Additionally, allies affected by your encouragement gain +1 Bonus Die on their next action when you Aid them.

You specialize in lies, guile, and subterfuge. When you succeed on a Deception-based Social check, you may create a false detail (a forged fact, misleading cue, or fabricated rumor) that counts as true until disproven.
You thrive on fear and menace. With a successful Social check VS your target’s Resolve you can intimidate a creature, you may force a target to become Anxious for the remainder of your encounter.
Your social graces and ability improve. Add +1 Bonus Dice on Social checks (total +2).

Your skill in allure and temptation is unmatched. When you succeed on a Deception check with a target predisposed to attraction or desire, you gain 1 free Charge on your next Social or subterfuge roll against them.

You project authority. Using the threat of violence, you can force a creature with whom you share a language to undertake 1 non-offensive action on their turn. The target is entitled to a Resistance reaction VS your Menace (intimidation) roll, particularly if they are not overly convinced of the dangers of noncompliance.
You have a well-developed insight into others. Increase your Empathy Score by 1d10.

Your lies carry weight. You can weave terrible deceit that can cause a creature to act in erratic and unpredictable ways. Choose one creature that can hear and understand you. On a successful Social check, you can present a simple lie in a completely believable way. This lie can’t completely contradict reality, such as the sky being green or that they are a dog, but more insidious lies are possible. Their friends and allies are using them, they are a chosen hero, or that you care about them more than anyone. Unless the target makes a successful Resistance reaction VS your Deceit roll, they believe your manipulative lies and act accordingly. This is not a fast process and requires 1d4 turns of interaction and deception, but it is extremely effective.

As an action, you can project an aura of terrifying intent. Your aura extends to all creatures that can see you within 30 feet. All creatures in range may make a Resistance reaction VS your Social roll minus 10. Otherwise, each creature suffers from terror, being paralyzed with Fear until the end of its next turn.
Your charisma is overwhelming. Add +1 Bonus Dice on Social checks (for a total of +3).
Your manipulations are seamless. Once you have successfully Manipulated a creature, for the next 1 hour per Empathy Bonus Dice you can issue Commands to them as an action, no check necessary.

Your presence is crushing. As an action you may unleash a fearful display: all enemies within range must make a Reisitance reaction VS your Social Check. On failure, they are Dazed for 1 turn and Terrified against you or your allies.
Running, jumping, swimming, and climbing, you may be called upon to make an Athletics check for many physical activities. Athletic skills include advanced balance, climbing, tumbling, and swimming.
You can take the following Chances with Athletics action rolls:
Strength Surge. Increase your effective Strength or Speed Score by 5 points when making an Athletics check for Lifting, leaping, or sprinting.
Endurance Boost. Recover 1d6 Vigor after making an Athletics check.
Add +1 Bonus Dice to all Athletics action and attack rolls (sprinting, climbing, jumping, swimming, vaulting).
When you sprint, you can cover twice your normal Speed Score for 1 minute at the cost of 15 Vigor.

With 1 minute of preparation, you can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to 1/2 your Stamina Ability Score. Each minute beyond this, you can continue to hold your breath at the cost of 15 Vigor. You cannot Recover Vigor while you are holding your breath.
While climbing you can climb at your normal movement speed (rather than half your speed) at the cost of 15 Vigor per turn. You cannot recover your Vigor while you are climbing at this speed.
You have built significant poise and stamina. You add +1 bonus Dice you Athletic Checks (for a total of +2).
You have become accustomed to moving and acting while weighed down. You can carry an additional Strength x 5 pounds beyond your lift value before you are encumbered.

While moving over challenging terrain, you can maintain your full Speed at the cost of 10 Vigor per minute. You cannot recover your Vigor whale you are engaged in Parkour movement. With an Athletics check VS 20, you can reduce the damage from a fall by half.

You gain an additional +1d6 Vigor for each level of experience. This is retroactive from when you learn this talent, for example, if you learn this talent at level 8 you would immediately gain 8d6 Vigor.
Your explosive power and carrying capacity increase. Increase your Strength score by +1d10.
Your top speed and quickness sharpen. Increase your Speed score by +1d10.
Your bodily endurance improves. Increase your Stamina score by +1d10.
Your balance, coordination, and reflexes deepen. Increase your Agility score by +1d10.
You are a peak athlete. Add +1 Bonus Dice to your Athletic Checks, for a total of +3.
As an action you can summon incredible, superhuman strength. This feat consumes 30 Vigor but for one turn you can lift up to your Strength Score times 80 lbs.
You move with such speed it blurs the eye. Once per scene, by spending 30 Vigor, you may instantly traverse up to your full Speed score × 5 in feet as a single action, up to once per turn.

You have near perfect ability to move through your environment. While you are Sprinting you may make jumps, run along walls, and even ascend vertical surfaces as part of your move action so long as the total distance covered is within your movement range.

Your endurance is limitless. When you are reduced to 0 Health from an Attack or Assault, you may consume 30 Vigor, if you have it, to instead be reduced to 1 Health. After you use this ability, you must complete a full rest to use it again. At the end of each night’s rest, you automatically recover 1 rank of fatigue, if you have any.
The art of deft hands, illicit entry, and subtle tricks. You’ll be asked to make a Legerdemain check to crack locks or lift something unnoticed, break into security systems, and
You can take the following Chances with Legerdemain action rolls:
Fast Hands. Reduce the time to pick a lock, disarm a trap, or lift an item by 1 Action.
Ghost. Make a Stealth check as part of a Legerdemain action.
Plant. Use sleight of hand to plant something on a target without their knowledge.

You have an understanding of sleight-of-hand and lock-mechanisms. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Legerdemain action rolls (lockpicking, sleight of hand, disarming, prestidigitation). The VS and number of actions necessary to pick a lock or disarm a trap depend on the mechanism’s Quality:
Lock Quality VS

You excel at defeating locks and bindings. You may attempt to pick locks without a pick or tool by consuming 1 useable part, destroying the lock but reducing the pick time by 1 action. However, the lock VS is increased by 5.

You have a knack for traps, both disabling and setting them. You may disarm traps without triggering them on a successful legerdemain. Additionally, you may construct a simple trap (snare, caltrop field, noise-maker) in 1 minute given 2 usable parts.

Your hands move faster than sight. While interacting with a person out of combat, you may attempt to remove items, such as jewelry, wallets, or belt pouches, from their person or pockets with a successful Legerdemain check VS the target’s Guard. The person can even be looking at you
Locks you open take only half the normal time. Add +1 Bonus Dice to Legerdemain checks involving locks (total +2).

You can craft Ordinary offensive traps in 4 turns from 2 useable parts and 1 core component which deal 3d8 damage, or set pre-crafted traps with 2 actions. When you successfully disarm a trap you can harvest it for part, or re-arm it behind you.
As an action you can make a Legerdemain check VS 30 to confuse and distract onlookers, inflicting a 1 Challenge penalty to Awareness checks made against you for 2 turns.

You have developed a light step and natural aversion to trap triggers. You may add your Legerdemain bonus dice to any attempt to avoid or detect a trap without triggering the mechanism. This includes high-tech detection mechanisms as well as primitive traps.

You specialize in infiltration. When you succeed on a Legerdemain check involving locks, wards, or barriers, you may also reduce the action cost by half (stacking with Lockbump). In addition, you may attempt to bypass magical or arcane locks.
Up to once per tern, when you succeed on a light weapon or unarmed attack roll, you may instead steal a worn or actively held item (like a weapon, key, or focus).
You have mastery of delicate deceit. You gain +3 Bonus Dice to Legerdemain Checks total. Once per turn, you may bypass any lock or barrier of poor or feeble quality automatically.

Once per turn, you may quickly disable or rewire a trap, mechanism, or device at half the action cost. You can also boobytrap the mechanism of traps to trigger when disarmed or otherwise tampered with or collected.
You may disarm or set traps as a free action during movement, without slowing down. You may also carry 1 “prepared trap” that can be deployed in 1 action.

You may create a false sensory “tell” (sound, flash, gesture) when using Sleight-of-Hand. turn, you may force all creatures watching within 10 feet to make an Awareness check VS your Legerdemain roll or be distracted, treating you as hidden until your next turn. You may also attempt to lift or plant or otherwise disappear medium sized objects… and creatures.

Your hands seem to move supernaturally. As often as once per turn, you may perform a Sleight-of-Hand trick at range (up to 10 feet). This allows stealing, planting, or manipulating small objects without direct touch.

The art of remaining unseen, unheard, and unsuspected. Stealth is the skill of thieves, assassins, scouts, and spies, anyone who needs to move without notice, strike unseen, or disappear into shadow. A Stealth check covers hiding, tailing someone without being spotted, infiltrating guarded areas, striking from concealment, or masking one’s presence in plain sight.
Special: Moving Unseen. With a Successful Stealth check you can move at 1/4 your usual movement speed, but do so quietly and out of sight.
You may take the following Chances with Stealth action rolls:
Swift Movement. Move at half your normal speed while sneaking.
Ambush. When you attack from Stealth, add +1 Charge to your weapon attack bonus dice.
Mimicry. Blend into the environment or social setting; making your appearance and disappearance un-memorable.
You have practiced moving unseen and unheard. Add +1 Bonus Die on all Stealth checks (hiding, shadowing, ambushing).
Your footwork is trained for silence. You may move at half your full Speed without adding penalties to your Stealth checks. If you move at one quarter Speed, reduce the VS to remain hidden by 5.
You specialize in concealment. You gain +1 Bonus Die on stealth checks, for a total of +2. Additionally, you can hide behind smaller cover than normal (waist-high, narrow shadows).

You blend into the flow of a space. As long as you mimic the motions of those nearby (workers, guards, travelers), you may attempt a Stealth roll to pass through openly observed spaces without drawing suspicion.

You excel at sudden violence. If you have successfully snuck up on a creature, you may make an exceptionally lethal ambush attack before initiative is rolled. You receive +1 bonus dice to damage on this attack if you score a hit.

You can disguise yourself with your environment. After 1 minute of preparation, gain +2 Bonus Dice on Stealth checks in that area until you move more than 60 feet away. While you are hidden you gain partial cover.
When you move with a group (of allies, civilians, or enemies), you may extend your concealment to one adjacent ally. Both of you roll Stealth checks as if you were one unit, using your higher roll.
As an action you may spend 15 Vigor to mark a single target within sight. Until the end of the turn, your first attack from Stealth against that target automatically gains +2 Damage Dice.
You are a master of shadows and stealth. You add +1 Bonus Dice to all your Stealth checks, for a total of +3.

You can move through guarded areas as though unseen. By spending 20 Vigor when you make your Stealth check, you may render yourself undetectable to mundane senses for 1d4 minutes. During this time, only sophisticated magical or technological detection may reveal you.
Once per turn, when you kill or incapacitate a target from Stealth, you may immediately attempt another Stealth roll as a free action. On success, you remain hidden and may chain into another ambush.

Operating, maneuvering, and pushing vehicles and mechs to their limits. You’ll be called upon to make a Piloting check when you attempt to drive or pilot a vehicle or other heavy machine. Piloting skills are the operations of specific vehicle categories that you might pilot with exceptional proficiency.
You can take the following Chances with Piloting action rolls:
Evasive Action. For one turn, increase your vehicle’s Guard by +5.
Slipstream. When tailing another vehicle, increase your Speed by 10% without added fuel cost.
Overdrive. Spend 15 Vigor to increase vehicle Speed or Maneuverability by 1 rank for 1d4 rounds.
Add +1 Bonus Die to all Piloting checks (maneuvers, travel, basic combat driving). You may operate common vehicles of your setting (cars, boats, gliders, bikes).

Choose one vehicle type you can operate with mastery (car, aircraft, spacecraft, power armor, mecha, etc.). While you are in command of a vehicle of your chosen type, you may take reactions with your vehicle, such as Defense, and take an opening with your vehicle. You may take this talent multiple times, selecting a new vehicle type each time.
You add +1 Bonus Die to Piloting checks (total +2). You may operate uncommon vehicles of your specialty type (tanks, combat aircraft, frigates, exo-frames).
You may push vehicles’ mobility to the absolute limit. With a successful Pilot check VS 25 and at the cost of 10 Vigor you may perform a number ace maneuvers including:
Overdrive. Push your vehicle past the limit, increasing max speed by 1/4 for 1 minute.
Powerslide. You can ignore most difficult terrain created by slick surfaces or turbulence/jet wakes for 1 minute.
J Turn. As an action you complete a full 180 degree turn.
Slipstream. You ride the wake of the vehicle in front of you, allowing you to either swiftly pass, or drop from visibility.

You may apply Combat skill talents (attack bonuses, targeting tricks) while using mounted weapons. Furthermore, the Guard of vehicles when you are in command is increased by your Speed Bonus Dice number, i.e., if you had a Speed of 20, you would increase your vehicle’s Guard by +2.
You may spend 10 Vigor to ignore one instance of terrain or atmospheric hazard that would normally slow or stall your vehicle. For 1 turn, your vehicle’s maximum speed is doubled.
When piloting a vehicle crewed with allies, as an action on your turn you can line up their attacks and grant +1 Bonus Die to one ally’s next attack or maneuver roll.
With this talent you may pilot vehicles in your specialty marked with the elite classification, such race cars, jet fighters, battleships, command carriers, prototype mecha.

Once per turn, as a reaction you may spend 20 Vigor evade an attack targeting your vehicle by pulling off an impossible maneuver, adding your Piloting bonus dice to your Defense bonus dice for your Defense roll.
Spend 1 Usable Part to push a vehicle-mounted weapon beyond safe limits. The weapon’s damage die increases by +1 rank for the next attack, but the weapon takes 2d6 Durability damage.

You are a legendary pilot. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Piloting checks (total +3). Once per turn, you may declare a “signature maneuver” (like an Ace maneuver or death spiral, comet dive, zero-G spin) that automatically succeeds unless directly countered by an equally legendary foe.

You can conceal your vehicle’s movement in combat or travel. Once per turn, you may make a Piloting check opposed by enemy Sensors/Awareness; on success, your vehicle is considered Stealthed for 1d4 turns.

When piloting you may use your vehicle as a weapon. This goes well beyond a controlled collision or ram; you vehicle takes negligible damage, but you deal a number of damage dice based to your vehicle’s quality and size, i.e an ordinary car deals d8, where as a poor motorcycle would deal d6. The number of dice is based on the size:

This skill tree covers perception, detection, and intuition. You might be called upon to make an Awareness check to detect that you are being followed, notice a clue in your surrounds, or intuit that another is being dishonest. Awareness skills include Noticing things, Investigation, and Danger Sense.
You can take the following Chances with Awareness action rolls:
Astute Investigation. Gan 1 additional piece of relevant information from your investigation.
Hidden Detection. Notice hidden or obscure details, reducing the Challenge penalties for facing hidden opponents by 1.
Sharp Perception. Increase the quality of information gained from observation or investigation by 1 Rank.
You notice small inconsistencies, surface clues, and immediate threats faster than most. Add +1 Bonus Die on Awareness checks.
You are trained to keep track of surroundings. You cannot be surprised if you have line of sight to the threat; only hidden or distant enemies can successfully surprise you.
You know how to follow leads. When you succeed on an Awareness check, you may generate 1 Clue that grants +1 Bonus Die to your or an ally’s next related Investigation or Studies roll.
Your subconscious alerts you to peril. If you are subject to Ambush initiative, you may make an Awareness check VS the attacker’s highest initiative. If you succeed, you are not ambushed.
You have a keen sense of your surroundings. You add +1 bonus dice to all Awareness checks (for a total of +2).

When you encounter multiple clues, inconsistencies, or tracks, such as an Crafting check to determine structural stability, a Light Weapons check to note the quality and construction of a blade, or a Studies check to verify the authenticity of a manuscript, you may connect them into a Deduction. Each Deduction grants you +1 Bonus Die to a chain of related skill checks, up to +3. You may retain a number of Deductions equal to your Memory Bonus Dice, and swap a change a Deduction during a break.

You have developed preternatural prescience. You cannot be ambushed and attacking you from behind does not grant any benefits to the attacker. Finally, you may make Defense reactions against attacks you are unaware of.
Your awareness extends to your allies. As a reaction you may also spend 5 Vigor to swap Initiative order with an ally who can hear you.

During Downtime, you may analyze evidence to “lock in” one Clue. This permanent Insight grants +1 Bonus Die to Awareness, Studies, and Science checks whenever the same type of crime, entity, or phenomenon is encountered again. You may retain a number of Clues equal to your Memory Bonus Dice. Each time you complete a rest you may swap one clue for another, or forget a Clue altogether.

At the start of each day, roll an Awareness check VS 40. On success, gain 1 Premonition Token. You may spend this token to automatically succeed on an Awareness check made in action during that day (not during downtime).
Little escapes your keen attention and notice. Add +1 Bonus Die on all Awareness checks (for a total of +3).
Once per session, you may ask the GM a single question about an unresolved mystery, crime, or pattern. The answer must be true and useful, though not always complete.

When an ally within sight is struck by an attack or assault that wounds them, as a Reaction you may spend 20 Vigor and “reset” that moment – shouting a warning, or even leaping in the way if they are adjacent. That ally may reroll their Defense or Resistance roll with +1 Bonus Dice, or you may intercept the attack, but you may not roll Defense or Resistance. Once you have used this omen, you must complete a full rest before you can use it again.

More than animal husbandry and horse riding, virtually any interaction with animals will be covered by Beast Handling. You’ll make a Beast Handling check when you attempt to keep control of a mount in combat or to train an animal. Beast Handling skills include riding animals, training and hunting with animals, and soothing beasts.
You can take the following Chances with Beast Handling action rolls:
Soothing Presence. Beasts have 1 Challenge to Resist your soothing.
Training Mastery. Accelerate the training process, reducing the time required by 20%.
Trust Bond. Add 1 Charge to your animal companion’ action roll when you command them.
Add +1 Bonus Die on Beast Handling checks (taming, training, calming, riding). You can recognize animal moods and basic intent on sight.

Given time you can form a bond with one loyal animal. This creature is roughly equal to a wolf, hawk, horse, or similar natural beast. It obeys commands, acts on your initiative, and improves as you gain levels. If your companion dies, you may bond with another after 1 month of training. See Mounts and Companions under equipment for more information.
You can intuit animal needs and feelings. You gain +1 Bonus Die on Beast Handling checks (to a total of +2).
You train your companion in martial synergy. Once per turn, when you strike an enemy, your companion may make a free attack against the same target if adjacent.

You specialize in mounted combat and travel. While mounted, you gain +1 Bonus Die on Athletics, Defense, Heavy Weapons, and Light Weapons checks. Your mount’s Speed is increased by 5 feet for each of your Beast Handling skill Bonus Dice.

Face to face with an aggressive or scared animal, you can attempt to sooth it and convince the beast you are not a threat or prey. Above and beyond a normal Beast Handling check, once an animal is soothed, it will behave normally and may be willing to approach or be handled. To Sooth a beast, you need to make a contested Beast Handling check against the creature’s Resolve. This ability is useless on magical creatures.
Your companion’s attacks count as Flanking when you and it engage the same target. Additionally, when your companion downs an enemy, you regain 1d6 Vigor.

You can turn your mount’s force into your weapon’s force. Once per Turn when you attack with melee weapons from the back of an animal trained for combat, you have a significant advantage, adding 1 Charge to all your Damage Bonus Dice.
Add +1 Bonus Die to all Beast Handling checks (total +3). You may have up to 2 trained companions (though only one can be directed in battle per turn without penalty).
You and your companion are seamless predators. Once per turn, when your companion strikes a target, it creates an Opening you may choose to take and make an attack against that same foe.

You may send your animal aloft, provided it has some means to ascend, to provide overwatch. While active, you gain +1 Bonus Die on Awareness checks, and may ignore cover penalties when targeting enemies your companion has marked.

You and your mount fight as one. While mounted, you gain +1 Weapon Damage Dice on melee attacks and may trample enemies (forcing an Athletics VS 25 or knocking them Prone) as an action using the Charge technique.
You may command up to 3 allied mounts or war-beasts simultaneously. Once per scene, you may rally them with a Beast Handling roll VS 40 to gain +1 Bonus Die to all of their attack rolls for 2 turns.

Hunting, fishing, orienteering, and woodscraft skills, you might make a Survival check to find food or fresh water in the wilderness, start a fire, or track an animal. Survival skills include Orienteering, Woodscraft, and Hunting and Tracking.
You can take the following Chances with Survival action rolls:
Resourceful Foraging. Hunting and foraging will yield an additional day of resources.
Advanced Tracking. Improve the accuracy and detail of tracking information.
Durable Shelter. Tools and shelter you craft are of 1 rank higher quality.
Add +1 Bonus Die to all Survival checks (foraging, navigation, tracking, weather prediction). You can always find food and water sufficient for yourself in any habitable biome.

You can harvest usable materials from nature or slain beasts. On a Survival check VS 30, you can collect the parts of slain creatures; gain 1d4 Usable Parts or one Core Component of a quality matching the creature’s (Generally ordinary, heroic or epic).
You can live off the land with ease. Add +1 Bonus Dice to Survival checks (for a total of +2). With a break you can prepare food and water for up to 4 allies in all but the most arid conditions.

Your maps, instincts, and celestial sense rarely fail. You may reroll any 1s you roll on survival checks for navigation and orienteering, but must accept the second roll, even if it is a 1. While traveling, your group cannot become lost unless under magical influence.
You are a skilled hunter. When you pursue a quarry, on Survival check VS 20 (or Stealth opposed roll) you can follow tracks, and may immediately identify how fresh they are (within 1 hour).

You know how to vanish in terrain. Your group gains +1 Bonus Die to Stealth checks while traveling under your lead. Once per day, you may force pursuers to roll Awareness VS your Survival check to avoid losing your trail.

You can cut roads where none exist. Increase your overland travel speed by 1/3 for your group when blazing a new trail. You may establish a semi-permanent route that others may reuse without penalty once, and can recover this ability to do so after you complete a full rest.
You have the build of a trained predator. Increase your Agility and Stamina Ability Scores by 1d6 each.
You move like a predator. Gain +1 Bonus Die on Stealth checks in natural terrain. If you attack from hiding in the wilderness, add +1 Damage Die to your first attack.

You are a master of snares, pits, and deadfalls. You can use Legerdemain to craft and set traps in a wilderness environment without components. Increase the rank of your Traps by 1 Rank in natural environment if you do have components.
You are unshakable in the wild. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Survival checks (total +3). During Rests in the wilderness, you recover double the normal Health and Mana.
Your knowledge of terrain lets you disappear without trace. While traveling overland you may add your Survival Bonus Dice to Stealth Bonus Dice to determine the VS of any pursuer’s Awareness VS.
You cannot become lost so long as you remain in the same reality and have sight of major celestial bodies. Underground or in a labyrinth you can always feel the direction of north.
When attacking from Stealth in natural terrain, your strike is devastating. Add +2 Weapon Damage Dice and impose the Dazed condition on the target until the end of their next turn.

You can set multiple traps with frightening efficiency. While in a natural environment, you may reduce the time to set traps by half (see legerdemain) to a minimum of 1 action. Traps you set are also well camouflaged, increasing the VS to notice them by 1 rank.

You make an Occult Arts check when casting spells, invoking rituals, commanding spirits, transmuting matter, or crafting enchanted objects. Occult actions often consume Mana and may require rare reagents, focuses, or ritual circles. Like weapon kills and crafting, mastery over the occult grants you special abilities as well as improved creations.
You can take the following Chances with Occult action rolls:
Channel. Take a Chance to increase the effect rank of your ritual or invocation by +1.
Sacrifice. Spend Health or Vigor to gain +1 Mana per point sacrificed during an action.
Seal. Take a Chance to bind or suppress a hostile magical effect for 1 turn.

You understand the basics of spell casting and incantations. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Occult checks (rituals, summoning, wards, alchemical preparations). You may know all basic rituals and formulas and may learn Novice Rituals and Novice Formulas.

When you enter Cultivation Mediation, your recover 2 Mana per minute. You can recover up to twice your Soul Score in Mana in this way. You may still only gain the benefits of Cultivation meditation once per day.

You have the power to command the most basic of spirits: whisps. On your turn you may spend 2 actions and attempt an Occult check VS 25. If you succeed you Invoke one of the following for 1 turn for each of your Soul Bonus Dice. This minor invocation can have one of the following effects:
Distract. Impose the Dazzled condition on one foe.
Bolster. Ally gains 1 Charge to their Weapon skill checks.
Flicker of Flame. Create a small flame or puff of smoke. Enough to light a torch, scorch paper, or briefly obscure vision.
Echoing Voice. Send a whispered message (up to 20 words) to a creature you can see within 30 ft; they hear it as if spoken beside them.
Spectral Push. Shove a lightweight object (up to 10 lbs) across the ground or table; enough to knock over a cup, move a lever, or slam a door shut if it isn’t barred.
Spirit Lantern. Call up a small luminous mote that sheds bright light in a 5-foot area, and dim light in a 20-footfollo, following you.

Chill of the Grave. Lower the temperature suddenly in a small area; creatures in it must succeed a Resistance check VS your Occult roll or shiver and shake, imposing 1 challenge to Athletics, Legerdemain, and Stealth checks.
Whisper of Warning. A spirit shrieks or flares if danger is imminent; allies within 10 feet gain +1 Bonus Die on Initiative checks and Awareness checks to detect enemies or traps.

You have delved deep into the mysteries of the esoteric and arcane. You gain 1 charge on attempts to learn new spells and rituals. If you fail your check, your next attempt can be made in just half the time.
When you make a Science and Technology, Studies, or Awareness check related to the investigation or nature of supernatural forces, you may add your Occult skill Bonus Dice.
You have a keen understanding of the works of magic and the supernatural. Add +1 Bonus Dice to all Occult checks, for a total of +2. You may learn Expert spells and rituals.
You learn to summon and bind a lesser infernal spirit as a servitor. With 4 Actions and an Occult Check VS 30 you may summon a lesser spirit for 1 hour per Soul Bonus Die.

For the duration the servitor will perform tasks and fight as you command. When you cast a spell you may choose to use the servitor as the origin of your spell so long as it is within 30 feet. This Servitor will take virtually any form you desire, but its basic characteristics remain the same:

Size: Small

Durability: 5 times your Soul Bonus Dice
Armor Rank: Equals your Resolve Bonus Dice
Damage: 2d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice

Actions: 2. Acts on your turn; may move, perform basic tasks or attack.

Movement: 20 feet, plus 5 per Soul Bonus Dice

Binding option: If you take a Chance on the summoning roll, you may bind the spirit more tightly, increasing the duration to 1 day per Soul Bonus Dice,

You can commune with nature spirits. You may project your intent making your emotions, questions, or warnings known, and sense their hostility, mood, or desires. This is limited to simple or emotional communication unless you use additional rituals. Once per turn, you may spend 4 Mana to call on a lesser nature spirit to add a minor elemental effect for a number of turns equal to your Soul Bonus dice.
Forest, Earth and Ice can shield with partial cover or grant +3 Armor Rank.
Lighting and Fire will ignite or add +1d6 elemental damage to your weapon attacks.
Ice and Water will cleanse, douse medium fires, or heal 1d6 Durability once per turn.
Wind will push you forward, increasing your Movement by +5.

You have the ability to bring about minor graces from higher powers. With 2 actions and an Occult check VS 25, you create a minor manifestation of Grace – the divine power of those beings worshiped as gods. Unless stated otherwise, your minor grace lasts for 1 turn for each of your Soul Bonus Dice.
Benediction. You or an ally in the area regains 1d4 Durability or Health at the end of your turn.
Radiant Rebuke. A flash of consecrated light forces all enemies within 15 feet to make a Resistance Check VS your Occult roll or suffer 1 Challenge on all Attack rolls for the duration.
Guiding Word. Ally gains +1 Bonus Die on their next Social or Studies roll, flavored as divine clarity or inspiration.
Sacred Ember. Conjure a tiny flame that burns without fuel, shedding bright light in 10 ft. It can ignite flammable objects.
Symbol of Warding. This sigil will prevent eldritch and creatures from beyond from passing through it for the duration.

Voice of Authority. You draw this sigil on a single creature. For the duration the creature cannot attack you, and must succeed a Resistance VS your Occult check or obey your basic commands, assuming it poses no risk to its safety.
Sanctuary Armor. Draw this symbol on a creature. For the duration a faint aura shields them, granting +1 Armor Rank for each of your Soul Bonus Dice.

You are an expert in transmutations and concoctions. Items you craft from formulas are 1 quality rank higher. When you are creating an alchemical consumable, you may take a chance to produce 1 extra dose without increasing the quantity of materials needed.
You achieve a kind of spiritual awakening. Increase your Soul Score by +1d10.

You have mastery of binding and imbuing objects with magic. You can craft Charms and Talismans (these equipment Mods are found in the equipment section) with an Occult check VS 25 and two days of work.
You can create Scrolls and Grimoires (these magical items can be found below) with an Occult Check and appropriate materials.
Finally, your Enchantments are more potent, increasing the rank of your Force circle by one (See Enchanting, below).

You attempt to form formal pact with a spirit. This requires 15 minutes of ritual, at the end of which you make an Occult check VS 35. On a success a spirit is willing to make a contract with you for 1 day or until you dismiss it.
This spirit will act as a conduit for your magic, granting you +1 Bonus Dice to spell casting checks, and + 1 Bonus dice to Resistance checks against magic.
While your bond remains your spirit will provide one of the following benefits, which you must select when you form your pact.

Bastion of Wards. The spirit forms a persistent ward that grants +8 Armor Rank against magical and elemental damage while the Bastion holds. Spirit Weapon. The spirit can form a spirit weapon for the caster. This magical armament deals 2d10 damage plus Soul Bonus Dice regardless of its form.

Well of Elements. The spirit acts as a potent battery of magical energy, holding 10 Mana for each of you Soul Bonus Dice.

You sanctify relics with divine law. You may sanctify amulets, reliquaries, and blessed charms (Charms and Amulets are equipment mods found in the equipment section) with 2 days of work and an Occult check VS 25. Reliquaries are special amulets that can hold 20 Mana for each of your Soul bonus dice. Once used, the energy is exhausted, and the reliquary becomes inert.

Your mastery of matter lets you refine core materials. With an Occult Check VS 35 and 20 Mana you can increase a material, useable part, or core component’s quality by 1 rank. You can also refine materials to a higher rank. Materials may only be perfected once; further attempts automatically fail. Legendary and Mystic-tier materials cannot normally be Transmuted.
You are an undisputed master of the occult and magic. You add +1 bonus dice to Occult checks, including spell and ritual casting checks, to a total of +3. You may learn Master spells and rituals.
The spirits whisper secrets and sorceries. When you cast a spell or any kind, you can always take 1 additional action while casting to add +1 Bonus Dice to your casting roll.
When you summon a Servitor, you can intreat that entity to instruct you in an Apocryphal ritual, provided it remains long enough to teach you.
When you form a spirit contract, you may take 2 Chances (+30 VS) to form a Pact with that spirit, keeping your spirit bound indefinitely.
You can invite a divine or numinous presence into your body. With 2 actions and an Occult check VS 45 you can invite one of the following miracles:
Healing. You lay on hands and restore 3d10 + Soul Bonus Dice to Durability.
Exorcism. Target a possessing or bound spirit; divine energy forces the entity to flee or be destroyed unless it can succeed a Resistance Check VS your Occult action roll.
Revelation. You are granted the knowledge of one blessing ritual.
You have the knowledge and ability to craft Carmot – the philosipher’s stone. Using Carmot as a reagent in an alchemical formula increases the quality rank of the creation by one, up to Legendary.

Talents give you unique bonuses or abilities or modify your skills. Most talents are purchased through skill trees, but some can be purchased outright. These are General Talents. While most talents will provide a static bonus to a trait or an action roll, some will simply provide access to other abilities, allowing you to develop some extraordinary skills.

Talents cost 1 skill point to acquire. Most talents can only be purchased once, but a few can be purchased more than once, each time gaining the benefits listed. This will be listed in the description.

Prerequisites. Some general talents are only available to 1evel 1 characters, others may require you are that you are level 2 or have another talent as a prerequisite. Otherwise, these talents are available to all characters.

Choose one extreme climate: heat or cold. When you are forced to act in these extreme climates, you have reduced penalties. If you choose heat, you ignore the penalties for Hot temperatures and treat Scorching temperatures as only hot. If you choose cold, you ignore the penalties for Chilly temperatures and treat Frigid conditions as only chilly.

Finally, when you make a Resistance check against extreme temperatures, regardless of your choice, you may reroll any 1 you roll on each dice. However, you must take the second roll (even if it is a 1).
1st Level Talent

Characters with ambidexterity possess aptitude with each hand equally and may use either hand as their dominant hand. You may use two weapons with greater ease, choosing which weapon is the dominant hand in any round. Otherwise, you have no other advantages and must learn dual-wield fighting like any other character or face the associated penalty.
You find that your ability to learn new material is improved significantly. When you roll to learn a new technique or spell, you receive 1 Charge on your roll.

With this talent, one of your ability scores improves at an accelerated rate. Choose one ability score when you select Aptitude; each time you increase that ability score through a skill or talent, you move the dice you roll up 1 rank. Each time you purchase this talent, you must select a new ability score.

You can create Art in your downtime using Crafting (visual), Entertain (poetry and music), or Science and technology (digital). The art you create is ordinary quality, but you may take a chance to increase its quality by 1 rank.
You have a way with words. When you are called upon to make a Social or Entertain check and are able to spend at least one additional turn speaking, you receive 1 free Charge on your roll.
Level 2, Novice Heavy Weapons
Choose one Heavy Exotic Weapon, such as a meteor hammer, scythe, or three-piece staff. You are proficient in this weapon and may employ this weapon’s features.
Level 2, Novice Light Weapons
Choose one Light Exotic Weapon. This includes chain weapons, whips, chakras, sickles, and similar exotic weapons. You are proficient in this weapon and may employ this weapon’s features.
Level 2, Novice Ranged Weapons
Choose one Exotic Ranged Weapon or Exotic Firearm, be it a sling bow or hand cannon. You are proficient in this weapon and may employ this weapon’s features.
Level 2, Novice Unarmed Weapons
Choose one Exotic Unarmed Weapon. You are proficient in this weapon and may employ this weapon’s features.
Some people simply do not know fear. With this talent, you are naturally resistant to the effects of anxiety and terror, adding +2 bonus dice to all checks to resist these effects.
1st Level Talent

You possess breathtaking facial and body symmetry. Moreover, you move with exceptional grace. Your beauty allows you to add 1 Charge to all Social and Entertain checks where the target can see you clearly.

With a mixture of natural brawn and sturdy build, you are noticeably more resilient than the average character of similar ability and stature. You gain +3d6 to your Durability. You can purchase this talent once per level, each time increasing your Durability.
1st Level Talent

With this talent, you have better than 20/20 vision and are able to make out details at ranges much farther than the average person. For example, you could read this text from 25 feet away. You do not suffer the dice step penalty on attack rolls with ranged weapons made against targets in the Long range.
You have delved deeply into the mysteries you have learned. Choose one magic school you have access to. You learn 3 addition Spells from any Circles 1 through 3.
You have studied more deeply in your martial schools. Choose one combat school you have access to. You learn 2 additional Techniques.

You’ve trained extensively to develop a superior mnemonic capacity. Add +1d4 to your Memory Score, and you add 1 Charge to your bonus dice on any attempt to memorize a discrete piece of information, such as a code, password, phrase, number, or technique or spell.

You’re keenly aware of other creatures’ behaviors, body language, and micro expressions. This gives you an advantage on interpersonal checks, such as Awareness to detect lies and falsehood, Social checks, and Entertain checks. In these situations, you get 1 Charge on your bonus dice.
Your spirit overflows with energy. Roll +5d6 and add this amount to your Mana. You may purchase this talent up to once per level, each time adding +5d6 to your maximum Mana.
1st Level Talent
You possess a perfect sense of pitch and tone and are musically talented. You receive +1 bonus dice to musical Entertain checks and hearing Awareness checks.
Select one of your ability scores and increase that score by 1 feeble (d4) dice. Prowess can be purchased once per level and applied to different abilities or to the same score, it does not matter.
Under sudden pressure and threat, you never hesitate. When you roll for Initiative, you may add your Speed Bonus Dice to your roll.

While you are engaged in combat, you may spend 1 action on your turn to observe and study an opponent as they attack and defend. If you are able to do this for two consecutive turns you gain an insight into their fighting style, learning one of the following:

items: Attack Bonus or highest weapon skill bonus compared to your own (lower, about the same, higher, much higher)
Defense Bonus or highest defense skill bonus compared to your own (lower, about the same, higher, much higher)
Total Vigor compared to your own (lower, about the same, higher, much higher)
Total Durability compared to your own (lower, about the same, higher, much higher)
Any Techniques they have used

blocks.html: You’ve a natural reservoir of physical energy. Increase your Vigor by +5d6 points. You may purchase this talent once per level, each time adding +5d6 to your Vigor.

The contract was supposed to be a metaphor. The name was supposed to be just a word. You learned, the way every Bargained-Word learns, that some words are doors. Some names are addresses. And the things on the other side of those addresses remember every syllable that has ever reached them across the threshold. The Path of Vile Names is the path of pacts — of careful negotiation with entities whose interests are not yours, whose patience is not infinite, and whose memory is exact. Every talent on this path is a clause signed in red. Every name learned is a leash that runs both ways.
Bargained-Word Initiate

You have crossed the threshold and spoken with what answered. The conversation cannot be undone, and you bear its echo in the back of your throat for the rest of your life. You speak and understand one Old Tongue of your choice — Infernal, Sepulchral, Vermillion, or Ash — instinctively, as if remembering a language you once spoke in sleep. You can sense pact-bound entities and creatures of contract-magic within 60 feet, perceiving them as a faint pressure behind the ears.

You begin knowing two Pact-Words — short invocations of named lesser entities. As an Action and 5 Mana, you may speak a Pact-Word to receive a single, immediate boon: +1 BD on your next roll, knowledge of a single concealed fact in the immediate area, a 5-foot warding glyph that lasts one round, or a similar minor effect agreed with the GM. Each known Pact-Word may be invoked once per scene. You learn one additional Pact-Word at every odd character level.
Gain an initial Corruption Score of 2d6.
Lesser Pact

You inscribe and sign a binding contract with a lesser named entity — an imp, a salt-revenant, a hearth-fiend, a parchment-fly. With 10 minutes of ritual and 20 Mana, you summon and bind a Lesser Servitor for 1 hour per Soul Bonus Die. Statistics follow standard Servitor rules (see Diabolism), but the servitor is named — it remembers you, and the next time you summon a creature of its court, the negotiation is easier or harder depending on how you treated the last one. The GM tracks your reputation among the named courts as a hidden faction score. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d6.
Tongue of the Threshold

You speak the binding cadences of the Old Tongues. Once per scene, as an Action and 8 Mana, you may pronounce a creature's descriptor or station — "the wolf at my door," "the captain of this watch," "the woman who killed my brother" — and issue a single command of up to ten words. The target must Resist (Resolve) VS your Corruption Score or obey for one round. If you know the target's true name, the command lasts until the end of the encounter and the Resistance suffers 1 Challenge. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d6.
Sigilcraft of the Bound

You learn to inscribe pact-sigils that constrain entities or empower the pacts you have already made. With 1 hour of work and 20 Mana, you may inscribe one of the following: a Binding Circle (an entity of Corruption equal to or less than yours cannot cross until the circle is broken or 24 hours pass), a Charter Sigil (your next bound servitor gains +1 BD to all rolls and lasts double duration), or a Ward of the Named (a creature within 10 feet of the sigil may use your Corruption Score as their Resistance against charm, possession, and domination by other corrupted entities). A sigil holds until triggered, broken, or 1 week passes. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d6.
Greater Pact

You may now bind named entities of significant station — barbed envoys, minor infernals, court-clerks of the dark sees. The ritual requires 30 minutes, 40 Mana, and 1d6 Health as tribute. The Greater Servitor has Durability equal to 10 × your Soul BD, Armor Rank equal to your Corruption BD, deals 3d8 + Soul BD damage on its attacks, has 3 Actions per round, and persists for 1 day per Soul BD. The bound entity may possess one signature ability appropriate to its court (a court-clerk reads thoughts, an envoy flies, a barbed thing burns those it grapples), at GM discretion. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d8.
Name-Reader

You can extract truenames from those who hold them. With 1 hour of meditation focused on a single target you have observed, an Occult check VS the target's Corruption Score + 20 (or VS 25 for mortals without a Corruption Score), you learn the target's truename. The truename functions as written above for Tongue of the Threshold and may be sold, traded, or inscribed onto pact-sigils. A creature whose truename is learned senses it dimly, as a chill in the bones at the moment the reading completes. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d8.
Court Tribute

You have learned that the courts accept payment in flesh. As a free action, you may sacrifice Health to fuel any Occult casting or pact-talent at a ratio of 1 Health : 2 Mana. The Mana so generated must be spent on the same turn or it is lost — the tribute is paid, the boon is granted, the ledger closes. Health spent this way cannot be recovered by ordinary rest for 24 hours; the wound is a covenant, and covenants take time to seal. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d8.
Compel the Named

When you know a creature's truename, you may Compel them at any distance on the same plane. The target receives a Resistance check VS your Corruption Score with 2 Challenges. On a failure, they are Enthralled for 1 hour and must carry out a single instruction of up to one sentence — not necessarily immediately, but with all reasonable urgency. The instruction may be delivered telepathically through the named bond. This costs 20 Mana per use and may target only one creature per day per Soul BD. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d8.
Court of Whispers

You no longer serve one patron. You serve many, and they serve you, and the ledger between you grows so vast that the individual debts cease to matter. You may maintain a number of active pacts (including bound servitors and known truenames in active use) equal to twice your Corruption Bonus Dice. Once per scene, you may call upon the Court of Whispers — describe what you need, and the GM selects which of your patrons answers. The answer may not be welcome, but it will be useful. Costs 40 Mana. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d10.
Banish the Named

You know the lawful methods of forcible return. As an Action and 30 Mana, target an eldritch, infernal, or contract-bound creature within 60 feet whose Corruption Score is less than yours. The target must Resist (Soul) VS your Corruption Score or be immediately and permanently returned to its plane of origin. Even on a success, the target suffers 4d10 + Corruption BD damage as the binding tears at the edges of its form. Beings without a home plane (eldritch horrors, true devils of the deepest courts) are not banished but suffer the damage and are Stunned for one round. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d10.
The Black Ink

You have learned to write contracts that hold mortals as well as entities. With a willing or coerced signatory, an Occult check VS 35, and 1 hour of inscription, you create a binding contract on parchment, skin, or stone. The contract specifies a service (an act, a span of loyalty, the surrender of a memory or possession) and a price (mortal flesh, Mana, time). If either party breaks the contract, the breaker suffers 1d10 Health damage per Corruption Bonus Die of the contract's author, ignoring all armor and resistance, and is treated as Wounded until the contract is renegotiated or dissolved. The Black Ink does not require the signatory to understand what they have agreed to. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d10.
Lord of the Dark Court

You are recognized by the named courts as a peer — not a supplicant, not a customer, but a lord. Once per session, with 1 minute of ritual and 80 Mana, you may summon a named entity from one of the courts you have dealt with. The entity arrives, listens to your request, and grants a single boon of significant power: a portent of the future, the killing of a specific creature whose name you provide, the corruption of an artifact, the answering of one question of cosmic significance. The boon is granted. The price will be presented at a time and place of the court's choosing. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d12.
Final Word

You have learned to speak sentences that cannot be unsaid. Once per week, as an Action and 100 Mana, you may pronounce a Final Word upon a target you can see and name. The Word is a single clear declaration — "Yvain Doloux will love no one but me," "the stones of this tower will never know peace," "this child will not see her thirteenth winter" — and it inscribes itself into the local fabric of fate. The target may Resist (Soul) VS your Corruption Score; on a success, the Word fails and you lose 1 point of permanent Soul to the recoil. On a failure, the Word holds. It may be undone only by a Final Word of equal or greater authority, or by the destruction of the speaker. Use this talent with care. The world remembers what you have said. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d12.
Vassalage Inverted

The traffic that ran one way has begun to run the other. Your patrons — at least the ones you still pay tribute to — now serve at your call more than you serve at theirs. The Health cost of Court Tribute is halved (rounded down). The Mana cost of all Pact-Words, Lesser Pacts, and Greater Pacts is reduced by 25%. You may Compel the Named at no Mana cost once per day. However, your Corruption Score increases by 2 every time you sleep in the same place for more than three nights running — the courts are now invested in you, and they expect you to keep moving, keep working, keep paying interest on the leverage you have accumulated. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d12.
The Black Covenant

This is the threshold. The last contract. You sign yourself permanently and entirely into the registers of the dark courts, and in exchange you become something that mortal law no longer fully describes. Your creature type changes to Eldritch. You gain immunity to disease and natural aging, +20 permanent Mana, and your Corruption Score now functions as a Resistance Score for all checks against charm, fear, possession, and domination by entities not of your courts. When you would be reduced to 0 Health, once per day, your patrons intervene: you stabilize at 1 Health, and one randomly determined patron presents an immediate demand that must be answered within 24 hours.
You have not died. You have been archived. The distinction will become clearer over time. Increase your Corruption Score by 1d20.
Witness in the Glass

Your reflection sometimes speaks for you, and sometimes contradicts you. Once per day at GM discretion, a reflective surface near you will display you saying or doing something other than what you are actually saying or doing. The reflection's actions are often more honest than yours.
The Inscribed Skin

Sigils slowly form on your flesh — fine lines like calligraphy that no needle made. The marks are faintly cold to the touch and visible through ordinary clothing. You suffer 1 Challenge on Legerdemain (Disguise) checks. Each additional time you develop this malediction, the marks deepen, glow faintly in darkness, or begin to rearrange themselves; the Challenge increases by 1, to a maximum of 3.
Bound Voice

You cannot speak without the cadence of contracts. Your statements parse as proposals, your questions as offers. You suffer 1 Challenge on Social (Persuasion) checks where casual sincerity is required. You gain +1 BD on Social (Negotiation) checks.
The Court's Notice

The named courts know where you are at all times. Entities and the Templars they answer to can locate you with a 1-hour ritual. You will be visited by debt-collectors, rivals, and recruiters with increasing frequency. The GM should treat this as a setting fact, not a periodic check.
Honor of the Named

Once you have given your word in earnest, you cannot break it without suffering. Breaking a sincerely-given promise costs you 2d6 Health and 1d6 Mana, and you are treated as Wounded for the next 24 hours. The promise need not have been formal — a casual assurance carries the same weight.
The Inkwell

You bleed black ink instead of red. The first wound you take in any encounter costs an additional die of damage as the ink resists clotting. Cuts to your hands stain everything you touch for hours afterward.
Names Unspoken

You can no longer say your own name aloud. The word will not form in your throat. Others may speak it freely, and you may write it, but the syllables refuse to leave your mouth. You suffer 1 Challenge on all Reputation checks where personal identification is required.
Plurality

Under stress — combat, fear, sudden emotion — other voices speak through you. Brief phrases in tongues you do not consciously know, comments from the wrong gender or age, observations that you could not have made. Anyone within 10 feet of you during such an event must make a Resistance (Resolve) VS your Corruption Score or suffer Anxiety until the end of the encounter.
The Open Book

You cannot conceal your contracts from those who know to look. Entities, Templars, and corrupted creatures of comparable rank can read your active pacts as plainly as you might read a tavern's name above its door. You cannot infiltrate any organization that knows what you are.
Bargainer's Sleep
