While in this stance, you gain +2 Bonus Dice to weapon damage, ignore the first source of damage of each type you take each round, and recover 5 Vigor at the start of each of your turns. Ends when you are no longer Wounded, are knocked unconscious, or choose to end it. Considered the apex stance of the Hard school. Qualifies as Aggressive Stance.

Iron Strike — Vigor 2. A fully committed swing. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. In Aggressive Stance, if any damage Bonus Die bursts on the hit, the target loses 5 Guard until the end of your next turn.

Pressing Step — Vigor 2. You step into your opponent's space as you swing. Make a basic attack; on a hit, the target cannot willingly move more than 5 feet on their next turn unless they spend 5 Vigor. In Aggressive Stance, increase the restriction to 10 feet.

Bone-Jarring Blow — Vigor 3. A heavy strike intended to rattle rather than finish. Make a basic attack; on a hit, the target suffers 1 Challenge on their next basic attack roll. In Aggressive Stance, the Challenge also applies to their first Defense reaction before the end of their next turn.

Hewing Strike — Vigor 3. You aim for the joint, the seam, the gap. Make a basic attack ignoring armor of one Quality Rank lower than your weapon. In Aggressive Stance, ignore armor of equal Quality Rank as well.

Anvil Blow — Vigor 6. The signature blow of the school. Make an attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage. If the target is Wounded, add 1 Charge to those Bonus Dice. In Aggressive Stance, Large-size and smaller targets are knocked prone on a hit.

Armor-Splitter — Vigor 7. A blow aimed to ruin rather than kill. Make an attack ignoring armor of equal or lower Quality Rank. On a hit, reduce the target's Armor Rank by 2 until their armor is repaired. In Aggressive Stance, the armor itself also takes 2d6 damage.

Battering Onslaught — Vigor 8. Two heavy strikes in succession. Make two basic attacks against a single target. The second gains +1 Bonus Die to damage if the first hit. In Aggressive Stance, if the first attack hit, the target cannot use Defense reactions against the second.

Driving Shove — Vigor 5. A weapon-and-body crash with the terrain as accomplice. Make a Strength check VS the target's Stamina or Strength Score. On success, push the target 5 feet and create an Opening. If they collide with terrain or another creature, they fall prone. In Aggressive Stance, push 10 feet instead.

Sundering Blow — Vigor 7. A blow that finds the weak point in a tool. Make an attack. If you hit and any damage Bonus Die bursts, choose one of the target's items (weapon, shield, helm, etc.) and apply weapon damage to it. In Aggressive Stance, the chosen item also drops 1 Quality Rank until repaired.

Wolf's Grip — Vigor 6. You catch them as you cut them. Make a basic attack; on a hit, you may immediately attempt to Grapple the target as a free action without spending the usual Vigor. In Aggressive Stance, grappled targets suffer 1 Bonus Die of damage at the start of each of your turns while you maintain the hold.

Bonebreaker — Vigor 12. You strike to maim, not merely to wound. Make an attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage. If the attack deals Health damage, the target suffers a Broken Bone: lose 1d4 from one physical Ability Score (Strength, Agility, or Speed; target's choice) until they receive surgical or magical healing. In Aggressive Stance, you choose which Ability Score is reduced.

Splitting Cleave — Vigor 10. One stroke, two enemies. Make a single attack that strikes your primary target and one adjacent target. Both defend separately and take full weapon damage on a hit. In Aggressive Stance, the primary target is also knocked prone on a hit.

Pulping Strike — Vigor 14. A blow timed to land between heartbeats. Make an attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, these dice automatically burst. The target is Stunned until the end of their next turn. In Aggressive Stance, the target is Stunned until the end of your next turn instead.

Crushing Riposte — Vigor 10. Reaction. When an enemy adjacent to you misses you with a melee attack, you may immediately make a basic attack against them with +1 Bonus Die to damage. This is a Hard answer to a Soft moment: the failed strike is its own opening. In Aggressive Stance, the Crushing Riposte gains an additional +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Stand and Strike — Vigor 10. A planted stance-change executed mid-combat, briefly. Until the end of your next turn, you cannot be Pushed, Pulled, or Knocked Prone, and you gain +2 Bonus Dice to weapon damage. You cannot move further than 5 feet during this time. This technique requires Aggressive Stance to use. While maintained, the technique's effects stack with any active Hard Light Weapons stance.

Shield-Breaker — Vigor 12. A blow aimed specifically at the defender's defense. Make an attack targeting an opponent's shield. The attack ignores the shield's cover bonus and applies weapon damage directly to the shield's Durability, ignoring its Armor Rank. In Aggressive Stance, if the shield breaks, the wielder is also knocked prone.

Worldbreaker — Vigor 22. The blow you have practiced ten thousand times, for this moment. Make an attack with +4 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, all of your damage Bonus Dice automatically burst. The target is knocked back 10 feet and falls prone. In Aggressive Stance, the attack gains Threat equal to the number of distinct Hard Light Weapons techniques you have used since the start of the encounter, to a maximum of Threat 6.

Tyrant's Sweep — Vigor 25. A single, terrible arc. Make one attack that strikes every enemy within your weapon's melee reach. Each defends separately. On each hit, apply weapon damage with +2 Bonus Dice and knock the target prone. In Aggressive Stance, the attack cannot be answered with Defense reactions.

Skull-Splitting Doom — Vigor 20. The killing strike, delivered as if there will be no more after it. Make an attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage. If the damage dealt exceeds the target's remaining Durability, double the overflow damage applied to Health. In Aggressive Stance, the doubled overflow applies even when Durability is not exhausted.

Final Strike — Vigor 25. The technique the Hard school is whispered about for. Make an attack against a target at 0 Durability or below half Health. On a hit, the target must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your attack roll or be killed instantly. In Aggressive Stance, if the target is below 25% Health, no Resistance check is allowed.

Named Chains are specific sequences of school techniques that, when performed in the same round against the same target, produce an additional effect beyond the techniques themselves. These names are generic; GMs and players are encouraged to rename them in setting.

The Three-Hit Doctrine — Iron Strike → Anvil Blow → any Expert or Master Hard Light Weapons technique against the same target. If all three techniques hit, the final technique's damage is doubled when the target is prone, restrained, or Stunned at the moment the strike lands.

Press to Ruin — Driving Shove + Pressing Step or Battering Onslaught + Sundering Blow or Armor-Splitter, performed in any order against the same target during one round. On completion, the target suffers 1 Challenge on all attack rolls until the end of their next turn — armor loosened, footing lost, weapon-arm shaken.

The school of yielding to redirect. The practitioner does not block; they guide. They do not refuse the strike; they let it pass and answer it. The weapon moves in small arcs, conserving motion, reading the opponent's tempo and pressure. Every attack the opponent makes is studied. Every miss is invited. Every successful Defense is itself the start of the next attack. The Soft school does not seek to be untouched. It seeks to make every contact serve its purpose.

Yielding Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You hold the weapon loose and high, ready to flow with any attack. While in this stance, you gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense reactions with the weapon, and after any successful Defense reaction, your next basic attack before the end of your next turn gains +1 Bonus Die to damage. Ends when you make an attack action while not following a successful Defense, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance for class features.

Mirror Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You move only when your opponent moves. While in this stance, when an adjacent enemy makes a basic attack that misses you, you may immediately make a basic attack against them with no Vigor cost — the attack uses the weapon you are holding and gains +1 Bonus Die. You may benefit from Mirror Stance no more than once per turn. Ends when you take a non-reaction attack action, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance.

Master's Repose — Vigor 15 to enter. You appear to act last. While in this stance, you may delay your Initiative by up to 10 in any combat round, choosing to act at any point during the round after that delay. All attacks against you made by creatures who acted before you in the round suffer 1 Challenge. When you do act, your first attack or reaction this round gains +2 Bonus Dice to damage. Ends at the end of the round; must be re-entered each round at the listed Vigor cost. Considered the apex stance of the Soft school. Qualifies as Defensive Stance.

Beat — Vigor 2. On your turn, make an Agility check VS the target's weapon attack roll bonus (1d20 + their attack BD). On success, you strike their weapon aside, creating an Opening against them. No damage is dealt by this technique. In Defensive Stance, you also gain +1 Bonus Die on your next Defense reaction before the end of your next turn.

Bind — Vigor 3. You catch the opponent's weapon with yours. Both you and the target may not make weapon attack rolls until they succeed an Agility check VS your weapon skill roll. While the Bind holds, neither party may take Openings against the other. The Bind ends at the start of your next turn unless you maintain it with 1 Action. In Defensive Stance, while binding, you gain +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions against attacks from creatures other than the bound target.

Parry-Riposte — Vigor 3. Reaction. When an adjacent enemy makes a melee attack against you and misses, you may immediately make a basic weapon attack against them with no damage bonus. In Defensive Stance, the Parry-Riposte gains +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Yielding Step — Vigor 2. Reaction. When targeted by a melee attack from an adjacent enemy, move 5 feet in any direction without creating an Opening. If you move out of the attacker's reach, the attack misses automatically. If you remain in reach, the attack proceeds normally. In Defensive Stance, you also gain +1 Bonus Die on the Defense reaction if you choose to defend after the step.

Disarming Twist — Vigor 6. Reaction. When you successfully Defend against a melee weapon attack, you may attempt to disarm the attacker. They make a Resistance (Strength) check VS your Defense roll or lose their weapon. The weapon falls 5 feet in a direction of your choice. In Defensive Stance, the target makes the Resistance check at 1 Challenge.

Counter-Time — Vigor 7. Reaction. When an enemy declares a Technique against you that requires multiple Actions to complete, you may interrupt it with a basic weapon attack at +1 Bonus Die to damage. If your attack hits and deals damage, the enemy's Technique fails but the Vigor is still spent. In Defensive Stance, if the Technique was an Expert or Master rank, gain +1 additional Bonus Die to damage.

Drawing the Storm — Vigor 5. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, enemies who attack you suffer 1 Challenge on their first attack roll against you. When such an attack misses, you recover 2 Vigor. In Defensive Stance, missed attacks also create an Opening you may exploit.

Riposte — Vigor 7. Reaction. After successfully Defending against an adjacent enemy's melee attack, make a basic weapon attack against them with +1 Bonus Die to damage. In Defensive Stance, the Riposte gains an additional +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Defense in Depth — Vigor 6. You set yourself to defend repeatedly. Until the start of your next turn, you may use Defense reactions against up to two different attacks without paying additional Vigor for the second reaction. In Defensive Stance, the second Defense reaction also gains +1 Bonus Die.

Cutting Bind — Vigor 8. When you are currently in a Bind with an enemy weapon, you may slip your blade along the binding. As an Action, make a basic weapon attack against the bound target with +1 Bonus Die to damage. The Bind ends at the resolution of this attack regardless of outcome. In Defensive Stance, the target cannot use Defense reactions against the Cutting Bind.

Perfect Parry — Vigor 10. Reaction. When targeted by a melee attack, you make a Defense reaction at +2 Bonus Dice. If the Defense succeeds, the attack misses entirely with no incidental damage of any kind. In Defensive Stance, also recover 5 Vigor on a successful Perfect Parry.

Riposte of Iron — Vigor 12. Reaction. After a successful Defense reaction against an adjacent enemy's melee attack, make a basic weapon attack against them with +2 Bonus Dice to damage and Threat 2. In Defensive Stance, the Threat increases to 4.

Time-Stopper — Vigor 14. Reaction. When an enemy declares a Technique against you, force them to make a Resolve check VS your Defense roll to complete it. On failure, the Technique fails but the Vigor is still spent. This may be used against any Technique that targets you specifically. In Defensive Stance, the Resolve check is at 1 Challenge.

Mirror of Steel — Vigor 12. Reaction. The next melee attack made against you within the next round is deflected onto an adjacent enemy of your choice. The original attacker rolls their attack against your Defense; on success, the damage is applied to the redirected target instead. No deflection is possible if no adjacent target exists. In Defensive Stance, you may also choose to deflect to any enemy within 10 feet rather than only adjacent.

Cutting Counter — Vigor 11. Reaction. After a successful Defense reaction, make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. If the attack deals Health damage, the target suffers Bleeding (1d6 per round) until they receive medical attention. In Defensive Stance, the Bleeding damage Bonus Die bursts on 9 or 10.

Master's Read — Vigor 10. Stance change. Choose one opponent within 30 feet you have seen attack in this combat. Until the end of your next turn, you gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions against that opponent and +1 Bonus Die on damage rolls against them. You may only have one Master's Read active at a time. In Defensive Stance, the duration extends to the end of the combat or until you choose to end it.

Untouchable — Vigor 22. Until the end of your next turn, all melee attacks against you automatically miss. You may not make attack actions during this time, though you may make Defense reactions for allies within your reach and use techniques that do not require an attack. In Defensive Stance, you may also make one basic weapon attack at the end of the duration as a free action with +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Storm's Eye — Vigor 20. Reaction. When targeted by a melee attack you can see, you may consume your Destined state to redirect the full intended damage of that attack back to the attacker. The attacker takes the damage they would have dealt to you, with all its Bonus Dice and effects. You take no damage from the attack. In Defensive Stance, the redirected damage gains +2 Bonus Dice.

Mirror's Edge — Vigor 25. Until the end of your next turn, whenever an enemy makes a melee attack against you, they roll the attack twice and take the worse result. Any attack that misses you deals weapon damage (yours, no Bonus Dice) to the attacker as the failed strike turns on them. In Defensive Stance, missed attackers also drop 5 Guard until the end of their next turn.

Final Yield — Vigor 20. Reaction. When targeted by any attack — melee or ranged, weapon or technique — you may redirect it to any other valid target within the original attacker's reach. The attacker rolls again against the new target, using all original modifiers, against the new target's Defense. The original attack against you is treated as never made. In Defensive Stance, you may also choose to be the new target yourself, gaining +3 Bonus Dice on the resulting Defense if you do.

Wave Pattern — Beat → Bind → Riposte performed in sequence against the same target within one round. If all three succeed (the Beat creates an Opening, the Bind holds for at least one turn, and the Riposte hits), the Riposte deals +3 Bonus Dice to damage.

Mirror's Cycle — Three reactions performed in a single round against attacks from adjacent enemies. On completion, gain one Bonus Action that may be used during your next turn to take any Action requiring a single Action.

The school of denied trade through speed. The practitioner moves more than they strike. They strike more than they linger. They are never quite where the opponent expected them to be. The weapon is light in the hand, the feet are always moving, the head is always tracking the next position rather than the current one. Where Hard schools commit to the meeting and Soft schools convert the meeting, Swift schools refuse the meeting itself. The opponent commits; the practitioner is already three feet to the left.

Light Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You hold the weapon loosely, feet ready. While in this stance, you gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense reactions and may move 5 feet without spending an Action once per turn; this movement does not create Openings. Ends when you fail to use the free movement for two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance for class features.

Hawk Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You read the opponent's openings and slip through them. While in this stance, your basic Light Weapons attacks gain Threat 2 against any target within your reach. If you moved 10 feet or more on your turn before the attack, increase to Threat 4. Ends if you fail to move at all on two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance.

Dance of Edges — Vigor 15 to enter. Your weapon and feet are continually in motion. While in this stance, you may make up to three basic weapon attacks per turn against any targets within your reach as a single Action. Each attack costs 5 Vigor and suffers -1 Bonus Die to damage. You may move 5 feet between each attack without creating Openings. Ends after one round; must be re-entered each round at the listed Vigor cost. Considered the apex stance of the Swift school. Qualifies as Measured Stance.

Quick Cut — Vigor 2. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll (not damage). You receive 1 Challenge on Defense reactions until the end of your next turn. In Measured Stance, the Challenge is removed.

Slip and Strike — Vigor 3. Move 5 feet in any direction as part of this action, then make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. The movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, you may move 10 feet instead.

Skirmisher's Step — Vigor 2. Make a basic weapon attack. Immediately after the attack resolves, move up to 5 feet in any direction without creating an Opening, even if you are within enemy reach. In Measured Stance, move up to 10 feet instead.

Twin Cut — Vigor 3. If you are dual-wielding two Light Weapons, make a basic attack with your primary weapon, then a free additional basic attack with your off-hand at -1 Bonus Die to damage. The off-hand attack does not consume an Action. In Measured Stance, the off-hand attack suffers no damage penalty.

Eagle's Strike — Vigor 5. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. Immediately after the attack resolves, move up to 10 feet without creating an Opening. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage as well.

Flicker Cut — Vigor 6. Make two basic weapon attacks against the same target with -1 Bonus Die to damage on each. If both hit, the target suffers Bleeding (1d4 per round) until they receive medical attention. In Measured Stance, the second attack does not suffer the damage penalty.

Lunge and Withdraw — Vigor 7. Extend your reach by 5 feet for a single weapon attack. After the attack resolves (hit or miss), return to your starting square as a free action. No Openings are created by this movement. In Measured Stance, extend reach by 10 feet instead.

Whirlwind — Vigor 8. Attack one target within your reach with a basic weapon attack. After the attack resolves, move up to 5 feet; if you end your movement adjacent to another enemy, you may make a second basic weapon attack against that target at -1 Bonus Die to damage. In Measured Stance, the second attack suffers no damage penalty.

Tempo Break — Vigor 5. Reaction. When an adjacent enemy hits you with a melee attack, you may immediately move 5 feet without creating an Opening, and create an Opening on the attacker that your allies may exploit until the end of your next turn. In Measured Stance, move up to 10 feet instead.

Phantom Step — Vigor 6. Move up to your full Speed in feet without creating Openings, even when moving through enemy reach. Until the end of your next turn, you gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense reactions. In Measured Stance, the Defense bonus extends to +2 Bonus Dice.

Killing Tempo — Vigor 12. Make three basic weapon attacks against the same target with -2 Bonus Dice to damage on each. The first hit deals normal damage with the penalty; the second adds 1 Charge to the damage roll; the third adds 2 Charges. Each attack is resolved independently against the target's Defense. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty entirely.

Edged Dance — Vigor 10. Move up to 30 feet in a path of your choosing. Make one basic weapon attack with -1 Bonus Die to damage against up to three targets you pass within your weapon's reach. Each defends separately. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty and gain Threat 2 on each attack.

Storm of Blades — Vigor 14. If you are dual-wielding two Light Weapons, make four basic weapon attacks split between the two weapons in any combination. Each attack suffers -2 Bonus Dice to damage and is resolved independently. In Measured Stance, the damage penalty is reduced to -1 Bonus Die.

Cutting Wind — Vigor 11. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, your basic weapon attacks gain Threat 4 and you may move 5 feet without creating an Opening with each attack you make. Requires Measured Stance to use. The duration extends to the end of the round when used in Measured Stance.

Vanishing Strike — Vigor 12. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. Immediately after the attack resolves, make a Stealth check VS the highest Awareness of any enemy that can see you. On success, you are Hidden until your next attack action or until an enemy passes a contested Awareness check to spot you. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to the Stealth check.

Tempo Lock — Vigor 10. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to attack roll. On a successful hit dealing damage, the target loses one Action on their next turn — their entire action economy is disrupted by the precision of your strike. In Measured Stance, on a hit where any damage Bonus Die bursts, the target also loses their reaction for one round.

Dance of a Hundred Cuts — Vigor 22. Make as many basic weapon attacks as your Speed Bonus Dice count against any targets within your reach during your movement. You may move up to your full Speed as part of this action. Each attack suffers -3 Bonus Dice to damage. Whenever any damage Bonus Die bursts during this technique, recover 5 Vigor. In Measured Stance, the damage penalty is reduced to -2 Bonus Dice and the Vigor recovery increases to 8 Vigor per burst.

Untouchable Step — Vigor 20. Until the end of your next turn, you are immune to Openings and to opportunity attacks. Your basic weapon attacks during this duration gain +1 Bonus Die to attack roll and you may move 10 feet without spending an Action once per turn. In Measured Stance, the duration extends to the end of the round.

Severing Wind — Vigor 25. Choose any straight line up to your full Speed in length. Move along this line, making one basic weapon attack against every enemy you pass within your weapon's reach. Each attack gains Threat 4. Each target defends separately. The movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage on each attack.

Final Cut — Vigor 20. Move up to twice your full Speed in feet, then make one basic weapon attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, the damage Bonus Dice automatically burst. If this attack reduces the target to 0 Health, your turn does not end — you may take an additional movement of up to your full Speed and one further Action. In Measured Stance, the additional movement after a kill becomes twice your Speed.

Hawk's Descent — Eagle's Strike → Lunge and Withdraw → Phantom Step performed in any order within a single round. On completion, gain 5 Vigor and you may make one additional basic weapon attack as a free action with +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Three Cuts — Three basic weapon attacks against the same target in a single turn, by any combination of attacks, techniques, or off-hand strikes. If all three hit, the third gains +2 Bonus Dice to damage and Threat 4, applied retroactively.

The school of mass meeting bone. Where Hard Light Weapons commits weight to the strike, Hard Heavy Weapons commits the entire mass of a long, heavy arm at the end of a swung arc, with the full momentum of body and weapon driving the impact. Armor that turns a light blade is split by an axe-head. Shields that hold against rapiers shatter under a maul. A wall of opponents becomes a single sweeping target. The Hard Heavy practitioner does not strike to wound. They strike to break — and what breaks tends to stay broken.

Sundering Stance — Vigor 8 to enter. You hold the weapon raised and ready, committed to the next blow. While in this stance, you gain +2 Bonus Dice to weapon damage rolls, but you cannot use Defense reactions and gain -1 Bonus Die on Initiative if combat begins anew. Ends when you take a Defense reaction, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Aggressive Stance for class features.

Earthbreaker Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. Your every swing aims for the foundation as much as the enemy. While in this stance, when you hit with a Heavy Weapons attack, the target makes Resistance (Stamina) VS the damage dealt or is knocked prone. You suffer -1 Bonus Die on attack rolls while in this stance. Ends when you fail to land a Heavy Weapons attack for two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Aggressive Stance.

Cataclysm Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. Your strikes carry the suggestion of the avalanche. While in this stance, your basic Heavy Weapons attacks affect every enemy within your weapon's melee reach, each defending separately at -1 Bonus Die to damage. Ends after three rounds, when you are knocked prone, or when you choose to end it. Considered the apex stance of the Hard Heavy school. Qualifies as Aggressive Stance.

Heavy Strike — Vigor 3. A committed two-handed swing. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a miss, you create an Opening on yourself due to the recovery time. In Aggressive Stance, you do not create the Opening on a miss.

Wide Swing — Vigor 3. A sweeping arc threatening multiple foes. Make one attack roll at -1 Bonus Die to attack; on success, apply weapon damage to every enemy within your weapon's melee reach, each defending separately. In Aggressive Stance, ignore the attack penalty.

Ground Smash — Vigor 3. You drive the weapon into the earth or floor at the target's feet. The target makes a Resistance (Speed) check VS your attack roll or falls prone. No weapon damage is dealt. In Aggressive Stance, on a failed Resistance, the target also suffers 5 reduction to Guard until the end of your next turn.

Skull-Cracker — Vigor 3. A vertical chopping blow aimed at the head. Make a basic attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the target suffers 1 Challenge on Resolve checks until the end of their next turn. In Aggressive Stance, the Challenge also applies to their next Resistance check.

Mountain Strike — Vigor 6. A committed two-handed swing with full body weight. Make a basic attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage. If you used a stance-changing technique on your previous turn, add 1 Charge to the damage Bonus Dice. In Aggressive Stance, also add 1 Charge regardless of stance change.

Earthbreaking Blow — Vigor 7. A blow aimed at the ground beside the target, sending shock outward. Make a basic attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, all enemies adjacent to the target make Resistance (Stamina) VS your attack roll or are knocked prone. In Aggressive Stance, the Resistance is at 1 Challenge.

Cleaving Onslaught — Vigor 8. A heavy sweep against two enemies. Choose two enemies adjacent to each other and within your reach. Make one basic attack, applying weapon damage with +1 Bonus Die to each on a hit. Each defends separately at 1 Challenge. In Aggressive Stance, the cleave may target three adjacent enemies.

Crushing Press — Vigor 5. On a successful weapon attack that deals damage, the target loses one Action on their next turn unless they spend 5 Vigor at the start of that turn. In Aggressive Stance, the Vigor cost to recover the Action increases to 10.

Bonebreak Swing — Vigor 7. A blow specifically meant to break a joint. Make a basic attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit dealing Health damage, the target makes Resistance (Stamina) VS the damage or one of their physical Ability Scores (target's choice) is reduced by 1 until they receive surgical or magical healing. In Aggressive Stance, you choose which Ability Score is reduced.

Sundering Stroke — Vigor 6. Reaction. When an adjacent enemy makes a Defense reaction with a weapon or shield against your attack or an ally's attack, you may make an immediate basic weapon attack against their weapon or shield with +1 Bonus Die to damage. This damage applies directly to the item's Durability, ignoring its Armor Rank. In Aggressive Stance, if the item breaks, the wielder is also knocked prone.

Cataclysm Strike — Vigor 12. A blow whose impact reshapes the ground itself. Make a basic attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage. On a hit, the terrain in a 10-foot radius around the impact point becomes Challenging Terrain (cracked, broken, strewn with debris) for the remainder of the encounter. In Aggressive Stance, the radius extends to 15 feet.

Tyrant's Cleave — Vigor 10. A 90-degree sweeping arc. Make one basic weapon attack against every enemy within your reach in a forward arc. Each defends separately and takes weapon damage on a hit. In Aggressive Stance, also add +1 Bonus Die to damage against each target.

Pulverizing Blow — Vigor 14. A blow timed for maximum impact. Make an attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage; these dice automatically burst on a hit. On a hit, the target is Stunned until the end of their next turn. In Aggressive Stance, the target is Stunned until the end of your next turn instead.

Sweeping Hand of Doom — Vigor 12. A full 360-degree sweep with the weapon. Make a basic weapon attack against every enemy within your reach, each defending separately at -1 Bonus Die to damage on your part. Any target attempting a Defense reaction against this attack must spend 1 additional Vigor. In Aggressive Stance, remove the damage penalty.

Shieldsplitter — Vigor 12. An attack specifically aimed at the opponent's shield. Make a basic attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. The attack ignores the shield's cover and Armor Rank bonuses entirely; if the target uses a Defense reaction with a shield, the shield itself takes the full damage of the attack. In Aggressive Stance, on damage exceeding the shield's remaining Durability, the wielder is also disarmed of the shield as it breaks.

Foundation Strike — Vigor 10. You strike the ground, wall, pillar, or other fixed surface within reach. Every enemy within 15 feet of the impact point makes Resistance (Speed) VS your attack roll or is knocked prone. In Aggressive Stance, enemies who fail the Resistance also suffer 1 Challenge on their next attack roll.

Mountainfall — Vigor 25. A blow with the full mass of the swing committed. Make an attack with +4 Bonus Dice to damage. On a hit, the target's Armor Rank drops to 0 until repaired. If the target is Wounded at the moment of impact, multiply overflow damage to Health by three. In Aggressive Stance, the Armor Rank reduction persists until the target completes a Long Rest, even after repair.

Earthbreaker's Wrath — Vigor 25. You drive the weapon downward with the full force of your body. Every enemy within a 30-foot radius makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your attack roll. Those who fail take full weapon damage with +2 Bonus Dice and are knocked prone. Those who succeed take half damage and remain standing. In Aggressive Stance, those who fail also suffer 1 rank of Fatigue.

Sundering Doom — Vigor 22. Choose a single item carried by an enemy within your reach — weapon, shield, helm, or armor piece. Make a basic attack targeting that item; on a hit, if the item is of lower Quality Rank than your weapon, it is destroyed. If of equal Quality Rank, it drops two Quality Ranks. If higher, it drops one. In Aggressive Stance, the item is destroyed if of equal or lower Quality Rank, and drops two Quality Ranks if of higher rank.

Final Cleave — Vigor 25. A swing that does not stop at the body. Make a basic weapon attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage and Threat 6. If this attack reduces the target to 0 Health, your turn does not end and you may move up to your full Speed and take one additional Action. In Aggressive Stance, the additional Action may be another Master-rank Heavy Weapons technique.

The Falling Mountain — Heavy Strike → Mountain Strike → Cataclysm Strike performed in sequence against the same target within one round. On completion, if the Cataclysm Strike hits, double its damage and increase the radius of its Challenging Terrain effect to 20 feet.

The Press — Ground Smash → Crushing Press → Bonebreak Swing performed in any order against the same target during one round. If all three succeed (the Ground Smash knocks prone, the Crushing Press damages, the Bonebreak Swing hits), the target is also Stunned until the end of your next turn.

The school of leverage and reach. The Hard Heavy practitioner brings mass to the impact; the Soft Heavy practitioner uses the length of the weapon to deny the impact ever happens. The blade or head of the weapon is held at distance — a wall of point and edge that punishes anyone who tries to close. Where a Hard Heavy fighter wants the enemy at arm's length and rotating into the swing, a Soft Heavy fighter wants the enemy six feet away and reconsidering. The weapon is shifted, choked up on, gripped at the haft, used as lever and brake and threat. The opponent does not get to choose the range.

Reach Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You hold the weapon at full length, point or head extended. While in this stance, your weapon's reach increases by 5 feet, and you may make a Defense reaction against any enemy attempting to move within your reach (treating their movement as if it were a melee attack against you). Ends when you choose to enter a different stance, are disarmed, or are knocked prone. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance for class features.

Bracing Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You plant the butt of the weapon and ready for the charge. While in this stance, you gain 1 Charge to weapon attack damage against any enemy who moves toward you on their turn, and you gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions against creatures attempting to charge into your reach. Ends when you move more than 5 feet on a turn, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance.

King's Wall Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. You set yourself as the immovable line. While in this stance, you and one ally within 5 feet of you gain +5 Guard. Enemies attempting to move into your reach must spend an additional 5 feet of movement to do so. You cannot move more than your weapon's reach allows while in this stance. Ends when you move beyond your weapon's reach, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. Considered the apex stance of the Soft Heavy school. Qualifies as Defensive Stance.

Set Against Charge — Vigor 2. Reaction. When an enemy moves into your weapon's reach during their turn, make a basic weapon attack against them with +1 Bonus Die to damage as they enter. In Defensive Stance, gain an additional +1 Bonus Die to damage and Threat 2.

Half-Sword Strike — Vigor 2. You shift your grip down the haft or blade for a controlled strike. Make a basic weapon attack at reduced reach (lose 5 feet of reach) but with Threat 2 and +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. In Defensive Stance, the Threat increases to 4.

Tripping Sweep — Vigor 3. Using the haft or hook of your weapon, you sweep at the target's legs. Make an Agility check VS the target's Speed or Stamina Score. On success, the target falls prone. No weapon damage is dealt. In Defensive Stance, on success the target also creates an Opening that allies may exploit until the end of your next turn.

Hooking Pull — Vigor 3. If your weapon has a hook (halberd, glaive, bardiche), you may attempt to pull the target with it. The target makes Resistance (Strength) VS your attack roll or is pulled 5 feet toward you. If the pull ends within your reach, you may create an Opening on them. In Defensive Stance, on success, the target also suffers 1 Challenge on their next basic attack.

Long Strike — Vigor 5. An attack at the very edge of your weapon's reach. Make a basic weapon attack at extended reach (+5 feet) with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll but -1 Bonus Die to damage. In Defensive Stance, remove the damage penalty.

Trip and Strike — Vigor 7. A combined motion using the haft to sweep and the head to strike. The target makes Resistance (Speed) VS your attack roll or falls prone. Whether or not they fall, make a basic weapon attack against them at +1 Bonus Die to damage as a free action. In Defensive Stance, against prone targets, also gain Threat 2.

Defensive Sweep — Vigor 6. Reaction. When an enemy attempts to move into your weapon's reach, you may make a Strength check VS their Stamina or Strength Score to push them 5 feet away rather than allow the movement. No damage is dealt; the movement is denied. In Defensive Stance, push 10 feet instead.

Anchored Defense — Vigor 5. Stance change. While wielding a Heavy Weapon two-handed, you gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions against melee attacks for the remainder of the encounter or until you switch stances. You cannot make weapon attacks faster than basic speed during this time. Requires Defensive Stance to use. Stacks with active Soft Heavy stances.

Hold the Line — Vigor 7. Until the end of your next turn, any enemy that moves through your weapon's reach takes full weapon damage automatically (no attack roll), once per turn per enemy. This damage cannot be defended against — the enemy chose to move through the threat. In Defensive Stance, add +1 Bonus Die to the damage.

Cunning Strike — Vigor 6. A feint with the haft followed by the strike with the head. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. The target's Defense reaction (if any) is at 1 Challenge. In Defensive Stance, the Defense Challenge increases to 2.

Spear-Wall — Vigor 10. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, all allies within 5 feet of you gain your weapon's current reach for the purposes of opportunity attacks and reactions against creatures moving within range. Requires Defensive Stance to use. The duration extends to two rounds when used in Defensive Stance.

Crook and Strike — Vigor 12. An attack designed to disarm. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. On a hit, the target makes Resistance (Strength) VS your attack roll or is disarmed; their weapon falls 5 feet in a direction of your choice. No weapon damage is dealt by this attack. In Defensive Stance, the Resistance is at 1 Challenge.

Bracing Counter — Vigor 14. Reaction. When targeted by a melee attack, you make a Defense reaction at +3 Bonus Dice. If the Defense succeeds, the attacker is pushed 10 feet directly away from you; if they collide with an obstacle, they fall prone. In Defensive Stance, the push increases to 15 feet.

Pillar Stance — Vigor 11. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, you cannot be Pushed, Pulled, or Knocked Prone. You may make Defense reactions against ranged attacks within your reach as well as melee attacks. Requires Defensive Stance to use. While in this technique's stance, the King's Wall Stance Guard bonus extends to all allies within 10 feet rather than only 5 feet.

King's Refusal — Vigor 12. Your reach now extends to all enemies adjacent to allies within 10 feet of you. You may make Defense reactions on behalf of those allies against melee attacks that originate within your extended reach. Lasts until the end of your next turn. In Defensive Stance, the duration extends to the end of the round.

Crushing Reach — Vigor 10. Make a basic weapon attack at full reach with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the target cannot move toward you on their next turn unless they spend 5 Vigor to do so. In Defensive Stance, the Vigor cost to recover the movement increases to 10.

Fortress of Steel — Vigor 22. Until the end of your next turn, no enemy may willingly enter your weapon's reach without first passing a Resolve check VS your highest weapon attack roll. Those who fail the Resolve check halt their movement at the edge of your reach and cannot attack you that turn. In Defensive Stance, the duration extends to the end of the round, and allies within 5 feet of you share the protection.

Spearmaster's Wrath — Vigor 20. Until the end of your next turn, your weapon's reach extends to three times its normal reach, and you may make Defense reactions automatically against any enemy attack within that reach — including ranged attacks aimed at you or at allies within your reach. In Defensive Stance, the extended reach also applies to your basic weapon attacks during this duration.

The Long Reach — Vigor 25. Make one basic weapon attack at four times your weapon's reach. The attack gains +3 Bonus Dice to damage and Threat 4. The target may use Defense reactions normally. In Defensive Stance, the attack also ignores cover.

King's Last Stand — Vigor 25. Stance change requiring you to be Wounded. While you are in this stance, your weapon's reach extends by 10 feet, you gain +3 Bonus Dice to weapon damage, and enemies within your reach must spend 1 additional Action to move at all (even within your reach). Lasts until you are no longer Wounded, are knocked unconscious, or choose to end it. Requires Defensive Stance to use. The damage bonus increases to +4 Bonus Dice when in Defensive Stance.

Wall and Push — Set Against Charge → Defensive Sweep → Tripping Sweep against any combination of enemies attempting to close on you within a single round. On completion (all three succeed), you gain a free Bonus Action that may be used on your next turn to perform any Soft Heavy Weapons technique requiring a single Action without paying its Vigor cost.

King's Line — Pillar Stance → Hold the Line → Spear-Wall in sequence within two consecutive rounds. On completion, all allies within 10 feet of you gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions and the benefits of the Pillar Stance until the end of the encounter.

The school of mass in motion. The largest weapons in the catalog become, in the hands of a Swift Heavy practitioner, instruments of circular grace. The greatsword does not chop — it whirls. The polearm does not thrust and recover — it spins and reverses. The maul does not fall — it dances around the wielder, gathering momentum, finding new openings with every rotation. Where Hard Heavy commits to the line of the strike and Soft Heavy controls the line, Swift Heavy bends the line into a circle. Every motion flows into the next. The weapon never quite stops.

Whirling Stance — Vigor 8 to enter. You keep the weapon in motion at all times, low slow circles that never settle. While in this stance, you may move 5 feet without spending an Action once per attack action, and you gain +1 Bonus Die to weapon damage on any attack made after moving 5 feet or more on the same turn. Ends when you fail to move at all on two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance for class features.

Spinning Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You move with the weapon, the weapon with you. At the start of your turn while in this stance, until the end of the turn, your first basic weapon attack gains Threat 2 and you may move 10 feet without spending an Action. Each additional 5 feet moved during your turn adds 1 Charge to the damage of your next attack. Ends if you fail to attack at all on two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance.

Tempest Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. You and the weapon are one moving body. While in this stance, you may make basic weapon attacks against up to three different targets within your reach as a single Action; each attack suffers -1 Bonus Die to damage. You may move 5 feet between each attack without creating Openings. Ends after two rounds, when you are knocked prone, or when you choose to end it. Considered the apex stance of the Swift Heavy school. Qualifies as Measured Stance.

Wide Cut — Vigor 2. A long sweeping arc. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a miss, you create an Opening on yourself. In Measured Stance, you do not create the Opening on a miss.

Half-Step Strike — Vigor 3. A short step into the strike, weapon flowing forward with the motion. Move up to 5 feet in any direction, then make a basic weapon attack. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage on the attack.

Whirling Sweep — Vigor 3. A sweeping cut against two adjacent enemies. Make one basic weapon attack against two enemies adjacent to each other within your reach, each defending separately at -1 Bonus Die to damage on your part. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty.

Circling Step — Vigor 2. You move around the enemy rather than through them. Move up to 10 feet around a single enemy (maintaining roughly equal distance to them). The enemy cannot create Openings from this movement, and you end the movement still within your weapon's reach of them. In Measured Stance, your next attack against that enemy gains +1 Bonus Die to attack roll.

Dancing Edge — Vigor 5. Make a basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll, then move 10 feet without creating Openings. If you end your movement in the flank or rear of an enemy, your next basic attack against that enemy gains +1 Bonus Die to damage. In Measured Stance, the flank/rear damage bonus increases to +2 Bonus Dice.

Spinning Cleave — Vigor 7. A full 360-degree rotation. Make one basic weapon attack against every enemy within your weapon's reach, each defending separately at -2 Bonus Dice to damage on your part. In Measured Stance, reduce the damage penalty to -1 Bonus Die.

Momentum Strike — Vigor 6. A swing built on motion. If you moved 10 feet or more before making this attack, make a basic weapon attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage. On a hit, the target is knocked back 5 feet. In Measured Stance, the knockback increases to 10 feet.

Twirling Block — Vigor 5. Reaction. When targeted by a melee attack, make a Defense reaction with +2 Bonus Dice. If the Defense succeeds, you may immediately move up to 5 feet without creating an Opening. In Measured Stance, also gain +1 Bonus Die on your next basic weapon attack before the end of your next turn.

Stepping Volley — Vigor 7. Make a basic weapon attack against one target, then move up to 10 feet without creating Openings, then make a basic weapon attack against a second target. Each attack suffers -1 Bonus Die to damage. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty on both attacks.

Flowing Reset — Vigor 6. You end your turn with a controlled recovery, the weapon coming to gentle stillness. At the start of your next turn, gain 5 Vigor and remove one Challenge from any pending action. In Measured Stance, the Vigor recovery increases to 8.

Storm's Heart — Vigor 12. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, every enemy within your weapon's reach who takes any Action (attack, move, technique, reaction) takes weapon damage with -2 Bonus Dice as you cycle through them. This damage cannot be defended against — they triggered it by acting. Requires Measured Stance to use. The duration extends to the end of the round when used in Measured Stance.

Maelstrom Cleave — Vigor 10. Move up to your full Speed in a path of your choosing. Make one basic weapon attack against every enemy you pass within your weapon's reach, each defending separately at -1 Bonus Die to damage on your part. Each enemy is targeted only once per Maelstrom Cleave. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty.

Whirlwind of Steel — Vigor 14. A 360-degree sweep at full power. Make one basic weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage against every enemy within your reach. On a hit, the target is Dazed until the end of their next turn. In Measured Stance, on a hit dealing Health damage, the target is also knocked prone.

Spinning Doom — Vigor 11. Stance change. Whirl the weapon continuously. Until the end of your next turn, you gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions, and your basic weapon attacks gain Threat 2. Requires Measured Stance to use. The Threat increases to 4 in Measured Stance.

Dance of the Great Blade — Vigor 12. Until the end of your next turn, on every successful hit you land with a Heavy Weapon, you may move 5 feet without spending an Action as part of the attack action. This movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, the movement increases to 10 feet per hit.

Crushing Spin — Vigor 10. Make two basic weapon attacks against different enemies within your reach in sequence; the second attack must follow a 90-degree turn or more. If the first attack reduces its target to 0 Health or knocks them prone, the second attack gains 1 Charge to its damage Bonus Dice. In Measured Stance, gain 1 Charge to the second attack regardless of the first attack's outcome.

Tempest's Fury — Vigor 22. Until the end of your next turn, you may combine movement and attack actions: as a single Action, you may move up to your full Speed and make one basic weapon attack at any point during the movement, with +1 Bonus Die to damage. You may do this up to your Speed Bonus Dice count of times per turn. In Measured Stance, the damage bonus increases to +2 Bonus Dice.

Wheel of Annihilation — Vigor 25. A perfect full rotation. Make one basic weapon attack against every enemy within your weapon's reach with +2 Bonus Dice to damage; each defends separately. As part of this action, you may move 10 feet at any point without creating Openings. In Measured Stance, also gain Threat 2 on each attack.

Storm Walker — Vigor 20. Move up to twice your full Speed in feet, in any direction or path. Every enemy you pass within your weapon's reach takes weapon damage with +1 Bonus Die as you spin past, and is Dazed until the end of their next turn. The movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, targets that take Health damage are also knocked prone.

Final Spin — Vigor 25. One perfect whirling sweep. Make one basic weapon attack against every enemy within your reach with +2 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, the damage Bonus Dice automatically burst. Targets that take Health damage are knocked prone. In Measured Stance, also push targets that take Health damage 5 feet directly away from you.

The Dance Forward — Dancing Edge → Momentum Strike → Stepping Volley performed in sequence within a single round, each technique following at least 5 feet of movement. On completion, gain 10 Vigor and your next basic weapon attack before the end of your next turn gains +1 Bonus Die to damage and Threat 2.

Storm's Path — Whirling Sweep → Spinning Cleave → Whirlwind of Steel performed in sequence within a single round. On completion, if at least one target was hit by all three techniques, that target takes an additional +2 Bonus Dice of weapon damage as a final delayed strike at the end of the round.

The school of lethal precision at distance. The Hard Ranged practitioner does not fire quickly. They fire once, and the shot ends the matter. The draw is heavy. The bow is held until the wrist trembles. The arrow is placed where armor seams or bone seams give. Where Swift Ranged practitioners loose many shafts and trust to volume, the Hard Ranged shooter loses one — and the one is sufficient. Killing range, killing draw, killing aim. The whole school is built around the moment between the held breath and the release.

Heavy Draw Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You hold the bow at full draw or the weapon fully cocked, every shot committed. While in this stance, you gain +2 Bonus Dice to weapon damage on ranged attacks, but you may make only one ranged attack per turn and suffer -1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions. Ends when you make more than one ranged attack in a turn, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Aggressive Stance for class features.

Killing Aim Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You take time to aim each shot precisely. While in this stance, the Aim action grants +2 Bonus Dice to your next ranged attack roll (instead of the usual +1), and your ranged attacks ignore the dice step penalty for shooting at Long range. You may move no more than 5 feet per turn while in this stance. Ends when you move more than 5 feet on a turn, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Aggressive Stance.

Headhunter's Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. Your shots target weak points by default. While in this stance, all your ranged attacks gain Threat 4 and ignore armor of equal or lower Quality Rank than your weapon. Multi-shot techniques suffer -1 Bonus Die to damage on each shot. Ends after three rounds, when you are knocked prone, or when you choose to end it. Considered the apex stance of the Hard Ranged school. Qualifies as Aggressive Stance.

Heavy Shot — Vigor 2. A fully drawn, fully committed release. Make a basic ranged weapon attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. In Aggressive Stance, if any damage Bonus Die bursts on the hit, the target loses 5 Guard until the end of your next turn.
Killing Shot — Vigor 3. Aimed at the throat, the eye-slit, or the heart. Make a basic ranged attack with Threat 2. In Aggressive Stance, the Threat increases to 4.

Hewing Arrow — Vigor 3. An arrow placed at the seam between plates. Make a basic ranged attack ignoring armor of one Quality Rank lower than your weapon. In Aggressive Stance, ignore armor of equal Quality Rank as well.

Bleeding Arrow — Vigor 3. A wide-bladed broadhead aimed to bleed rather than to kill at once. Make a basic ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit dealing Health damage, the target suffers Bleeding (1d4 per round) until they receive medical attention. In Aggressive Stance, the Bleeding damage increases to 1d6.

Pinning Shot — Vigor 7. A shot that pins the target's clothing, armor, or limb to a surface or to the ground. Make a basic ranged attack with -1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the target is Restrained until they spend an Action to pull free, succeeding a Strength check VS your attack roll. Requires a solid surface within 5 feet of the target. In Aggressive Stance, the Strength check is at 1 Challenge.

Armor-Piercer — Vigor 7. A heavy shot aimed at the heaviest armor seam you can see. Make a ranged attack ignoring armor of equal or lower Quality Rank. On a hit, also reduce the target's Armor Rank by 2 until their armor is repaired. In Aggressive Stance, the armor itself also takes 2d6 damage.

Crippling Shot — Vigor 5. A shot aimed at the leg, the foot, or the lower joint. Make a basic ranged attack. On a hit, the target's Speed Score is reduced by 5 until they receive medical attention or magical healing. In Aggressive Stance, the Speed reduction is 10 instead.

Heart-Shot — Vigor 7. A shot placed where the major vessels run. Make a ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. If the attack deals Health damage, the target suffers Bleeding (1d6 per round) until they receive medical attention. In Aggressive Stance, the Bleeding damage Bonus Die bursts on 9 or 10.

Power Volley — Vigor 8. Two heavy shots in succession against a single target. Make two ranged attacks; the second gains +1 Bonus Die to damage if the first hit. Requires either a quick-loading weapon or a held pre-nocked second arrow. In Aggressive Stance, if the first attack hit, the target cannot use Defense reactions against the second.

Bonebreak Arrow — Vigor 6. A heavy shot meant to maim rather than kill. Make a ranged attack. On a hit, the target suffers 1 Challenge on their next physical action (attack, technique, or movement-based check). In Aggressive Stance, the Challenge applies to all their actions until the end of their next turn.

Killing Range — Vigor 12. A shot whose lethality is amplified by distance. Make a ranged attack at Long range or beyond with +3 Bonus Dice to damage. The dice step penalty for shooting at Long range is removed for this attack. In Aggressive Stance, the damage bonus also applies at Medium range; the range itself becomes a weapon.

Bonebreaker Shot — Vigor 12. A shot aimed at the joint, the knee, the elbow. Make a ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to damage. If the attack deals Health damage, the target suffers a Broken Bone: lose 1d4 from one physical Ability Score (target's choice) until they receive surgical or magical healing. In Aggressive Stance, you choose which Ability Score is reduced.

Crushing Volley — Vigor 10. Three shots in rapid sequence at a single target, each fired with full intent. Make three ranged attacks against the same target. The first deals normal damage; the second adds 1 Charge to its damage Bonus Dice; the third adds 2 Charges. Requires a weapon capable of three shots within a turn (held arrows for a bow, magazine-loaded crossbow, etc.). In Aggressive Stance, the third shot also gains Threat 2.

Stunning Arrow — Vigor 14. A heavy shaft aimed at the side of the helm or the base of the skull. Make a ranged attack with +2 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, these dice automatically burst. The target is Stunned until the end of their next turn. In Aggressive Stance, the target is Stunned until the end of your next turn instead.

Master's Mark — Vigor 11. A signature shot that marks the target for amplified follow-up. Make a basic ranged attack. On a hit, mark the target; all your Hard Ranged Weapons techniques against the marked target until the end of the encounter gain +1 Bonus Die to damage. The mark ends when the target dies or escapes your awareness. In Aggressive Stance, the mark also grants Threat 2 on all your Hard Ranged Weapons attacks against the target.

Sunder Shot — Vigor 12. A heavy shot aimed at the target's equipment rather than their body. Make a ranged attack against an item the target holds or wears (weapon, shield, helm). The attack ignores the item's Armor Rank and deals weapon damage directly to its Durability. In Aggressive Stance, if the item breaks or shatters, the wielder also creates an Opening.

Worldpiercer Shot — Vigor 22. The shot you have practiced ten thousand times, for this moment. Make a ranged attack with +4 Bonus Dice to damage; on a hit, all damage Bonus Dice automatically burst. The attack ignores armor and cover entirely. In Aggressive Stance, the attack gains Threat equal to the number of distinct Hard Ranged Weapons techniques you have used since the start of the encounter, to a maximum of Threat 6.

Death March Volley — Vigor 25. Every shot this turn lands with deadly intent. Until the end of your turn, all your ranged attacks gain +3 Bonus Dice to damage and Threat 4. You may make as many basic ranged attacks this turn as your Actions allow. In Aggressive Stance, the Threat increases to 6 on each shot.

Sundering Doom Arrow — Vigor 20. A killing shot aimed at the target's equipment. Choose a single item carried by an enemy within range — weapon, shield, helm, or armor piece. Make a ranged attack targeting that item; on a hit, if the item is of lower Quality Rank than your weapon, it is destroyed. If of equal Quality Rank, it drops two Quality Ranks. If higher, it drops one. In Aggressive Stance, the item is destroyed if of equal or lower Quality Rank.

Final Shot — Vigor 25. The technique the Hard Ranged school is feared for. Make a ranged attack against a target at 0 Durability or below half Health. On a hit, the target must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your attack roll or be killed instantly. In Aggressive Stance, if the target is below 25% Health, no Resistance check is allowed.

The Hunter's Patience — Heavy Shot → Killing Shot → any Expert or Master Hard Ranged technique against the same target within one round. If all three shots hit, the final shot's damage is doubled when the target is prone, restrained, Stunned, or otherwise unable to defend.

The Killing Sequence — Master's Mark → Killing Range → Final Shot against the same marked target, with each technique used on a different turn within three rounds. On completion (the mark is placed, the Killing Range shot hits, and the Final Shot is taken), the Final Shot's Resistance check is at 2 Challenges and the damage gains an additional +2 Bonus Dice.

The school of denial and read. The Soft Ranged practitioner is rarely the one to shoot first. They wait. They watch. They identify the moment the opponent commits — the draw, the cast, the swing — and then they answer. Their shots disarm, suppress, dissuade. A shot may not kill but may make the enemy reconsider the next step. The Soft archer is the sentinel at the gate, the bodyguard who fires only when the assassin's hand moves, the watcher in the tower whose first arrow ends the battle before it began.

Sentinel Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You hold ready, watching all directions. While in this stance, you gain +1 Bonus Die to ranged attacks made as reactions, and you may make a ranged reaction attack (Reaction Shot or similar) once per round even outside your own turn. Ends when you fail to make a reaction shot for two consecutive rounds, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance for class features.

Reading Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You study the enemy as they fight. While in this stance, after observing a creature make a melee or ranged attack within 60 feet, you gain +1 Bonus Die on attack rolls and damage rolls against that creature with ranged weapons until the end of the encounter. You may track up to your Memory Bonus Dice number of read creatures simultaneously. Ends when you cease to observe creatures attacking, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Defensive Stance.

Master Watcher Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. You act last, deliberately. While in this stance, you may delay your Initiative by up to 10 in any combat round, choosing to act at any point during the round after that delay. All your ranged attacks this round against creatures who acted before you gain +2 Bonus Dice on attack rolls and the Aim action is treated as already taken for your first shot. Ends at the end of the round; must be re-entered each round at the listed Vigor cost. Considered the apex stance of the Soft Ranged school. Qualifies as Defensive Stance.

Cover Shot — Vigor 2. A shot fired from behind cover without breaking that cover. Make a ranged attack from behind partial or full cover. The cover bonus to your Guard remains in effect against any counterattack made before the end of your next turn. In Defensive Stance, also gain +1 Bonus Die on the attack.

Reaction Shot — Vigor 3. Reaction. When an enemy moves into your weapon's effective range during their turn, make a basic ranged attack against them as they enter. In Defensive Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage on the Reaction Shot.

Warning Shot — Vigor 2. A shot deliberately fired close but not at the target. The target makes a Resolve check VS your attack roll or loses one Action on their next turn as they react to the threat. No damage is dealt by this technique. In Defensive Stance, on a failed Resolve, the target also creates an Opening for one round.

Disarming Shot — Vigor 3. A shot aimed at the target's weapon hand or held weapon. Make a ranged attack at -1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the target makes Resistance (Strength) VS your attack roll or is disarmed; their weapon falls 5 feet from them. In Defensive Stance, the Resistance check is at 1 Challenge.

Counter-Volley — Vigor 6. Reaction. When an enemy makes a ranged attack against an ally within your sight, you may make a ranged attack against that enemy. If you hit, your shot deals weapon damage and the enemy's attack against your ally suffers 1 Challenge. In Defensive Stance, the ally also gains +1 Bonus Die on their Defense reaction against the enemy's shot.

Reading Mark — Vigor 5. You mark a creature you have observed in combat. The mark lasts until the end of the encounter. Against the marked creature, your ranged attacks gain +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. You may track only one Reading Mark at a time. In Defensive Stance, also gain +1 Bonus Die to damage against the marked target.

Hampering Shot — Vigor 7. A shot aimed to disrupt rather than kill. Make a ranged attack with -1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the target suffers -2 Bonus Dice on their next attack roll as the shot disrupts their stance or grip. In Defensive Stance, the penalty also applies to their first Defense reaction before the end of their next turn.

Concealment Shot — Vigor 8. A shot fired from hiding that does not reveal your position. Make a ranged attack while Hidden. The attack does not break your Hidden status, even if it hits. Each subsequent Concealment Shot within the same minute makes a contested Awareness check against the target's passive Awareness; on failure, you remain Hidden. In Defensive Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die on the Awareness contests.

Shielding Shot — Vigor 7. A shot aimed not at the enemy but at the space they would enter. Make a ranged attack against an empty space within 5 feet of one of your allies. The next enemy that would enter that space or attack the protected ally suffers 1 Challenge on their attack roll. The shielding effect lasts until the end of your next turn or until triggered. In Defensive Stance, the Challenge increases to 2.

Counter-Cast — Vigor 6. Reaction. When an enemy within range begins casting a spell, you may make a ranged attack against them. If the attack hits and deals any damage, the caster must succeed a Resistance check (whichever is appropriate to their casting school) VS your attack roll or lose the spell and the Mana cost. In Defensive Stance, the Resistance check is at 1 Challenge.

Perfect Read — Vigor 10. Reaction. After observing an enemy complete an attack or technique, you may immediately make a ranged attack against them with +3 Bonus Dice to the attack roll. This Reaction Shot may be made even if you have already taken your reaction this round. In Defensive Stance, also gain +1 Bonus Die to damage.

Interrupt Shot — Vigor 14. Reaction. When an enemy declares a Technique that requires multiple Actions to complete, you may make a ranged attack against them at +1 Bonus Die to damage. If the attack hits and deals damage, the enemy's Technique fails but the Vigor is still spent. In Defensive Stance, on Expert or Master rank Techniques, gain +1 additional Bonus Die to damage.

Mirror Shot — Vigor 12. Reaction. When you would be targeted by a hostile ranged attack, you may attempt to deflect or redirect it. Make a contested ranged attack roll against the original attacker's roll. On success, the attack is redirected to the original attacker as if it were yours. On failure, the attack proceeds normally against you. In Defensive Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die on the contested roll.

Master's Watch — Vigor 10. Stance change. Until the start of your next turn, you may hold your action ready to fire. When any specific trigger you have named (an enemy enters a particular doorway, casts a particular kind of spell, attacks a particular ally, etc.) occurs, you may immediately make a ranged attack as a reaction with +2 Bonus Dice on the attack roll. Requires Defensive Stance to use. The triggered shot also gains +1 Bonus Die to damage in Defensive Stance.

Reading Volley — Vigor 11. Make two ranged attacks against creatures you have observed attack in combat this encounter. Each attack gains +1 Bonus Die from prior knowledge of their movement patterns. Each may target a different creature. In Defensive Stance, gain Threat 2 on each shot.

Crippling Counter — Vigor 12. Reaction. When an enemy targets you with a melee attack and misses, you may make a ranged attack against them at -1 Bonus Die to damage. On a hit, the attacker's Speed Score is reduced by 5 until rest, and they suffer 1 Challenge on their next attack roll. In Defensive Stance, the Speed reduction is 10 instead.

Untouchable Range — Vigor 22. Until the end of your next turn, you cannot be targeted by ranged attacks — your reading of the field is such that any attempted shot finds your position uncertain or your stance unreadable. Melee attacks proceed normally. You may continue to make ranged attacks during this duration. In Defensive Stance, the protection extends to one ally within 10 feet of you.

Watching Storm — Vigor 25. Until the end of your next turn, every ally within 60 feet who can see you gains +1 Bonus Die to their next ranged attack roll, as your read of the battlefield translates into shared awareness. Each ally may benefit from this only once. In Defensive Stance, the bonus also includes +1 Bonus Die to damage on the ally's shot.

Final Witness — Vigor 20. You hold your shot indefinitely until the perfect moment arrives. As an Action, declare Final Witness; on any turn during the next minute, you may make a ranged attack with +3 Bonus Dice to damage as a free action, no further Vigor cost. The Final Witness ends when you make the shot, fall unconscious, or after one minute passes. In Defensive Stance, the held shot also gains Threat 4.

Sentinel's Verdict — Vigor 25. Make a ranged attack against a Reading Marked target. On a hit, the attack ignores all cover and armor entirely and deals weapon damage with +3 Bonus Dice; if the damage Bonus Dice burst, the target must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your attack roll or be killed instantly. In Defensive Stance, no Resistance check is allowed if the target is below 50% Health.

The Sentinel's Read — Reading Mark → Reading Volley → Perfect Read against the same marked target across consecutive rounds. On completion, the Perfect Read shot's damage is doubled if the target is marked, and the target is also Dazed until the end of their next turn.

The Watcher's Net — Three reaction-based techniques (Reaction Shot, Counter-Volley, Counter-Cast, Mirror Shot, Crippling Counter, or Perfect Read) performed in a single round. On completion, you may take one additional Action on your next turn, representing the foresight you have demonstrated.

The school of motion and volume. Where Hard Ranged practitioners loose one shaft and end the matter, Swift Ranged practitioners loose many and let the count of arrows do the work. The bow stays in the hand, but the bow is always in motion — drawn, released, drawn again. The shooter moves between shots. The shooter shoots between movements. They flank. They strike from cover and disappear. They volley across the gap of a corridor and vanish through the next door. The Swift Ranged school is the school of the skirmisher, the horse-archer, the harasser, the raider — and at its height, the school of the storm of arrows that no commitment can answer.

Skirmisher's Stance — Vigor 6 to enter. You stay light on your feet, weapon ready for quick release. While in this stance, you may move 5 feet without spending an Action once per turn, and this movement does not create Openings even if you are within enemy reach. You also gain +1 Bonus Die to Defense reactions while wielding a ranged weapon. Ends when you fail to use the free movement for two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance for class features.

Hunter's Stance — Vigor 10 to enter. You shoot moving targets as easily as still ones. While in this stance, your ranged attacks gain Threat 2 against any target that has moved 5 feet or more on its last turn. If you yourself moved 10 feet or more before making the attack, increase to Threat 4. Ends when you fail to move at all on two consecutive turns, are knocked prone, or choose to end it. This stance qualifies as Measured Stance.

Storm of Arrows Stance — Vigor 15 to enter. Your hand and bow are in continuous motion. While in this stance, you may make up to three basic ranged attacks per turn as a single Action; each attack costs 5 Vigor and suffers -1 Bonus Die to damage. You may move 5 feet between each shot without creating Openings. Ends after one round; must be re-entered each round at the listed Vigor cost. Considered the apex stance of the Swift Ranged school. Qualifies as Measured Stance.

Quick Shot — Vigor 2. A fast release with no time to settle. Make a basic ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll (not damage). You receive 1 Challenge on Defense reactions until the end of your next turn. In Measured Stance, the Challenge is removed.
Move and Shoot — Vigor 3. You loose while still in motion. Move up to 10 feet, then make a basic ranged attack. The movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, move up to 15 feet instead.

Twin Shot — Vigor 3. Two arrows nocked together, or two rapid releases with a single-shot weapon. Make two basic ranged attacks against the same target at -1 Bonus Die to damage each. In Measured Stance, the second shot does not suffer the damage penalty.

Slip Shot — Vigor 2. Shoot and immediately reposition. Make a basic ranged attack. Immediately after the attack resolves, move up to 5 feet without creating Openings. In Measured Stance, move up to 10 feet instead.

Eagle Eye Shot — Vigor 5. A swift but precise release. Make a basic ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll. Immediately after the attack resolves, move up to 10 feet without creating Openings. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage as well.

Flicker Volley — Vigor 6. Three quick releases against any combination of targets within range. Make three basic ranged attacks at -2 Bonus Dice to damage each. Each attack may target a different creature; each defends separately. In Measured Stance, reduce the damage penalty to -1 Bonus Die.

Lunge Shot — Vigor 7. A step forward gives you a clearer angle. Move up to 5 feet, then make a ranged attack with +1 Bonus Die to the attack roll, then move back to your starting square as a free action. This movement does not create Openings. In Measured Stance, also gain +1 Bonus Die to damage on the shot.

Whirlshot — Vigor 8. Shoot, move, shoot again. Make a basic ranged attack against one target. Move up to 5 feet. Make a basic ranged attack against a different target. Each attack suffers -1 Bonus Die to damage. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty on both attacks.

Tempo Counter — Vigor 5. Reaction. When an enemy makes a melee or ranged attack against you, you may immediately make a basic ranged attack against them. This reaction may be used even if you have already taken a reaction this round. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die to damage on the counter shot.

Phantom Shot — Vigor 6. A shot loosed from concealment, with the shooter melting away after. Make a ranged attack from Hidden. Immediately after the attack resolves, make a Stealth check VS the highest Awareness of any enemy that can see your position. On success, you remain Hidden. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die on the Stealth check.

Killing Tempo Volley — Vigor 12. Three shots at a single target, each gaining lethality from the last. Make three ranged attacks against the same target. The first deals normal damage at -1 Bonus Die; the second deals normal damage; the third gains +1 Charge to its damage Bonus Dice. Requires a weapon capable of three shots within a turn. In Measured Stance, the first shot also deals normal damage with no penalty.

Edged Wind Volley — Vigor 10. Move and shoot through the formation. Move up to your full Speed in a path. Make one basic ranged attack with -1 Bonus Die to damage against up to three targets you pass within your weapon's range. Each defends separately. In Measured Stance, remove the damage penalty on each shot.

Storm of Shafts — Vigor 14. A scattering volley across multiple foes. Make up to five basic ranged attacks split among any number of targets within range. Each attack suffers -3 Bonus Dice to damage. Requires a weapon capable of multiple shots per turn (held arrows, magazine-loaded crossbow, thrown daggers, etc.). In Measured Stance, reduce the damage penalty to -2 Bonus Dice.

Cutting Wind Bow — Vigor 11. Stance change. Until the end of your next turn, your ranged attacks gain Threat 4 and you may move 5 feet without creating Openings with each attack you make. Requires Measured Stance to use. The duration extends to the end of the round when used in Measured Stance.

Vanishing Salvo — Vigor 12. A burst of shots followed by disappearance. Make two basic ranged attacks at -1 Bonus Die to damage each, then immediately attempt to Hide. Make a Stealth check VS the highest Awareness of any enemy that can see you; on success, you are Hidden until your next attack. In Measured Stance, gain +1 Bonus Die on the Stealth check and the damage penalty is removed.

Tempo Lock Shot — Vigor 10. A shot that disrupts the target's rhythm. Make a basic ranged attack. On a hit, the target loses one Action on their next turn; their action economy is disrupted by the precision and timing of your strike. In Measured Stance, if the hit's damage Bonus Dice burst, the target also loses their reaction for one round.

Hundred Arrows — Vigor 22. A release of shots so rapid the eye loses count. Make as many basic ranged attacks as your Speed Bonus Dice number against any targets within range. You may move up to your full Speed as part of this Action. Each attack suffers -3 Bonus Dice to damage. Whenever any damage Bonus Die bursts during this technique, recover 5 Vigor. In Measured Stance, the damage penalty is reduced to -2 Bonus Dice and the Vigor recovery increases to 8 per burst.

Untouchable Volley — Vigor 20. Until the end of your next turn, you are immune to Opportunity attacks and Openings as you move. Your basic ranged attacks during this duration gain +1 Bonus Die to attack roll, and you may move 10 feet without spending an Action once per turn. In Measured Stance, the duration extends to the end of the round.
