The cloak ends if your Concentration is broken, if you cast another spell requiring Concentration, or if you choose to end it.
Elemental Ram

blocks.text: Mana: 16 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 turn Resistance: Speed for half damage

blocks.html: You unleash a whirling barrage of elemental energy that crashes into a target within Close range. The target takes double base weapon damage from the arm. The strike is hard to avoid; on a successful Resistance (Speed) check VS your casting roll, the target still takes half damage rather than negating the attack entirely.
Elemental Shield

blocks.text: Mana: 15 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: 3 turns Resistance: Special

blocks.html: You conjure a shield of your arm's element which orbits you in a slow protective arc. While the shield is active, you may block an attack against you with your choice of Defense reaction or an Arcane Arms casting roll (whichever Bonus Dice are higher). On a successful block, the shield explodes; if the attack was a melee attack, the attacker must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your casting roll or take your weapon damage. After the explosion, the shield is gone for the remainder of its duration.
Elemental Lash

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium Duration: Instant Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: You crack a whip of elemental energy from the tip of your arm. Make an Arcane Arms attack against a target in Medium range. On a hit, the target takes weapon damage and you choose one of two effects: pull the target up to 10 feet directly toward you, or restrain them for one turn (the lash holds them in place; they must spend an Action and succeed a Strength check VS your casting roll to break free). The lash is a single conjured thread — it does not persist beyond the moment of casting unless the Restrain effect is chosen.

Element Variants. Fire: targets restrained suffer Burning (1) for the duration of the restraint. Lightning: targets pulled suffer 1 Challenge on their next Defense reaction. Ice: targets restrained are also Slowed for one turn. Wind: the pull or push distance is 15 feet rather than 10. Earth: the lash also rips a chunk of stone from the ground, leaving Difficult Terrain at the target's square. Aether: the lash may pass through cover and around corners up to one bend.

These are the spells of the practiced arcanist — the magic that ends mass engagements, cuts through fortifications, redirects the magic of others. An Expert Arcane Arms wielder is no longer a duelist with a wand. They are an artillery piece, a counter-caster, a strategic asset. The Mana costs are higher. The casting times can be long enough that a wise wielder considers their positioning before declaring. The effects, when they land, change the shape of the fight.
Arcane Cannon

blocks.text: Mana: 18 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 turn Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You concentrate all your elemental energy into a tightly compressed beam of magic capable of cutting through the toughest foes. The beam is 25 feet long, plus 10 feet for each Mana Circle you possess, and approximately 6 inches in diameter. Everything in the path of the beam takes weapon damage plus your Arcane Arms Bonus Dice on a failed Defense (Dodge) reaction. The beam ignores Armor Rank entirely and damages armor and the wearer in equal measure. Shields, walls, doors, and other cover in the beam's path also take full damage.
Arcane Fracture

blocks.text: Mana: 15 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium Duration: 1 turn Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You release a small but fast-moving bullet of elemental force at a target in Medium range. The bullet streaks forward and explodes on impact. A creature struck by this bursting force takes weapon damage with +1 Bonus Die, and must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your casting roll or be Dazed until the end of your next turn.
Arcane Nexus

blocks.text: Mana: 20 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You conjure a nexus of elemental energy 20 feet across at any point you can see within Close range. Creatures in the area must succeed a Resistance (Speed) check VS your casting roll or take weapon damage and be drawn into the center of the nexus, ending the movement adjacent to the center point. Creatures who succeed take half damage and do not move.
Elemental Barrage

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: 1 turn Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You release a pulse of elemental force that crackles and bursts within an area you can see. Choose an area 10 feet across within Medium range; all creatures within that area must succeed a Resistance (Speed) check VS your casting roll or take weapon damage. Any creature that takes damage from this spell is also Dazzled until the end of your next turn (1 Challenge on attack rolls and Awareness checks involving sight).
Elemental Blessing

blocks.text: Mana: 18 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You infuse a number of weapons within Close range with elemental energy. You may bless up to 1 + your Soul Bonus Dice number of weapons. While the spell is maintained, the blessed weapons deal an additional 2d8 damage of your arm's element on each hit. The spell ends if Concentration is broken, if a blessed weapon leaves the range, or if you choose to end it. The blessing on weapons that leave range simply fades; the spell continues for those that remain.
Elemental Nova

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Far Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You release a bolt of hyper-compressed elemental energy at a target within Far range. On a hit, the bolt detonates on impact, dealing weapon damage with +5 Bonus Dice to the target and half as much damage (rounded down, no Bonus Dice) to all other creatures within 12 feet of the impact point. On a miss, the bolt continues past the target and detonates at the spell's maximum range, damaging any creatures or objects within 12 feet of that point.
Element Counter

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Medium Duration: Instant Resistance: Special

blocks.html: Reaction. When a creature within Medium range casts a spell whose effect would target you or an area you occupy, you may attempt to counter it by channeling an opposing or matching pulse through your arm. Make an Arcane Arms casting roll VS the original caster's casting roll. On success, the spell fails and the Mana is still spent by the original caster. On failure, the spell proceeds normally. You may attempt this counter once per round; the spell's Mana is consumed whether or not the counter succeeds.

Element Variants. If your arm's element opposes the incoming spell's element (Fire vs. Ice, Lightning vs. Earth, Wind vs. Aether — the GM determines opposition for unusual element pairings), gain +2 Bonus Dice on the contested casting roll. If your arm's element matches the incoming spell's element, gain +1 Bonus Die instead — your familiarity with the element's behavior offers a different kind of advantage. Neutral element pairings receive no bonus.

Master Spells are the working magic of the ascended. A character who can cast a Master Arcane Arms spell in combat is among the most powerful arcanists in their region, and the spells reflect it. Mana costs are heavy, casting times are long enough to require planning or protection, and the effects are reality-altering by the standards of common magic. A Cataclysm Aura erupts entire formations of enemies. An Elemental Ascendance turns a single round of combat into four. A Worldshaper's Maelstrom changes the terrain itself. These are not spells for routine fights. These are spells for the fights that decide whether the world continues as it was.
Cataclysm Aura

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Self (20-foot radius) Duration: Special Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You radiate an aura of your arm's element 20 feet around you in all directions, lasting until the start of your next turn. Any creature in the aura other than you must succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your casting roll or take weapon damage. On your turn, you may sustain the aura with 2 Actions and 15 Mana. Each sustain increases the aura's radius by 5 feet and adds 1 Charge to your damage Bonus Dice. These increases are cumulative across sustains. After being sustained for 4 turns, the aura erupts at the start of your next turn — every creature within 60 feet of you takes the full sustained damage with no Resistance, and the aura dissipates.
Elemental Ascendance

blocks.text: Mana: 60 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self Duration: Special, up to 4 turns Resistance: None

blocks.html: You ascend into a higher state of elemental power. While ascended, you levitate 3 feet from the ground and your casting times for Arcane Arms spells are reduced to one-quarter (rounded up to the nearest whole Action). If a spell's reduced casting time is less than 1 Action, you instead cast that spell multiple times with a single Action equal to its quarter-fraction (a 1-Action spell may be cast four times in 1 Action). Only one spell may be cast per Action; you cannot mix fractional casting times. You also gain Elemental Absorption: when you would take damage of your arm's element, you instead heal half that amount. You do not need to Concentrate to maintain Ascendance, but at the start of each of your turns while ascended you consume 20 Vigor. The spell ends after 4 turns, when you cannot pay the Vigor cost at the start of your turn, or when you choose to end it.
Elemental Fountain

blocks.text: Mana: 35 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: Your arm's element sprays into the air and cascades down upon your enemies. Choose up to six different targets within Close range; no two chosen targets may be adjacent to each other. Each target must succeed a Defense (Dodge) reaction VS your casting roll or take weapon damage with +2 Bonus Dice. Targets who succeed take half damage.
Elemental Singularity

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Instant (delayed) Resistance: Special

blocks.html: You create a singularity point of intense elemental energy in Medium range. All enemies within a 30-foot radius are drawn toward the center, moving to and ending adjacent to the singularity point. After 1 turn, the singularity explodes: all creatures within the original 30-foot radius (regardless of their current position) take weapon damage with +5 Bonus Dice. Creatures who survive the explosion are Blinded and Deafened for 3 turns. Creatures who succeed a Resistance (Speed) check VS your casting roll during the initial draw resist the pull but still take damage from the explosion if they remain within radius.
Elemental Squall

blocks.text: Mana: 40 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Concentration, up to 4 turns Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A roiling storm of your element fills the air in a designated area. Choose an area 20 feet across and 20 feet high within Medium range. Every creature in the area takes weapon damage when the spell is cast, and again at the start of each of their turns while they remain within the area. Creatures who take this damage must also succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your casting roll or be Blinded and Deafened for as long as they remain in the area. The squall blocks vision; ranged attacks made into, out of, or through the area suffer 2 Challenges unless they are composed of the same element as the squall.
Worldshaper's Maelstrom

blocks.text: Mana: 42 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration up to 3 turns Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You summon a cataclysmic maelstrom of elemental energy that engulfs all enemies within Close range. While maintained, the maelstrom deals weapon damage plus 4d12 damage of your arm's element to every enemy within Close range at the start of each of your turns. Creatures who succeed a Resistance (Stamina) check VS your casting roll take half damage. The terrain within Close range is permanently reshaped to reflect the caster's element — ice for Cold, scorched and cracked earth for Fire, glassed and statically charged ground for Lightning, and so on. The reshaped area becomes Challenging Terrain for the remainder of the encounter and beyond, until naturally restored or magically cleansed.
Element Form

blocks.text: Mana: 50 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Self Duration: 3 turns Resistance: None

blocks.html: You become your arm's element. For three turns, your body is composed of and animated by your element rather than flesh. You are immune to damage of your arm's element type. You take half damage from physical attacks (Striking, Slashing, Piercing). You may move through the element's natural medium freely: a Fire-form moves through flame and smoke without restriction, an Ice-form skates over water and through frozen surfaces, a Wind-form moves through air as if it were ground, an Earth-form passes through stone at half Speed, a Lightning-form moves along conductive surfaces at double Speed, an Aether-form passes through walls less than 5 feet thick at half Speed. Your basic Arcane Arms attacks while in Element Form deal +1 Bonus Die to damage and ignore the first Defense reaction made against them each round. The form ends after 3 turns; you may not extend it without an entirely new casting.

Element Variants. Each element imposes one minor restriction during the form: Fire — you cannot enter water deeper than 1 foot. Lightning — you ground out and end the form if you touch unbroken stone or sand. Ice — direct fire or heat sources within 5 feet end the form. Earth — you cannot fly, levitate, or be teleported. Wind — you cannot enter sealed containers larger than your body. Aether — you cannot interact with mundane objects more solid than parchment.

Incantare is the broad, generalist spell tradition of the trained Mage. It is the magic of spoken formulae and runic hand-signs, of compressed forces and concept-binding — a system of working built around the precise application of the world's fundamental forces through the caster's Mana. Where Arcane Arms channels the basic elemental forces (Fire, Lightning, Ice, and the like) through an elemental armament, Incantare reaches for the forces and properties that Arcane Arms cannot easily address: gravity, sound, force, light, time, soul, and the threading-together of multiple forces in a single working.

This division is by design. A Mage who wants to throw fire at an enemy uses an Arcane Arm; a Mage who wants to crush an enemy under their own weight, deafen them with a focused shout, drain their Mana, eject their soul from their body, or shape a stone wall to suit their will reaches for Incantare. The two catalogs are companions, not competitors. A working Mage typically carries both an Arcane Arm and a substantial Incantare repertoire, and chooses between them based on what the moment requires.

Incantare is cast through Occult skill check, with Soul Bonus Dice added to the casting roll. Casting a spell consumes Mana and Actions equal to the spell's Casting Time. The Mage's Novice Spellcraft talent reduces Casting Time by 1 Action (minimum 1); the Lesser Ritual mechanic extends it. Most Incantare spells take 1 to 3 Actions to cast, with rituals running longer.

From the Novice rank upward, most Incantare spells scale meaningfully with the caster's Mana Circle Bonus Dice. Scaling is the defining feature of an Incantare caster's progression: a Mage with three Mana Circles casts the same spell as a Mage with seven Circles, but the results are qualitatively different. Damage scales, duration scales, area scales, the number of affected targets scales, sometimes the very capabilities of the spell expand at higher Circle counts. Scaling notes appear in italics at the end of each spell's entry where applicable.

Resistance checks against Incantare spells use the caster's casting roll as the VS. Where a spell's Resistance is listed as None, the spell takes effect without contest. Dodge reactions apply to spells that present a directed projectile or area; Stamina, Speed, Resolve, and Soul Resistances apply per the listed type.
Glasswork Sight

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune to the residual light captured in reflective surfaces — mirrors, polished metal, still water, glass panes — within your line of sight. For the duration, you see what each surface within Close range currently reflects, regardless of whether you have direct line of sight to the reflecting surface yourself. A mirror around the corner shows you what it shows; a windowpane across the courtyard reveals its room.

The working does not let you see through the surface — you only see what the surface naturally reflects. It is the spell that catches the assassin in the silver tray, that reveals who is around the next bend, that turns every polished cup in a great hall into a small additional eye. Glasswork Sight is a favored working of investigators, thieves, and bodyguards.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the perceptible range per Mana Circle.
Marked Stone

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 minute (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 year Resistance: None

blocks.html: You bind a small magical signature to a stone, brick, doorpost, tree, or other fixed feature. The mark is invisible, has no detectable physical or magical signature to anyone but you, and persists for one year. You always know the precise direction and distance to any Marked Stone you have cast, regardless of barriers or distance, as long as the mark remains intact.

Marked Stone is the trail-maker's working — the spell that lets a Mage map a labyrinth, mark a meeting place, or remember the way home from a deep forest. The mark cannot be discovered by mundane search and is detectable only by deliberate magical investigation aimed at its specific location.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 Marked Stone to the maximum number you may track simultaneously per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4 marks active; Circle 7: 8 marks).
Coil of Silence

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You weave a small constricting coil around a target's throat. The coil is invisible. The target makes Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, no sound the target produces from their voice or lungs travels more than a foot from their body. They hear themselves speaking normally. No one else hears anything.

Coil of Silence is more invasive than Quiet Countenance and more focused than Obscuring Fog. It silences one person, completely, while leaving them entirely visible and apparently free to speak. The disorientation it produces is significant: the target gestures, mouths words, sees others fail to react, and slowly realizes their voice has been taken. By the time they understand the working, the moment requiring their voice has usually passed.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 seconds to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Compass of Care

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune to a creature with whom you share a meaningful connection — a familiar, a mentor, a beloved companion, a sworn ally — and feel their direction and approximate distance. The creature must be within 10 miles of you and known to you for at least a month before the working can be used to find them.

Compass of Care is not Seeking. It does not require a focus, cannot be used against strangers or recent acquaintances, and provides no help in locating an enemy. It is the working that lets the parent find the missing child, the apprentice find the lost master, the comrade find the absent friend. The bond is the working's currency, and the working is only as strong as the bond it draws on.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 miles to the maximum tracking range per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 40 miles; Circle 7: 80 miles).
Curtain Step

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You become briefly intangible to one specific barrier — a curtain, a tapestry, a paper screen, a thin door, a sheet of fabric — and step through it. The barrier must be less than 1 inch thick, must not be metal, stone, or other hard material, and must be a barrier rather than a wall (a tapestry on a wall does not let you pass through the wall behind it). The step is brief: you are intangible only for the duration of your passage. Carried items pass through with you.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, also passable through wood barriers less than 2 inches thick. At Mana Circle 7+, also passable through stone or metal barriers less than 1 inch thick.
Whisper of the Watching

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune yourself to the subtle pressure of attention. For the duration, you sense when a creature within 60 feet is actively observing you — watching, listening for you, attending to your presence with focus rather than casual awareness. You do not learn who is watching, only that someone is, and roughly from what direction.

The working is the bodyguard's working, the spy's working, the wanted person's working. It does not protect against attack, does not reveal hidden enemies' locations precisely, and does not distinguish between hostile attention and friendly. But it tells you, reliably, that someone is paying attention to you — and in many lives, that knowledge is the difference between survival and discovery.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 feet to the detection range per Mana Circle.
Lantern Tongue

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: Words you speak aloud during the duration appear as glowing script in the air immediately above your shoulder, visible to anyone who can see you, persisting for 30 seconds before fading. The script is in whatever language you spoke; if your words were heard in any language, the writing shows that language faithfully.

Lantern Tongue is the silent classroom's working, the deaf scholar's working, and the working of any conversation that must be witnessed from a distance without being overheard. The script can be read by anyone who can read the relevant language; the working does not provide translation. It is also a discreet form of evidence — the words said by a Mage with Lantern Tongue active are documented, briefly, by the working itself.
Lesser Light

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch or Self Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You bind a small mote of compressed luminous force to an object you touch or a point floating beside you. The mote casts clear light to 20 feet and dim light to 40 feet, may be moved with a thought to any location within 20 feet of you, and is cool to the touch. It is the apprentice's first practical working — the simplest possible binding of light to substance, and the spell that lets a Mage read in the dark, signal across a courtyard, or pin a target's silhouette against a wall.

Lesser Light is not bright enough to harm undead or sensitive creatures, but it is unwavering — it does not flicker, does not consume fuel, and does not require attention. It is the spell every Mage casts first and last each day.
Whisps

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration, up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You fill an area equal to Close range with tiny motes of light that blink in and out at random, like fireflies in summer dusk. In total darkness the area is dimly lit; in already-dim conditions, the area becomes normally lit for the duration. The motes cannot be directed, concentrated, or focused on individual targets — they fill the space with ambient radiance and nothing more. They are also harder to ward against than directed light, as no single mote is the source. Mages on stealth excursions find Whisps useful precisely because the light has no clear origin to extinguish.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius per Mana Circle.
Reveal Presence

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You reveal the presence of magic and eldritch creatures within Close range. For the duration, magical items glow softly with a golden shimmer visible to all, runic artifacts outline their inscriptions, magical wards display the edges of their boundaries, and eldritch creatures (not arcane beasts) burn with a sickly foxfire-light that clings to their forms. The light is bright enough to spot at a glance — even by allies who cannot see magic naturally.

Reveal Presence imposes 2 Challenges on Stealth checks made by eldritch creatures and by creatures carrying enchanted gear within range. It is the Mage's first line of detection — the spell that answers the question 'is there magic here?' and makes the answer visible to the entire party.
Residual Traces

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune your perception to the field's memory — the residual impressions that significant magical workings leave in the ambient Mana for hours after the casting. For the duration, you can see fading colored traces of magic cast within Close range during the last 24 hours. Different forces leave different signatures: Fire traces glow red, Cold traces blue, Aether traces silver-blue, Force traces gold, Soul traces deep violet, Entropic traces black, Light traces white.

You can estimate the rank of the spell that left each trace (Basic, Novice, Expert, Master) and the direction the caster was facing. Older traces are dimmer; traces less than an hour old are vivid and sharp. Residual Traces is forensic magic — the spell that answers the question 'what happened here?'
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 12 hours to the trace lookback per Mana Circle (Circle 3 makes traces visible for 60 hours; Circle 7 for 5 days).
Magistrate's Inquiry

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You touch a sapient creature and attune yourself to the cognitive dissonance generated by deliberate falsehood. The spell does not read thoughts. It reads the field's response to lying: when a creature speaks an intentional untruth, the resulting dissonance creates a detectable ripple, and Magistrate's Inquiry makes that ripple visible to you.

For the duration, you know whether the target's next statement is true, false, or deliberately misleading. You do not learn the truth — only whether the target believes what they are saying. A creature genuinely mistaken registers as truthful. A creature speaking a technical truth designed to deceive registers as misleading. The target becomes aware they are being examined if they succeed Resistance (Resolve), but the spell continues regardless.
Seeking

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Far Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: This is the simplest locator working in the catalog. You bind a focus object to a target — a piece of hair or blood from a person, a stone from a building, a shard of an object — and the focus pulls you toward what it once was a part of. The connection must be direct and undeniable. A painting of a person is not a focus; a strand of their hair is.

While the spell is active, the focus tugs at your hand like a lodestone drawn to iron. The pull is a straight line and ignores all barriers and obstacles, meaning you could be drawn into a wall, over a cliff, or through a closed trap. Seeking tells you where. It does not tell you how to get there safely.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 mile to the maximum tracking distance per Mana Circle (the spell's effective range extends regardless of the listed Far).
Resonant Tongue

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune your understanding to the resonant patterns underlying sapient speech. For the duration, you understand — but cannot speak — any spoken language you hear. You perceive meaning through cadence, tone, and the resonance of the speaker's intent.

Written text is not affected. Coded speech, deliberately deceptive phrasing, and magical languages cannot be decoded without separate training or higher magic. Resonant Tongue is the Mage's passport in any place where the local tongue is not one they have studied — useful enough that few practicing Mages omit it from their repertoire.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per Mana Circle.
Spectral Force

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close (30 feet) Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: A spectral whisp of pale force appears within Close range and obeys your thought as a free action on your turn. It can lift, manipulate, or carry objects up to 10 pounds. It cannot attack, activate magical items, or perform skilled tasks (it cannot pick locks, disarm traps, or write legibly). It vanishes if it moves more than 30 feet from you. It is the most-used spell of any Mage's first year, the working that retrieves the dropped quill, opens the heavy door from the wrong side, and slips the small object from the high shelf without climbing.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 pounds to the carrying capacity per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the hand may perform light skilled tasks (picking simple locks, writing legibly, etc.).
Reconstitution

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You apply restorative force to a damaged object, reversing entropy at the material level. Fractures seal. Splinters rejoin. Rust flakes dissolve back into clean metal. The object recovers 3d6 Durability. The spell cannot restore a completely destroyed object — the original form must be substantially intact for the working to have a template.

Reconstitution works on weapons, armor, tools, structures, vehicles, and any other crafted object. It does not work on living creatures (which require healing magic) or enchanted items (which require Occult or Crafting checks to repair without disrupting the enchantment).
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d6 Durability restored per Mana Circle.
Cinder

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close (5 feet) Duration: Instant Resistance: Dodge

blocks.html: You exhale a tiny mote of compressed flame the size of a fingertip. The mote is enough to light tinder, ignite lamp oil, or start a campfire from prepared kindling. It is, first, a working tool — every Mage learns it because every Mage eventually needs to start a fire.

As an attack, the cinder deals 1d4 Fire damage to a creature in Close range. The damage is trivial against armor but enough to ignite hair, dry clothing, oil-soaked flammables. The spell exists in Incantare rather than Arcane Arms because its utility — the small precise fire-making — is its primary purpose, and the combat damage is incidental.
Flash Frost

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: Dodge

blocks.html: A brief pulse of intense cold flashes from your hand. The pulse can flash-freeze a glass of water, extinguish a candle, cool an inflamed wound, or skim a thin layer of ice across a 5-foot surface. Wet surfaces struck by Flash Frost become slippery and count as Difficult Terrain for one round.

As an attack, the spell deals 1d6 Cold damage to a touched creature. Like Cinder, Flash Frost lives in Incantare because its utility — the precise application of cold for everyday purposes — is the spell's reason for existing. The combat damage is incidental.
Lesser Purification

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You apply a cleansing pattern to food, drink, or a living creature. Toxins and poisons of Poor quality are eliminated immediately. Up to 10 gallons of water or 20 pounds of food may be purified, including the conversion of contaminated water into drinkable water. Bacterial spoilage halts, and organic material remains fresh for 1d6 additional days.

Lesser Purification is the traveler's blessing — dirty water becomes drinkable, suspicious food becomes safe, the poisoned comrade's stomach is cleansed before the toxin can finish its work. It cannot heal damage already dealt by a poison; it removes the source, not the symptoms.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, the spell removes toxins and poisons of Ordinary quality. At Mana Circle 6+, Fine.
Befoul

blocks.text: Mana: 4 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 2d4 hours Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You instantly contaminate food or drink with an invisible toxin that takes effect over hours. The toxin is colorless, tasteless, and undetectable by mundane senses. Any creature who consumes the affected food or drink within the duration must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll or suffer the Sickened condition for 1d4 hours, beginning 1d6 hours after consumption.

The toxin's delayed action is the working's strategic value: by the time the target falls ill, the Mage is long gone. Befoul is the spell that ends feasts, ruins receptions, and incapacitates the watch without bloodshed. Its existence is one of the reasons that high-status meals are inspected by detection magic before serving.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1d4 hours to the sickened duration per Mana Circle.
Wind Step

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 2 turns Resistance: None

blocks.html: You reduce a target's effective weight to a fraction of normal by sustained application of counter-gravitational force. While the magic holds, the target takes no falling damage from any distance, may multiply their jumping distance by ten, and gains 2 Charges on Athletics checks involving leaping, climbing, or balance.

The effect is brief — measured in turns, not minutes. Wind Step is the spell that lets the party cross the chasm, scale the cliff face, and survive the fall. Timing is everything. The maximum weight the working can support is 500 pounds; heavier targets are affected only partially or not at all.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 50 pounds to the maximum supported weight and 1 turn to the duration per Mana Circle.
Rapid Recuperation

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You accelerate a creature's natural healing processes by forcibly compressing the work of days into seconds. The target recovers 3d10 Durability and 1d8 Health. The acceleration consumes their reserves: they lose Vigor equal to the total amount healed.

If the target has insufficient Vigor to pay the cost, they are healed only as much as their current Vigor permits, and are then immediately Winded. Rapid Recuperation is emergency medicine with a price tag — it heals the wound and exhausts the patient. A Mage who casts this on themselves in combat usually does so once.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d10 Durability and +1d8 Health restored per 2 Mana Circles.
Minor Reinforcement

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You fortify a creature's body, applying coordinated force to their musculature, skeletal system, and nervous response. For the duration, the target gains +5 to their Strength, Stamina, and Speed Scores. The bonuses propagate through any Bonus Dice or traits derived from those scores. Minor Reinforcement is the buff spell that turns the fighter stronger, the scout faster, and the porter tougher. The effect is roughly half a Bonus Die bracket, applied to three scores simultaneously — efficient for a Basic-rank working.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +2 to each affected score per 2 Mana Circles (Circle 2: +7; Circle 4: +9; Circle 6: +11).
Hold Breath

blocks.text: Mana: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: The target's lungs hold air for an hour rather than the few minutes physiology would otherwise permit. They may breathe normally before and after the spell, but during the duration they may go entirely without breath. The spell does not protect against drowning damage from rough water, pressure damage from extreme depth, or against the inhalation of poisonous gases — it suspends the need for breath, nothing more.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per Mana Circle.
Resist Element

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: Choose one elemental damage type at the time of casting (Fire, Cold, Lightning, Acid, or any other element with a standard manifestation in your setting). The target takes half damage from that element for the duration. The protection persists through up to three damaging events before reducing to one-quarter, then ending after one more.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional damaging event before degradation per Mana Circle.
Minor Glamour

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You make subtle, almost imperceptible adjustments to a creature's appearance — your own or another you touch. The changes are too small for a casual observer to notice; the target simply seems slightly more or less of whatever they already are. Only a deliberate Awareness or Occult check VS your casting roll will detect the working.

For the duration, the target may benefit from one of: appearing plain and hard to notice (1 Charge to Legerdemain checks); appearing more affable and pleasant (1 Charge to Social checks); appearing more imposing (1 Charge to Intimidation checks); altering hair, eye, and complexion color. Minor Glamour does not create a disguise. It adjusts.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional simultaneous effect per 2 Mana Circles.
Quiet Countenance

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A more invasive glamour. The target becomes instantly forgettable — uninteresting, unnoticeable, unremarkable. For the duration, they gain 1 Charge on Legerdemain checks and +1 Bonus Die on Stealth checks. Attempts to remember, recognize, or identify the target are made with 2 Challenges.

The trade-off: for the duration, the target suffers 2 Challenges on Social checks. They are so forgettable that they cannot make an impression even when they want to. Quiet Countenance is the spy's spell, the fugitive's prayer, and the introvert's secret indulgence.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per Mana Circle.
Whispers of Mind

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: 1 mile Duration: Instant Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You send a short message of up to 25 words to a target you can clearly visualize. The target makes Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; if they fail or choose not to resist, they hear you as if you were standing immediately behind them, speaking in a whisper.

The recipient knows who is speaking — your voice is unmistakable — but cannot respond through this spell. Whispers of Mind is the long-range message: detailed enough to be useful, restricted enough to be safe.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 mile to the maximum range per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the recipient may reply with a single sentence.
Calming Word

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You speak a measured word in a sapient creature's hearing within Close range. They must succeed Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll or have their immediate hostility soothed. The target will not attack you or those near you for the duration unless attacked first, magically compelled, or directly threatened. The spell does not establish friendship — only the absence of immediate violence. It is the working that ends inn-brawls and gives time for negotiation when nothing else will.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional creature affected per 2 Mana Circles. At Mana Circle 4+, the spell also affects Beasts.
Swift Slumber

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You quickly induce a light sleep in a target within Close range. Their eyelids grow heavy, their limbs slack, and they lay down and sleep wherever they are, whatever they were doing, unless they succeed Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll.

The slumber is easily broken. Loud noises, attacks against the target, even rough handling will wake them immediately. Swift Slumber is not a weapon of war but of convenience — the spell that puts the sentry to sleep, the prisoner to peace, the difficult guest to bed.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 minute to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, sleeping targets are not woken by ordinary noise or rough handling — only by direct injury.
Blinding Mark

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You cast a cruel hex on a target you can see. A glowing runic mark — searing white, painful to look at — appears on the target's forehead or directly over their eyes. The target makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll; on failure, they are Blinded for the duration.

The mark is visible to all and cannot be hidden by helm or hood. A Blinded creature cannot see, automatically fails sight-dependent checks, and all attacks against them gain +2 Bonus Dice. Blinding Mark is the combat hex that turns a dangerous enemy into a flailing, vulnerable target.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 round to the post-Concentration lingering Blindness per Mana Circle (Circle 3 means the target remains Blinded for 3 rounds after you drop Concentration).
Dementia

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You impose discordant cognitive interference on a target you can see. They make Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, they suffer the Dazed and Dazzled conditions for the duration. On a successful Resistance, they are merely Dazed until the end of your next turn.

Dementia disrupts the target's ability to process sensory input. They see double, hear echoes that are not there, and cannot maintain a coherent train of thought. Targets who have experienced it describe it as one of the most disorienting experiences of their lives — significantly more debilitating than a head injury, and unpleasantly lingering even after the spell ends.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 round of lingering Dazed condition after Concentration ends per Mana Circle.
Oculo Maelicum

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Concentration Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: The classical Evil Eye. You fix your gaze on a target within Medium range and they must succeed Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll or be struck with the Sickened and Dazed conditions for the duration.

The hex is heavy — older than the formal traditions, predating most catalogs, taught as part of Incantare more out of historical inevitability than ceremonial preference. The target feels the weight of the curse like a physical blow: nausea, disorientation, and the absolute certainty that something terrible has been done to them.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge. At Mana Circle 7+, on a failed Resistance, the target is also Frightened of you for the duration.
Aegis of Five Forces

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: The foundational defensive working. You weave a ward around a creature you touch, aligning the five most-encountered force-types into a resonant lattice that absorbs and diffuses incoming energy. For the duration, the target takes half damage from Fire, Lightning, Cold, Sonic, and Aetheric damage.

The Aegis does not address physical damage, Entropic damage, Light damage, Poison, or unusual elemental sources outside the named five. It is the simplest and most reliable elemental defense in the catalog — the spell every working Mage casts before walking into a fight against another caster.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, the protection extends to Force damage. At Mana Circle 6+, also Light and Crystal. At Mana Circle 8+, also Soul and Entropic.
Sentinel Mark

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 8 hours Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You inscribe a reactive glyph on a surface — a small palm-sized runic pattern that stores a sliver of your awareness. The glyph is faintly luminous in darkness and invisible in normal light unless examined with detection magic.

When a creature other than you touches or crosses the glyph, the formula discharges. You are alerted telepathically at a range of up to 1 mile — a brief pulse of awareness. The triggering creature must succeed Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll or take 1d6 Aetheric damage and be outlined in residual light for 1 round (negating Stealth or invisibility on them). Sentinel Mark is the Mage's perimeter alarm.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 4 hours to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, add +1d6 Aetheric damage on trigger.
Shattering Shout

blocks.text: Mana: 6 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Dodge (object); Dodge (carried)

blocks.html: You release a piercing focused shout — a resonant sonic pulse — directed at an object you can see within range. Only inanimate objects are affected, but the resonance is brutal: glass shatters, locks crack open, ceramic crumbles, even sturdy armor splits along stress lines. The target takes 3d10 Sonic damage plus your Soul Bonus Dice.

An object worn or carried by a creature can be targeted if your casting roll beats the creature's Guard. The creature is entitled to a Dodge reaction (to twist the object out of the path of the shout). Shattering Shout is the anti-material spell — it does not harm flesh, but it destroys equipment. Swords, shields, locks, chains, doors, magical foci.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d10 Sonic damage per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target object also has 1 Quality Rank reduced if not fully destroyed.
Aetheric Lash

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: A whip-thin filament of raw Mana — golden, visible only as a brief streak of light — snaps toward a target. The lash deals 1d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice Aetheric damage. On a hit, the target's next casting check this round suffers 1 Challenge as the discharge introduces dissonance into their Mana flow.

The lash has no physical mass; it cannot be deflected by shield or blade. At its cost — 3 Mana — it can be thrown repeatedly without draining reserves, making it the Mage's ideal opening gambit against an enemy spellcaster. The disruption effect is the working's real value: cheap, fast, and capable of fouling another caster's prepared formula before it can resolve.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d8 Aetheric damage per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the casting Challenge becomes 2 Challenges instead of 1.
Befouling Touch

blocks.text: Mana: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: Your hand carries a brief pulse of decay. As part of the spell, you make a touch attack against a target within reach. On a hit, the target takes 2d4 Entropic damage and must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll or have their next Resistance check (any type) before the end of their next turn at 1 Challenge.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d4 Entropic damage per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the lingering 1 Challenge becomes 2 Challenges.
Bind the Tongue

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You impose a working on a sapient creature within range that forbids deliberate falsehood. The target makes Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, for the duration, they cannot speak a sentence they know to be false. They may remain silent. They may speak truth. They cannot lie.

Bind the Tongue is not the same as Magistrate's Inquiry — it does not read the target's mind, does not detect lies, does not even compel speech. It simply forbids the lie. A creature bound by it may refuse to answer; may answer truthfully in misleading ways; may speak technical truths designed to deceive (these are not detected as lies by the spell). What they cannot do is speak words they know to be false.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 minutes to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target also cannot speak technical truths designed to deceive (misleading-but-true statements are blocked as if they were lies).
Veil of Recall

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 24 hours Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: A target within Close range makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll. On failure, their memories of the last hour become blurred and uncertain. They can recall that something happened, that they were somewhere, that they spoke with someone — but the details are vague, the conversations indistinct, the faces unclear. The blurring persists for 24 hours, after which the memories begin to settle, returning over the following day.

Veil of Recall does not erase memory and does not insert false memory. The working is gentler than that, and harder to detect. The target knows they were somewhere; they simply cannot quite remember what was said or who was present. Mages use the working to cover their tracks after sensitive conversations. Healers occasionally use it to ease the immediate trauma of witnesses to terrible events.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the blurred memory span per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4 hours blurred; Circle 7: 8 hours).
Hand of Atlus

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: A large invisible hand of compressed force appears at a point within Medium range and obeys your will. The hand has a Strength Score equal to your Soul Score and may lift, carry, push, pull, throw, or hold objects up to 200 pounds. It can be moved up to 30 feet per round on your turn as a free action.

Hand of Atlus is the working version of Floating Hand. It cannot use weapons or perform skilled tasks but can grasp a fleeing creature, throw a barrel through a window, hold a door closed against three armed men, or lift a wounded ally from danger. It cannot exceed the listed weight limit; an attempt to lift more results in the hand losing its grip.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 100 pounds to the maximum weight per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the hand may make a basic grappling attempt against a creature (contested against the target's Strength).
Reading Through Time

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch (a location) Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You stand at a specific point and reach backward through the field's memory of that point. During the casting, you specify a moment in the past — up to one month ago — and the working shows you what occurred at this exact location at that moment. The vision is silent, visual only, and lasts up to one minute of perceived past time per casting.

The working is more precise than Echoes in Stone and more limited: it returns only what happened in this exact square, not the surrounding rooms or fields. Investigators use it for crime scenes, archeologists for ruins, the bereaved for places where the lost were last seen. The spell cannot show speech, cannot show the future, and cannot show what was hidden from view at the moment shown.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 month to the maximum lookback per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4 months; Circle 7: 8 months). At Mana Circle 5+, also returns audio along with the visual.
Echoing Tongue

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Special (see description) Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You attune to a specific creature you have met within the last month. The target makes Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, you hear the next sentence they speak aloud, regardless of distance, as if they were whispering directly into your ear. After hearing one sentence, the spell ends. The target may notice nothing, or may sense briefly that they were heard (1 in 6 chance, or always if they are themselves a Mage of Novice or higher Incantare).

Echoing Tongue is not eavesdropping in the traditional sense. It catches one sentence — the next sentence the target speaks aloud after the working completes. If the target is silent for the duration, the spell expires unused. If they speak immediately, the working delivers their words and dissipates. Spies pay handsomely for the working, and Mages pay carefully for the focus that lets them target a specific person without that person knowing.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 seconds to the listening duration per Mana Circle (so longer-Concentration Mages catch more than one sentence; at Mana Circle 4+, the working captures up to 4 sentences before ending).
Hollow Form

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You compress your body into two dimensions briefly — a flat shadow-form that retains your face, your voice, and your sense of self, but is only a fraction of an inch thick. You can slip under doors, through keyholes, through narrow vents, through any opening with at least a half-inch gap in one dimension. Your equipment goes with you, similarly flattened.

In Hollow Form you cannot fight effectively — your physical attacks are barely-felt taps; you cannot wield weapons in any meaningful way. You retain the ability to cast non-physical spells, observe, listen, and move at half your normal Speed. The form is fragile: any focused attack against you (whether or not it would normally hit) deals damage as if your Armor Rank were zero. The working is the infiltrator's working, not the warrior's.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 minute to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may speak (Hollow Form normally produces only a faint whisper) and may cast spells of any rank you can normally cast.
Tongue of the Earth

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune to the audio impressions held by a section of stone, soil, brick, or other solid mineral material. While Concentrating, you hear conversations and significant sounds that occurred above the touched material within the last hour. The impressions are fragmentary — like overheard scraps through a thick wall — and limited to spoken words and sharp distinctive sounds (a scream, a shot, a hammer-blow). Ambient noise is filtered out.

Tongue of the Earth is the investigator's complement to Echoes in Stone: the stone remembers not only what passed visually but what was said. Together, the two workings can reconstruct events with surprising completeness. Apart, each gives only part of the picture. The working does not work on living wood, on water, or on air — only on solid mineral materials with sufficient density to hold the resonance.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the lookback per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, also captures emotional tone (anger, fear, grief, joy) as a feeling-impression accompanying each sound.
Borrowed Sight

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch (then unlimited) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You touch a willing creature and bind your perception to theirs. For the duration, you see, hear, and smell what they perceive, regardless of distance. The bound creature gains nothing in return — their experience is unchanged. They may end the working at any time by spending an Action and an effort of will.

Borrowed Sight is the scout's working. A Mage with a willing companion in the enemy camp can experience the camp at a remove, safely, while the companion conducts their business as normal. The working has obvious applications in surveillance, intelligence-gathering, and the protection of distant loved ones. It cannot be cast on an unwilling target — Read the Spirit at Expert rank performs that function, with all its attendant ethical complications.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 minutes to the maximum duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may also communicate one-way (sending whispered instructions) to the bound creature.
Sentinel's Patience

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 24 hours Resistance: None

blocks.html: A specialized variant of Sentinel Mark. You inscribe a glyph that triggers only on a specific condition you describe in detail at casting — a named creature, a specific category of intruder (anyone wearing red, anyone carrying a weapon, anyone speaking the local trade-tongue), a specific action (someone attempting to open this chest, someone reading this book). The condition must be observable by the glyph and unambiguous.

When the condition triggers, the glyph alerts you telepathically as Sentinel Mark does, and the triggering creature is briefly outlined in light for one round. Sentinel's Patience does not deal damage by default. It is a precision tool: the alarm that ignores everything you said to ignore, and notices only the specific event you cared about. Mages use it to watch for one named enemy, one specific kind of intruder, or one event among many possible ones.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 12 hours to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may set the glyph to trigger on multiple distinct conditions, each evaluated separately.
Unseen Forces

blocks.text: Mana: 6 + variable Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You imbue multiple inanimate objects with directed motion. The objects float and act on your Initiative with Speed equal to half your Soul Score. While you Concentrate, the objects follow your mental commands as if you were handling them directly.

The spell affects up to 3 objects with a combined weight of 15 pounds. Each additional 2 Mana spent at casting adds 5 pounds of total capacity. There is no upper limit on the Mana you can invest in a single casting. Living creatures and magical items are immune to the working. Unseen Forces is the advanced cousin of Floating Hand — where the Hand animates one object, this spell animates dozens, turning a room's worth of furniture into a whirlwind of flying debris.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 3 objects to the maximum count and 10 pounds to the base capacity per Mana Circle.
Agathion Sentinel

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You create a guardian construct — a hovering bound essence shaped from compressed Mana, roughly the size and silhouette of a small humanoid. The Sentinel obeys your spoken commands and acts on your Initiative count. It has Health equal to 20 + your Soul Score, Speed equal to 30 feet, and may attack with a basic Force strike (1d10 force damage at +1 Bonus Die equal to your Soul Bonus Dice).

The Sentinel cannot move more than 60 feet from where it was summoned. It can open doors, lift weights up to 30 pounds, and physically defend a doorway, hallway, or threshold. It does not require food, sleep, or air. It vanishes at the end of the duration or when reduced to 0 Health.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 Health, 5 feet of Speed, and +1d10 Force damage to the Sentinel per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the maximum tether distance increases to 120 feet.
Armor of Marsus

blocks.text: Mana: 15 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You clad a target — yourself or another — in an invisible shell of compressed force that guards against harm. The Armor has Armor Rank 10 against all attacks and 100 Durability. Once the Durability is exhausted, the working vanishes.

The Armor does not interfere with physical armor — it is a separate layer, an outer shell that absorbs damage before the target's worn protection is tested. It is the most powerful personal defense at Novice rank, and the working that lets a Mage in robes walk into a fight against armored soldiers with reasonable expectation of surviving the encounter.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +20 Durability to the Armor per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the Armor Rank increases to 12. At Mana Circle 7+, Rank 14.
Major Aegis

blocks.text: Mana: 16 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: An expanded form of Aegis of Five Forces. The target gains half damage protection against Fire, Lightning, Cold, Sonic, Aetheric, Force, Light, and Crystal damage simultaneously for the duration. The protection also extends to one Resistance check per round of the target's choice (the named check is made at 1 Charge regardless of trigger).
Mana Circle Scaling. Add one additional element to the protection roster per 2 Mana Circles (Poison, Acid, Entropic, Soul, and other unusual elements may be added at higher Circles).
Aegis of Spirit

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You weave a protective lattice around a creature's spirit and mind. The target gains +3 Bonus Dice on Resistance (Soul) and Resistance (Resolve) checks for the duration. Hexes, mind-control effects, and possession attempts targeting the protected creature suffer 2 Challenges. The working does not block damage of any kind; it protects the soul, not the body.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to the protected Resistance checks per 2 Mana Circles.
Sheltering Shield

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction. Triggered when an ally within Close range is targeted by an attack. You raise a brief sheet of compressed force between attacker and ally. The ally gains +3 Bonus Dice on the Defense reaction to that attack and, if they successfully Defend, the attacker is briefly Dazzled (1 round) by the discharge of force.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to the Defense reaction per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the Dazzled effect also applies on a missed but defended attack.
Pulse

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self (15-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: A radial pulse of compressed force erupts outward from your body. All other creatures within 15 feet must succeed Resistance (Strength) VS your casting roll or take 3d8 force damage and be pushed 10 feet directly away from you. Creatures who succeed take half damage and are not pushed. The spell does not discriminate between friend and foe; allies in the radius are affected normally.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d8 force damage per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the radius extends to 20 feet.
Repulse

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close (20-foot cone) Duration: Instant Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: A directed cone of force fans outward from you. Creatures in the cone must succeed Resistance (Strength) VS your casting roll or be pushed 15 feet directly away from you. Targets who collide with terrain or other creatures take 2d6 force damage and fall prone; targets who reach open ground are pushed but suffer no further effect. Unlike Pulse, Repulse can be aimed away from allies.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the push distance and +1d6 collision damage per 2 Mana Circles.
Lesser Swiftness

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: The target's movement is accelerated. For the duration, they gain +10 Speed Score and their effective travel speed doubles for long-distance movement (the spell does not grant additional Actions per turn). The working enhances reflexes as well as motion — the target gains +1 Bonus Die to Initiative checks while the spell holds.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional target affected per 2 Mana Circles. At Mana Circle 5+, also gain 1 additional Action per turn for the first round of any combat that begins while the spell is active.
Vanishing Step

blocks.text: Mana: 16 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You become invisible by bending light around your form. The invisibility persists only while you continue moving — the moment you stop, the working ends and you become visible.

While invisible, you may make one attack Action per turn (even if you have more Actions available). The invisibility breaks the instant your attack lands, but the first blow falls unseen. Vanishing Step is the assassin's approach: move unseen, strike once, hope the first blow is enough.

Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, you may make two attack Actions before the invisibility breaks. At Mana Circle 6+, three. At Mana Circle 8+, the invisibility persists even while stationary, breaking only on attack.
Tay El Ard

blocks.text: Mana: 18 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self (up to 60 feet) Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You fold a portion of nearby space and step through it, emerging at a point you can see within 60 feet. The destination must be unoccupied and you must have line of sight (clear visual access to the destination square). Cover does not interfere with the spell; obstacles between you and the destination simply do not matter, as long as your eye can find the point.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 feet to the maximum step distance per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may bring one willing creature in physical contact with you through the step.
Oddic Form

blocks.text: Mana: 20 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Self Duration: Concentration up to 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: Your astral body separates from your physical shell. Your body remains where it is, breathing and alive but unconscious. Your astral form is incorporeal, invisible to mundane sight, and capable of moving through any non-magical barrier at your normal Speed. You perceive Mana currents directly, see invisible creatures, and may speak telepathically with sapient beings within Close range.

Your astral form cannot interact physically with the world. You cannot attack, lift, or speak aloud. If your body takes damage equal to half its Health while you are separated, you snap back to it immediately. Oddic Form is the Mage's scouting and reconnaissance working — and the working that gives the Mage access to places no physical body could reach.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the maximum duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the astral form may move 10 feet per round through magical barriers as well as physical.
Cast Visage

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 8 hours Resistance: None

blocks.html: You alter a creature's appearance to mimic any humanoid form of approximately the target's size — a specific person, a different race, a person of a different sex, a person of significantly different age. The visage is full: skin tone, hair, eye color, voice, scent, posture, distinguishing marks. Casual observers are entirely fooled; only deliberate magical investigation or close acquaintance with the impersonated person can break the illusion (Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll, with the recipient gaining 2 Challenges if they know the target intimately).

Cast Visage does not change the target's actual physical capabilities — a small target made to look large does not gain reach; a tall target made to look short does not actually shrink in size. The illusion is visual and auditory. The body underneath remains the same.
Burden of Atlus

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: You impose terrible gravitational force on a single target, multiplying their effective weight tenfold. The target makes Resistance (Strength) VS your casting roll; on failure, they are Encumbered — their Speed is halved, they cannot run or charge, and physical actions cost double Vigor. They make Resistance again at the end of each of their turns to throw off the working.

Flying creatures fail their Resistance automatically and are pulled to the ground. Burden of Atlus is the focused-target counterpart to Unbearable Weight: where the area spell pins many enemies in place, this working singles out a priority target — the enemy caster, the dangerous champion, the fleeing villain — and slows them to a crawl.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 Challenge to the target's Resistance check per 2 Mana Circles.
Obscuring Fog

blocks.text: Mana: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You summon a thick billowing fog in a 50-foot diameter sphere at a point within range. The fog fully forms by the end of your turn, rolling outward from the center in opaque tendrils. Visibility within the fog is reduced to zero — creatures inside cannot see, targets cannot be selected for ranged attacks, and movement is effectively blind.

The fog is the Mage's tactical reset button. When a fight is going badly, when the enemy has ranged superiority, when allies need cover for retreat or repositioning — fog. It does not discriminate between friend and foe; it is a weapon that requires planning as much as power.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 feet to the sphere diameter per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the duration extends to 5 minutes.
Obscure

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium (30-foot radius) Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: A magical darkness fills a 30-foot radius at a point within Medium range. The darkness is impenetrable to mundane vision and to most magical vision; creatures with darkvision see through it as if it were dim light. Magical light of equal or lower rank is suppressed within the area. The darkness moves with the point it was cast at (or with the object, if cast on a held object).

Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, the darkness suppresses magical light of equal rank. At Mana Circle 6+, creatures with darkvision also fail to see through. At Mana Circle 8+, the darkness blocks all sensory magic that depends on sight.
Unbearable Weight

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: You create a field of tremendous gravitational pull at a point within range. Choose an area 20 feet across; all creatures within must succeed Resistance (Strength) VS your casting roll or be crushed under their own weight (Restrained for the duration). Creatures who succeed are Encumbered — Speed halved, actions cost double Vigor.

Flying creatures fail their Resistance automatically and fall to the ground. Unbearable Weight is area-denial at its simplest and most effective: every creature in the zone falls down and stays down, or moves at half speed and pays double. It is the Mage's answer to cavalry, flying creatures, and any enemy who relies on mobility.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the area's diameter per Mana Circle.
Revealing Flame

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You enchant an open flame within range — a candle, torch, fire pit, lantern. The flame becomes a beacon. Any living creature within 30 feet of the enchanted flame is outlined in a luminous glow, visible to all observers.

Hidden and invisible creatures are revealed by the outline, which clings to them like phosphorescence. Creatures within the area cannot enter Stealth or invisibility while the working holds. Only physical concealment behind line-of-sight obstacles will hide a creature from observers. Revealing Flame is the anti-stealth working — cast on every campfire in every dangerous camp.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 feet to the radius around the enchanted flame per 2 Mana Circles.
Shackles of Marsus

blocks.text: Mana: 16 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: Strength

blocks.html: You bind a target within Close range in chains of solidified force. The target makes Resistance (Strength) VS your casting roll; on failure, they are Restrained for the duration. While Restrained, the target may attempt a new Resistance (Strength) check at the end of each of their turns to break free. The chains have 30 Durability if attacked directly (they ignore the bound creature's defenses but can be targeted by allies).
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +10 Durability to the chains per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the bound target's break-free Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Split Moment

blocks.text: Mana: 18 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Self Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction. You collapse a brief moment of time, granting yourself an additional reaction this round. The reaction may be used immediately, on the trigger that prompted this spell, or held for any later trigger before the start of your next turn. The spell does not grant additional Actions on your own turn; only reactions outside it.

Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 5+, the extra reaction may also be a 1-Action spell cast as if it were a reaction (paying full Mana cost). At Mana Circle 8+, you gain 2 extra reactions instead of 1.
Echoes in Stone

blocks.text: Mana: 8 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You attune to the impressions held by stone, brick, or other inanimate solid material. For the duration, you perceive events that occurred immediately adjacent to the stone within the last week. The visions are silent, fragmentary, and limited to physical events — a fight, a death, a passage of important people. The stone does not record speech, conversations, or thoughts.
Echoes in Stone is the investigator's working. The stone remembers what passed in its vicinity, and the Mage who can read it can reconstruct events long after the witnesses have departed or died.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 day to the maximum lookback per Mana Circle (Circle 3 reaches 10 days back; Circle 7 reaches a month).
Distant Missive

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Special (any distance same plane) Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You bind a written letter or short document (up to one page) to its intended recipient. At the conclusion of the casting, the letter vanishes and reappears in the recipient's immediate vicinity within seconds — slipping into their hand if they are alone, settling on the nearest flat surface if they are not. The recipient must be a creature whose name you know and whose face you have seen. The letter cannot be intercepted in transit. Once it arrives, it may be read normally and reads as the original (no magical traces remain on the page).

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional page of text per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the missive includes a brief sensory impression (smell, sound, image) of your immediate location at the moment of sending.
Soul Syphon

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You draw on a target's reserves of Mana, siphoning it toward yourself. The target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll; on failure, they lose 1d10 Mana, which you immediately gain (up to your maximum). Mana drained beyond your maximum is lost.

The drain is not damage in the conventional sense. The target feels a brief cold weariness in their Mana-channels and a hollow weight where their power used to be. Mages targeted by Soul Syphon often describe it as more unpleasant than a wound — the loss of their reserves is felt more keenly than the loss of blood.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d10 Mana drained per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4d10; Circle 7: 8d10).
Steal Life

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You touch a target and draw their vital force into yourself. They make Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll; on failure, they lose 2d8 Health (Entropic damage) and you recover the same amount in Health (up to your maximum). On a successful Resistance, the target takes half the damage and you recover half the amount.

The working is uncomfortable to cast and considered impolite even by Mages who would otherwise have no objection. It is one of the workings the Citadel's older texts call 'magic of the left hand' — legal, but watched.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d8 Health drained per Mana Circle.
Poison Soul

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 hour Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You introduce a slow hex into a target's Mana-channels. The target makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll; on failure, they are Sickened for the duration and lose 1 Mana at the start of each of their turns. The hex cannot be removed by ordinary healing — it requires Lesser Purification at Expert rank or higher, or the natural passage of the duration.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 minutes to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the Mana drain increases to 2 Mana per round.
Dissonant Counter

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Long Duration: Instant Resistance: Special

blocks.html: Reaction. Triggered when a creature within Long range begins to cast a spell. You introduce a precise resonant dissonance into their working — a sonic-aetheric pulse calibrated to the rank of the spell being cast. If the spell is Basic, it automatically fails. If Novice, you must succeed a contested casting roll. If Expert, contested roll at 1 Challenge. If Master, contested roll at 2 Challenges.

The dissonance does not block the casting attempt — the target's Mana is still consumed whether your counter succeeds or fails. Dissonant Counter is the Mage's primary defensive tool against other casters: cheaper than full counterspelling, harder for the opponent to anticipate, and effective at lower ranks even from a Mage who is not specialized in countermagic.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to the contested roll per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, Master counters are at 1 Challenge instead of 2.
Suppression

blocks.text: Mana: 15 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (15-foot radius) Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: An invisible sphere of magical dampening forms around you. Within the area, all spells of Basic rank are suppressed (cannot be cast); Novice rank spells suffer 2 Challenges on casting rolls and 50% Mana surcharge; Expert and Master spells function normally but at 1 Challenge. Magical items reduce one Quality Rank in effect (Heroic becomes Ordinary, etc.) while inside the area. Your own spells are not affected.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius and increase suppression by one rank per 2 Mana Circles (at Circle 4, also suppresses Novice; at Circle 6, also suppresses Expert at 2 Challenges).
