Apollyon Javelin

blocks.text: Mana: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Instant Resistance: Dodge or Deflect

blocks.html: You raise your hand to the sky and conjure a lance of brilliant light — a spear of compressed Aether and force, radiant and terrible. You hurl the javelin at a target within range; make an attack roll using your casting check. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 + Soul Bonus Dice Aetheric damage.

The javelin can be Dodged or Deflected — it is a physical projectile of condensed light, and shields can turn it aside. But a shield that Deflects the Javelin takes the full Aetheric damage to its Durability, and at higher Circle counts, few shields will survive the encounter.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d8 Aetheric damage per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4d8; Circle 7: 8d8).
Regal Water

blocks.text: Mana: 10 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You summon a stream of pure water from nothing — clean, drinkable, cold. The flow continues at the rate of a healthy spring (roughly 5 gallons per minute) from a point you designate within range. The water is real and remains real after the spell ends; it is not an illusion.

Aside from the practical applications — filling a cistern, supplying a besieged keep, drowning a small fire — Regal Water can be aimed offensively. A creature in the water's path must succeed Resistance (Strength) or be Pushed 5 feet on the first turn they are exposed. The spell does not deal damage, but a Mage with patience can extinguish bonfires, soak gunpowder, and flood narrow corridors.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 gallons per minute of flow rate per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 4+, the water may be temperature-controlled (hot or icy as the caster wills).
Combustive Burst

blocks.text: Mana: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (15-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You release a concussive pulse of pure compressed force in a 15-foot radius around yourself. All other creatures in the area must succeed Resistance (Speed) VS your casting roll or take 3d10 force damage and be knocked back 10 feet. Creatures who succeed take half damage and are not knocked back. Note that this spell deals force damage rather than fire damage — it is the working that crushes rather than burns.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d10 force damage and 5 feet of knockback per 2 Mana Circles.
The Reading of Names

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Close Duration: Permanent (the knowledge) Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You probe the spiritual essence of a sapient creature within Close range. They make Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge. On failure, you learn their True Name — the unique resonant identity by which their soul is known to the field. The Name is meaningless to mundane perception but is a focus for further workings: any spell you cast targeting the creature whose Name you know gains +1 Bonus Die to the casting roll and the target's Resistance against it suffers 1 Challenge.

The Name is not a verbal name — it cannot be spoken aloud meaningfully. It is a binding, a recognition, a key. Mages of advanced standing learn to identify the Name as a feeling, a chord, a pattern. The Reading of Names is one of the workings the Citadel keeps watched, not because it is forbidden but because a Mage who can read Names is a Mage who has identified themselves as someone who intends to use them. The knowledge is permanent until the named creature dies, undergoes a true change of essence, or removes the Name through dedicated countermagic.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to the working's casting roll per Mana Circle (improving the chance to read against high-Soul targets). At Mana Circle 6+, the target suffers 2 Challenges instead of 1 on their initial Resistance.
Veil of the Unborn

blocks.text: Mana: 28 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Self Duration: 1 turn Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction. Triggered when you would take damage or be subjected to an effect that requires a Resistance check. You briefly remove yourself from causal reality. You are immune to all damage, all conditions, all spells, and all environmental effects until the start of your next turn. You also cannot affect the world in return: you cannot move, attack, cast spells, or take any other Action while veiled. Your physical body is fully present and visible but cannot be interacted with by any force.

Veil of the Unborn is the survival working — the spell cast when the killing blow is mid-air and there is no other answer. The cost is high: a full turn of your participation in the world is forfeit, and the spell can be cast only once per encounter regardless of available Mana. Mages with the working prepared may survive what should have killed them. Mages without it sometimes do not.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 5+, the spell may be cast twice per encounter. At Mana Circle 8+, three times.
The Hollow Door

blocks.text: Mana: 22 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You open a temporary doorway through a wall, floor, ceiling, or other solid barrier. The opening is roughly 6 feet by 3 feet — a normal door size — and may pierce any non-magical barrier up to 1 foot thick. Creatures may pass through freely. The doorway closes at the end of the duration, sealing seamlessly, with no trace remaining of the working.

The Hollow Door does not work on magical barriers, force-fields, sealed wards, or barriers thicker than 1 foot at Expert rank. The opening is visible to both sides — anyone looking at the wall in the moment sees the door — so the working is not a stealth tool. It is an access tool: the spell that lets a Mage get into a sealed room, out of a locked chamber, or past a wall that would otherwise require demolition.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 6 inches to the maximum barrier thickness per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the door is briefly invisible from one side of your choosing during the first 30 seconds of its existence.
Conscript of Will

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Until completed or 1 year Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You impose a single specific task on a sapient creature you touch. The target makes Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge. On failure, they are compelled to pursue the named task until it is completed or one year passes, whichever comes first. The task must be specific and achievable — 'find the lost amulet,' 'deliver this letter to the Duke,' 'never speak a word against me' — and unambiguous in its completion.

Conscript of Will does not control the target's other actions. They live their life normally except where it conflicts with the conscripted task; when conflict arises, the task wins. The compelled creature is aware they are compelled but cannot resist the pull. Most accept and complete the task. Some break themselves trying to refuse it. The working is the closest Incantare comes to true mind-control, and it is among the most restricted workings the Citadel teaches.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 6 months to the maximum duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's Resistance is at 2 Challenges. At Mana Circle 7+, the target may be conscripted to a task that takes up to 5 years to complete.
The Three Doors

blocks.text: Mana: 26 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close (each) Duration: 1 turn Resistance: None

blocks.html: You open three temporary portal-points within Close range, each on a different solid surface. The portals are roughly 4 feet across, identical in appearance, and visible to all observers. On your turn or the next, you may step through any one portal as a free action; you emerge from a fourth, hidden destination point you specified at casting (any location within 100 feet that you can see and that has a surface to emerge from). Observers see all three portals open simultaneously and have no reliable way to predict which you will use until you commit.

The Three Doors is an escape working with theatrical applications. The misdirection is real: ranged attackers cannot effectively target the portal you will use because they do not know which one it is. Allies cannot follow you through the wrong portal because only one of them connects. The other two portals close at the end of the turn whether or not they are used. A Mage with this working prepared can flee almost any encounter with one prepared destination and a moment of free movement.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional decoy portal per 2 Mana Circles (Circle 2: 4 portals; Circle 6: 6 portals). At Mana Circle 5+, the destination may be up to 200 feet from the portal points.
Perfected Aegis

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: The completed protective working. The target gains half damage protection against all damage types — every element, every form of physical attack, every esoteric source. Additionally, the target gains +3 Bonus Dice on all Resistance checks for the duration. The working is exhausting to maintain; if you take damage, you must succeed Resistance (Concentration) at 1 Challenge per 5 damage or the spell ends.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to protected Resistance checks per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 6+, the half-damage protection becomes one-quarter damage protection.
Perfected Reinforcement

blocks.text: Mana: 22 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: An expanded form of Minor Reinforcement. The target gains +10 to all six Ability Scores (Strength, Stamina, Speed, Memory, Reason, Soul). Bonus Dice and derived traits all benefit accordingly. The target also gains +2 Bonus Dice on Initiative checks and immunity to Fatigue for the duration. Perfected Reinforcement is the spell every Mage casts on the party's champion before a major fight.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +2 to each Ability Score per 2 Mana Circles.
Aegis of the Citadel

blocks.text: Mana: 35 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Self (30-foot radius) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You raise a defensive aura that protects all allies within 30 feet of you. All protected allies gain +2 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions, +1 Bonus Die on Resistance checks, and immunity to fear effects of Novice rank or lower. The aura moves with you and refreshes its protection at the start of each of your turns. The working is the heart of any defensive caster's repertoire — the spell that turns a vulnerable party into a fortified one.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, also immune to fear effects of Expert rank or lower.
Sheltering Flame

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (10-foot radius) Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: Despite its name, this working uses no fire. You raise a translucent dome of compressed light and force around you and any allies within 10 feet. The dome blocks all incoming attacks of Basic and Novice rank automatically. Attacks of Expert rank suffer 2 Challenges on attack rolls against creatures inside; Master rank attacks resolve normally. Creatures inside may attack outward normally. The dome has 200 Durability.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +50 Durability per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, Expert attacks against inside creatures suffer 3 Challenges.
Shrugging Earth

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Instant Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You command a section of ground to convulse. Choose an area 30 feet across within range. All creatures in the area must succeed Resistance (Speed) VS your casting roll or fall prone and take 2d8 bludgeoning damage. Creatures who succeed remain standing but suffer 1 Challenge on attack rolls until the end of their next turn from the unstable footing.

The terrain in the area becomes Challenging Terrain — fissured, cracked, jagged — for the remainder of the encounter and beyond, until repaired by craftsmen or restored by greater workings. Buildings on the affected ground suffer structural damage (the GM determines specifics).
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the area's diameter and +1d8 damage per Mana Circle.
Frozen Blockade

blocks.text: Mana: 28 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Medium Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You raise a wall of jagged ice at a point within Medium range. The wall is 60 feet long, 15 feet tall, and 1 foot thick. Each 10-foot section has 50 Durability. Creatures cannot pass through the wall except by destroying a section. The wall does not deal damage on contact but is opaque, blocks line of sight, and provides full cover. It melts naturally over 10 minutes unless the spell ends earlier.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 feet to the wall's length and +25 Durability per section per Mana Circle.
Wings of Agathion

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: Wings of solidified force unfurl from the target's shoulders. The wings are visible — luminous golden-white, often described as feathered or fan-like — and grant the target a Fly Speed equal to their walking Speed. The wings allow hovering, diving, and full 3D maneuvering. They do not interfere with the target's other actions or worn equipment. The working ends if the target falls unconscious or if you choose to end it.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional target affected per 2 Mana Circles. At Mana Circle 5+, the Fly Speed is 1.5x walking Speed.
Hungry Grave

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Medium Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You designate a 20-foot radius area within range as devouring ground. Creatures who enter or end their turn in the area must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll or be slowly pulled into the earth — Restrained, sinking 1 foot per turn. A fully-sunk creature is suffocating and must succeed Resistance (Stamina) each turn to keep their head above ground.

Allies and creatures specifically designated by you at casting are unaffected. Hungry Grave is the area-control spell of the closing fight — it does not kill outright, but the moment an enemy enters it, the encounter shifts: their movement is forfeit, their attacks compromised, their attention divided between fighting back and not drowning in the dirt.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the sinking rate increases to 2 feet per turn.
Light of History

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You commune with an object's history. While Concentrating, you receive impressions of significant events that involved the object during the past year — who held it, what they did with it, where they took it, what important moments it witnessed. The visions are silent and fragmentary, like overheard scenes from someone else's dream.

The working does not provide a verbal narrative. The impressions are sensory: a flash of a face, the feel of a hand gripping, the room where the object was last set down. Skilled investigators can construct an entire history from these flashes; less skilled ones may simply learn that the object had a history at all.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 year to the lookback period per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 4 years; Circle 7: 8 years).
Read the Spirit

blocks.text: Mana: 28 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You probe a sapient creature's mind directly, reading their memories, intentions, fears, and concerns. The target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll; on failure, you have access to their conscious thoughts and recent memories for the duration.

The target is aware of the intrusion if they succeed Resistance, even though the spell continues against their will. Read the Spirit is the deep mind-reading working — distinct from Magistrate's Inquiry (which only detects lies) and Detect Thoughts (which only catches surface concerns). This spell reads the buried, the hidden, the willfully suppressed.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per 2 Mana Circles. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's awareness of the intrusion is suppressed (they do not realize they were read).
Ensorcelled

blocks.text: Mana: 22 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 hour Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: A single sapient creature within Close range must succeed Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll or regard you as a trusted friend and ally for the duration. They will not attack you, will defend you if able, will share information freely, and will give you the benefit of the doubt in disputes.

The charm does not grant absolute control. The target acts according to their own ethics — they will not commit obviously self-destructive or evil acts simply because you ask. When the spell ends, the target realizes they were charmed and reacts accordingly (often with hostility).
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Command Beasts

blocks.text: Mana: 25 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: Mind

blocks.html: All Beast creatures (non-sapient living animals) within Close range must succeed Resistance (Mind) VS your casting roll or follow your commands for the duration. The commands must be intelligible to a non-sapient creature (simple, direct, behavioral) and within the Beast's natural capacity. The working does not grant beasts intelligence beyond their normal capacity; it grants you authority over them.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 5 affected beasts to the maximum per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, dire and magical beasts may also be commanded (their Resistance is at +1 Challenge).
Madness of Ulvrik

blocks.text: Mana: 35 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You inflict raw cognitive overload on a sapient creature within Close range. They make Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, they are afflicted with temporary madness for the duration. The target's behavior is determined by a 1d6 roll at the start of each of their turns: 1) they attack the nearest creature, friend or foe; 2) they flee in a random direction at full Speed; 3) they stand frozen and Dazed; 4) they speak rapid nonsense aloud (1 Challenge on all rolls); 5) they hallucinate threats; 6) they re-roll, taking the second result.

Madness of Ulvrik is named for the chief codifier of Incantare, but the working predates him by centuries. It is among the most feared spells in the catalog — not because it kills, but because the target's mind is no longer their own.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 minute to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Dissolution

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Special

blocks.html: You target a single ongoing magical effect within Close range — a buff on a creature, a magical barrier, a sustained spell, a hostile enchantment, an active binding. Make a contested casting roll against the original caster's roll (or against a VS set by the GM for ambient or environmental magic). On success, the effect is dispelled entirely.

Dissolution is the targeted counterspell — distinct from Dissonant Counter (which targets spells being cast) and Suppression (which dampens magic in an area). Dissolution unmakes the spells that are already there, peeling them off the world. A Mage who has Dissolution prepared can answer almost any sustained magical effect, given enough Mana and time to identify the target.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to the contested roll per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, Dissolution may target up to 2 separate effects in a single casting (each contested separately).
Echoing Form

blocks.text: Mana: 28 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You create three echoing duplicates of yourself — semi-real images that move in perfect mimicry of your motions. When an attack targets you, the attacker rolls randomly to determine whether they strike you or one of the echoes. Each echo absorbs one hit before dissipating.

The echoes are not illusions in the strict sense — they have physical presence, and they cannot be detected by detection magic that distinguishes illusion from reality. They are reflections of you, briefly bound into existence. While at least one echo remains, you gain +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional echo per 2 Mana Circles.
Touch of Purity

blocks.text: Mana: 18 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You touch a creature and remove from them one Curse, one Disease, one Charm, one Possession, or one Petrification effect (target's choice if multiple apply). The spell also removes any temporary Ability Score reductions caused by magic. Permanent reductions, divine punishments, and planar bindings may require additional intervention beyond Touch of Purity. The working is among the most-requested Mage services in any city — there is always demand for someone who can lift curses.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 4+, remove two conditions in a single casting. At Mana Circle 6+, three.
Severing

blocks.text: Mana: 24 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You attempt to sever a target's connection to their own life-force. The target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll; on failure, they suffer 6d10 Soul damage (an unusual damage type that ignores most armor and resistance, treating only Soul-based or Aetheric-based protection as effective).

Severing is not death magic in the strict necromantic sense. It is the working that tells the target's spirit, briefly, that it does not belong in its body. The damage is real and substantial. A creature reduced to 0 Health by Severing dies in a particular way — quickly, painlessly, and with no visible wound. The Citadel keeps the working in Incantare rather than Nethermancy by formal classification only.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +1d10 Soul damage per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Tether of Souls

blocks.text: Mana: 20 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 1 hour Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You bind two creatures' spirits in tandem. Both creatures must be within Close range; the primary target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll (the secondary target, often the caster, is willing). On failure, the two creatures are linked: any damage the primary takes is also suffered by the secondary, and vice versa. This is shared damage, not transferred — both creatures suffer the original damage in full.

Tether of Souls is the prison spell — used to bind a captured enemy to a guard, to ensure a hostage's safety, to discourage an ally from being foolish. The link can be voluntarily broken by either party only with the caster's permission. Breaking it forcibly requires Dissolution or another counterspell.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 minutes to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the link distance is unlimited (the linked creatures may be in different cities and the effect still holds).
Siphon of Atlus

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A single target within Close range makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll. On failure, they lose half their current Vigor and you gain Vigor equal to half that amount. The drain feels like sudden exhaustion to the target — knees buckling, breath shortening, the immediate sensation of having run a long distance. The working is one of the favorites of long-encounter Mages, who use it to maintain their stamina from the reserves of the enemy.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add +10% Vigor drained per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 70% drained; Circle 5: 90%).
The Greater Memory

blocks.text: Mana: 80 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch (location) Duration: Concentration up to 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: You stand at a specific location and reach backward through the field's deep memory of the place. During the casting, you specify a window of time up to one week long, ending no more than one year before the present. The working returns to you, in full sensory replay, what occurred at this location during the named window — sight, sound, smell, the felt presence of those who passed through. You may experience the replay at any speed, may pause, may revisit, may follow one specific creature as they moved through the location, may attend to one specific corner.

The Greater Memory is an enormous working. It is the reason that murders committed in private a year ago can sometimes still be solved. It is the reason that very wealthy and very old families maintain wards specifically against this kind of reading on their homes. It is the reason that the Citadel keeps the working as Master rank rather than Expert: the historical and political implications of a Mage who can replay last week's secret council in any private chamber they can reach are significant.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 week to the maximum lookback per Mana Circle (Circle 3: 1 month; Circle 7: 2 months). At Mana Circle 5+, you may also experience the inner thoughts of one creature you focus on during the replay (their surface thoughts at the time, not their deeper reasoning).
Unbinding Word

blocks.text: Mana: 70 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Special (see description) Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You speak a single word that unmakes one specific magical effect you have personally observed and named. The effect ends instantly, regardless of its rank, its caster's power, or its remaining duration. The target effect must be something you have witnessed in person and can name with sufficient specificity: 'the curse on the Duke's eldest daughter,' 'the wards on the Western Tower,' 'the binding that holds the Glass Demon in the catacomb beneath the chapel.' Vague descriptions fail.

The Unbinding Word does not affect persistent natural magic, divine workings, or effects that are inherent to a creature's nature (a dragon's fire-breathing is not unmade by the working). It affects active magical effects that were cast by a sapient caster and that persist in the world. The working is one of the most powerful counter-magical tools in the catalog, and one of the most narrowly applicable. A Mage who has the Word prepared can end one specific magical problem, anywhere it exists, with three syllables and the proper focus. They cannot apply the working to a problem they do not specifically know about.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 1 additional Unbinding Word that may be prepared simultaneously per 2 Mana Circles. Each Unbinding Word is prepared during casting and held until used.
Veil of Generations

blocks.text: Mana: 100 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch (a remnant) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You commune with the spirit of a creature long dead, channeled through a physical remnant — a bone, a tooth, a lock of hair, an object the deceased held with significance during life. The remnant must have belonged to the named creature. The working summons a faint, semi-coherent impression of the creature's spirit, which you may converse with for the duration. The spirit retains its memories of life, retains its personality, and answers in the language it spoke when alive. It cannot lie deliberately, but its memories may be partial or unreliable depending on how long it has been dead and how strong the original impression was.

Veil of Generations is the historian's working, the investigator's working, the bereaved's working. The spirit is not summoned in the manner of necromancy — it is reached, briefly and with permission, through the lingering pattern that strong personalities leave in the field even after death. Most spirits accept the contact; some refuse, and the working then fails. The spirit cannot be compelled, cannot be harmed, and cannot be held longer than the spell's duration. When the working ends, the spirit dissipates back to whatever rest it had been keeping.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 minutes to the maximum conversation duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may also summon the spirit's faded visual likeness, allowing observers to see the speaker.
The Pull of Names

blocks.text: Mana: 65 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Unlimited (same plane) Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Special

blocks.html: A working accessible only to Mages who have previously performed The Reading of Names on the target. You speak the target's True Name and pull a brief impression of them to you. The impression appears in your space as a ghostly outline frozen in their current pose, lasting one minute. You may observe their current situation: their location (revealed as the apparent surroundings around the outline), their condition (wounds, conditions, equipment), their current activity (frozen as a tableau).

The target does not know they have been summoned. The working draws only an impression, not the target themselves; they remain wherever they actually are, doing whatever they were actually doing. The impression cannot be attacked or interrogated — it is a tableau, not a presence. The Pull of Names is the working that lets a Mage check on a hostile, a hostage, or a missing person they have named, from any distance on the same plane. It is the reason Mages who specialize in The Reading of Names are watched carefully by anyone whose Name they might have learned.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 seconds to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, you may interact briefly with the impression: ask one yes/no question that the impression's subconscious will answer truthfully if it can.
Command Form

blocks.text: Mana: 60 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Close Duration: Permanent Resistance: None

blocks.html: You reshape inanimate matter at the molecular level. The working affects a single, mostly homogeneous material — the blade of a sword, an axe handle, a boulder, a wall of poured concrete, a door of solid wood. A pile of bricks cannot be reshaped (it is not one material); a wall built of a single mass of stone can.

The shape may be anything you can imagine, and the transformation is permanent. To produce work of artistic or technical quality, a separate Crafting check is required — Command Form provides raw shaping, not expertise. Up to 20 pounds of material may be shaped per casting. Shaping a more massive object results in only a portion being affected, with the object taking 2d10 Durability damage from the stress.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 10 pounds to the shapeable mass per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the Mage may apply Crafting expertise to the working (their Crafting Bonus Dice apply to the result without requiring a separate check).
Gift of Inheritance

blocks.text: Mana: 80 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 24 hours Resistance: None

blocks.html: You transfer some or all of your knowledge and memories to a creature you touch. The recipient may use languages, skills, and talents you possess as if they were their own for the duration. You may transfer unlimited memories, languages, and similar information; you may transfer skill talents up to 1 skill point's worth per your Soul Bonus Dice.

Repercussions follow if the recipient's body cannot perform what their mind now knows. If their Ability Scores are insufficient, they suffer 1 Challenge on physical or combat checks using the transferred skills, and any Bonus Die that bursts on such a check inflicts 1d10 Durability damage. Magical knowledge is useless without Mana to fuel it. Only one Gift may be active at a time. If you die during the duration, the recipient may permanently retain some or all of the knowledge (GM determines).
Greater Transmute

blocks.text: Mana: 70 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Permanent Resistance: None

blocks.html: You refine and improve the quality of a non-magical item or material you touch. The item's Quality Rank increases by 1 (up to Legendary). The working cannot affect magical items or living creatures. Greater Transmute is the crafter's dream — the spell that turns Ordinary steel into Heroic steel, an adequate sword into a masterwork. Combined with a skilled smith, the working is the foundation of many regions' finest weapons and armor — and the source of many guilds' grievances against the Mage's College.
Mana Circle Scaling. At Mana Circle 5+, increase Quality by 2 Ranks instead of 1 (still capped at Legendary).
Shatter the Oddic Bond

blocks.text: Mana: 50 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: Dodge, then Soul

blocks.html: You forcibly evict the astral body of a creature you touch. In combat, you must succeed an attack roll against the target's Guard (the touch is the attack), and the spell can be Dodged. On a hit, the creature's incorporeal astral body is wrenched from its physical shell.

The effect is identical to Oddic Form — the target perceives the world, sees Mana currents, passes through non-magical barriers — but the separation is involuntary. The target may attempt to re-enter their body as an Action on their turn by succeeding Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll. You cannot end the working prematurely. The target's body remains alive but comatose for the duration. Shatter the Oddic Bond is one of the most feared workings in the catalog — it does not kill. It is worse than killing.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 30 minutes to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's body is also Sickened for 24 hours after being rejoined.
Storm Calling

blocks.text: Mana: 90 Casting Time: 1 minute Range: 1 mile per Mana Circle Duration: 10 minutes per Mana Circle Resistance: None

blocks.html: You call forth a storm of weather appropriate to the climate — a torrential downpour in temperate regions, a blizzard in cold ones, a sandstorm in arid lands, a thunderstorm anywhere. You are the storm's center and may control the intensity from light drizzle to torrential pounding.

The storm's effects are real weather, not illusion. Repeated castings in the same region rob the surrounding land of its normal precipitation and may cause drought, flooding, landslides, or ecological disruption over time. Storm Calling is the strategic weapon — it does not kill directly but controls the battlefield, ruins visibility, floods roads, and breaks sieges by drowning the besiegers' camp.
Transference

blocks.text: Mana: 100 Casting Time: 1 minute Range: Touch Duration: Permanent Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You take the spiritual essence of one creature and place it in another body, acting as the bridge between host and recipient. The transfer is permanent if completed. The receiving body must be empty (recently dead, ejected by Shatter the Oddic Bond, or willingly vacated). The transferring spirit is entitled to Resistance (Soul) if unwilling.

The transferred spirit retains its mental Ability Scores, Soul Score, and Mana, but the process permanently consumes 12 Mana from their maximum. If the cost reduces them to 0 or below, the working fails and the transferee dies. Health becomes the average of the two creatures involved. A 1d6 hour adjustment period follows during which the transferee is Sickened. The working is deeply taboo in most regions, considered uncomfortably close to necromancy by those who care about the distinction.
Spell Storm

blocks.text: Mana: 120 Casting Time: 1 minute Range: 1 mile per Mana Circle Duration: Concentration Resistance: Speed

blocks.html: You agitate the ambient Mana of a wide region, converting passive magical potential into active electrical force. The area around you becomes charged with sparks of static and crackling discharge. Creatures wearing 1 pound or more of metal — iron, steel, copper, bronze — become lightning rods for the storm.

Once the working is fully formed, an arcane storm rages overhead. Burdened creatures roll 1d6 at the start of each of their turns: on 2-4, they must succeed Resistance (Speed) VS your casting roll or be struck by lightning for 6d10 Lightning damage. Spell Storm is the anti-army weapon — every soldier in armor, every knight in plate, every guard with an iron sword becomes a target. The working does not discriminate.
Empty Well

blocks.text: Mana: 75 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (100-foot radius) Duration: Concentration Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You create a vacuum in the local Mana field — a hungry void that violently siphons magical reserves from every creature within 100 feet. Any creature with more than 1 Mana is susceptible. Creatures in the area must succeed Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll or immediately lose half their current Mana reserve. You receive the drained Mana, up to your maximum (excess is lost).

At the start of each of your turns for the duration, affected creatures lose an additional 1 Mana unless they succeed Resistance (Soul). The working is the anti-caster nuclear option — it does not target one enemy. It drains everyone in the area, friend and foe alike.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 20 feet to the radius and +1 additional Mana drained per round per Mana Circle.
Ruin of Foundations

blocks.text: Mana: 40 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Far Duration: Instant Resistance: None (for the structure)

blocks.html: You project a precisely calibrated pulse of structural-collapse force into the bearing matrix of a building, wall, or fortification. The pulse propagates through the structure in milliseconds, and load-bearing elements fail simultaneously. The structure collapses over the course of 1 round.

Creatures inside must succeed Resistance (Speed) VS your casting roll or take 6d10 impact damage and be Restrained under rubble, suffocating. On a successful Resistance, they take half damage and are thrown clear. Maximum target size is one keep, large building, or section of wall up to 100 feet long. The working is siege magic. The College teaches it only to those it trusts with the power to unmake castles.

Mana Circle Scaling. Add 25 feet to the maximum target dimension per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the structure collapses in 2 rounds instead of 1 (allowing creatures inside more chance to escape — or be caught).
Severance Absolute

blocks.text: Mana: 30 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: 24 hours Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: A devastating form of Severing. Where the lesser working kills a target by severing them from their life-force, Severance Absolute does something many consider worse: it cauterizes the target's connection to Mana entirely.

The target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll. On failure, they lose all current and maximum Mana for 24 hours. They cannot cast spells, activate magical abilities, or use Mana-fueled talents during that time. The connection returns at the next dawn. The working is called the Theorist's Execution in formal contexts — the punishment reserved for rogue casters who cannot be trusted with power. It does not kill. It takes away the only thing that makes a Mage dangerous.
Mana Circle Scaling. Add 6 hours to the duration per Mana Circle. At Mana Circle 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Aulder Prescience

blocks.text: Mana: 80 Casting Time: 4 Actions Range: Self (1 mile radius per Soul Bonus Die) Duration: Concentration Resistance: None

blocks.html: You cast your awareness wide. For the duration, you perceive the position and movement of every living creature with 10 or more Mana, every eldritch and arcane creature, and every active magical item within range. You know each target's distance, direction, and rough Mana-level relative to your own.

On your turn, you may spend an Action to focus on one perceived target, learning its precise location, current heading, Speed, and general appearance — a ghostly image impressed on your mind. Aulder Prescience is the omniscience spell. For the duration, nothing of magical presence can hide from you. It is the working generals cast before battle and the working a Master Mage casts when something in the region has gone very, very wrong.
Fold in the Heavens

blocks.text: Mana: 100 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Special Duration: 1 turn Resistance: None

blocks.html: You fold space and pierce a temporary hole through it. The breach is 8 feet in diameter and lasts until the start of your next turn. The far end opens at any location on the same plane that you have visited at least once and can remember clearly.

The range is theoretically unlimited but scaling: VS 15 for 1 mile, VS 20 for 10 miles, VS 30 for 100, VS 40 for 1,000, VS 50 for 10,000, VS 60 for 100,000, VS 70 for 1,000,000. The working consumes 20 Mana permanently from your maximum each casting, reflecting the strain of tearing the fabric of space. Fold in the Heavens is the Master Mage's emergency exit, surprise insertion, and ambush extraction.
Miracles
The Working Catalog of Divine Grace
Miracles are the channeled magic of the consecrated, performed much like magic, yet born form the power of grace and supplication.

Miracles are cast through the Priest's Faith an Occult skill check (with Soul Bonus Dice added to the casting roll), and consume grace. Practitioners who possess one more Chalices, that is divine receptacles in the spirit much like a Mage’s Circles, translate a Priest's standing with their covenant into the magnitude of the miracles they can produce. A Priest with three Chalices casts one kind of miracle; a Priest with seven Chalices petitions another. The scaling notes attached to each miracle below describe how Grace shapes the effect.

Casting a miracle takes Actions equal to its Casting Time. Most miracles take 1 to 3 Actions; rituals run longer. A Priest who lacks the Grace to fuel a miracle cannot petition for it, regardless of their Chalices or their standing. The petition still requires the petitioner to provide the working currency. Resistance checks against miracles use the Priest's casting roll as the VS; Resistance type is noted in the entry. Most beneficial miracles do not require a Resistance check from willing recipients.
Mending Word

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You speak a single quiet word over a wounded creature and the wound knits. The target recovers 1d8 plus your Soul Bonus Dice in Health. The miracle works on living creatures only; it does not affect constructs, undead, or fully incorporeal beings. The Word is the first miracle every Priest learns, and the one most often called for in the work of a parish.

Most Priests develop a habitual phrasing for the Word — the older the Priest, the more worn the phrase. Some say 'mercy.' Some say a name from their tradition. Some say nothing at all, only breathe over the wound. The miracle accepts whatever phrasing the petitioner has settled into. What the working requires is not the right word but the petitioner's sincere request.
Grace Scaling. Add +1d8 healing per Chalice.
Light of the Hearth

blocks.text: Grace: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A blessing of gradual restoration. The target recovers 1 Health at the start of each of their turns (during combat) or 1d6 Health every 10 minutes (out of combat) for the duration. The miracle does not stack with itself — a creature can have only one Light of the Hearth active at a time. Multiple Priests cannot stack Lights of the Hearth on the same recipient.

The miracle is often used by Priests traveling with a wounded companion on a long road: cast at first light, the Light works through the day, and the target arrives at evening better off than they began. It is also the miracle whispered over the sick child at bedside, the petition for one more night of recovery.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Health per turn / +1d6 per 10 minutes per Chalice.
Bind the Wound

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: The target stops bleeding. One Wounded condition is removed. The creature gains 1d4 + your Soul Bonus Dice in temporary Health that lasts 10 minutes or until consumed. The miracle does not heal the underlying damage — it stabilizes the immediate consequences, the way a battlefield prayer stops the blood long enough for the surgery to follow.

Bind the Wound is the miracle Priests learn for the work that follows battles. Wounded soldiers, dying common folk, the bleeding stranger by the road — the Priest kneels, lays a hand, speaks the petition, and the bleeding stops. Other healing follows after.
Chalice Scaling. Remove 1 additional Wounded condition per 2 Chalice ( Chalice 2: 2 conditions; Chalice 4: 3; Chalice 6: 4).
Hand of Mercy

blocks.text: Grace: 5 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You lay your hand on a creature and the toxin within them yields. One Disease, one Poison, or one Sickened condition affecting the target is removed. The Hand works on conditions of Basic and Novice rank; conditions of Expert or Master rank require Greater Mending or higher to address.

The miracle is the working most often requested of small-town Priests by neighbors with feverish children, sour stomachs, or bites from a venomous thing the village couldn't avoid. The work is humble and unending. A Priest performs a hundred Hands of Mercy over a lifetime and considers it the most important thing they ever do.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 3+, also remove conditions of Expert rank. At Chalice 5+, of Master rank.
Pulse of Vitality

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You press your palm against a fatigued creature's chest and they recover 10 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Vigor. The miracle cannot raise Vigor above maximum. A creature may benefit from Pulse of Vitality no more than once per scene. The miracle is the Priest's answer to a comrade who has spent themselves in the fight — the moment between exchanges where one is restored to fight again.

The petition is not a heal in the strict sense. The body is unwounded; the heart and lungs and limbs are tired. The miracle restores the wind. The recipient is the same creature they were a moment before, only with their reserves rebuilt.
Chalice Scaling. Add +5 Vigor recovered per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the once-per-scene limitation is removed.
Quickening Pulse

blocks.text: Grace: 6 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: The target's reflexes sharpen for the rest of the current round. They gain +2 Bonus Dice on Initiative checks (if combat has not yet begun) or take an additional reaction this round (if it has). The miracle is brief and small — the moment before the danger, when the Priest's hand on the warrior's shoulder gives them the half-second they would otherwise have lacked.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 3+, also gain +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions for the current round. At Chalice 5+, also gain 1 additional Action on the recipient's next turn.
Ward Against Malice

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: A blessing of guarded approach. The target gains +1 Bonus Die on Resistance (Resolve) and Resistance (Soul) checks for the duration. Creatures with hostile intent toward the warded target who attempt to attack them suffer 1 Challenge on their first attack roll each round.

Ward Against Malice is the petition cast over the parishioner who must travel through dangerous country, over the diplomat going to treat with hostile rulers, over the child crossing the dark wood alone. The protection is modest — not an armor, not a barrier. A reminder, in the field's listening, that the warded creature has friends who are watching.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per Chalice to the protected Resistance checks. At Chalice 4+, the first-attack Challenge increases to 2.
Quiet Mind

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A blessing against fear and intrusion. For the duration, the target is immune to magical Fear effects of Basic and Novice rank, immune to magical Charm effects of Basic rank, and gains +2 Bonus Dice on Resistance (Resolve) checks against mind-affecting effects of any rank. The miracle does not affect the target's mundane emotions — a frightened person is still frightened; the working only resists the magical induction of fear.

Priests cast Quiet Mind on the soldier the night before a great battle, on the witness preparing to testify against the powerful, on themselves before entering a place that intends to break their nerve. The petition is for stability of mind. The miracle answers as far as it can.

Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 3+, also immune to Charm effects of Novice rank. At Chalice 5+, immune to all Fear of Expert rank. At Chalice 7+, immune to all mind-affecting effects of Expert rank or lower.
Sanctified Step

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A small blessing of unfindable passage. For the duration, the target leaves no tracks, casts no scent, and produces no resonance detectable by Aura Sense or Reveal Presence or similar workings. Mundane detection (smell, sight, sound) still functions normally. Sanctified Step is the bodily complement to Quiet Countenance — it protects the trail rather than the appearance.

Priests grant the blessing to the messenger carrying a sensitive letter, to the witness fleeing the powers they testified against, to the small group slipping through a hostile camp. The working assumes the recipient knows enough not to be seen by the eye; the miracle only protects from being known by the field.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 hour to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the blessing also defeats magical scrying and divination targeting the recipient.
Veil of Mercy

blocks.text: Grace: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 8 hours Resistance: None

blocks.html: A protection against the worst kinds of magical attention. The target cannot be the target of divination magic, scrying, or location-finding workings cast at a distance greater than Close range. Mundane investigation, direct observation, and Close-range divination still function normally. The miracle is the working that protects the vulnerable — the child whose enemies hunt them, the witness whose testimony has not yet been given, the heir whose succession is contested.

Priests in dangerous regions sometimes maintain Veil of Mercy as a standing protection on the most vulnerable members of their parish. The cost is significant; the protection is real. A Priest who blesses a child with Veil of Mercy and then watches the child grow into a person no enemy could ever find at a distance has done one of the more important workings of their service.
Chalice Scaling. Add 8 hours to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the protection also defeats Close-range divination.
Crown of Watchfulness

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: The target's perception is sharpened by divine attention. For the duration, they gain +1 Bonus Die on Awareness checks, see through magical illusion of Basic rank automatically, and sense the presence of hostile creatures within 30 feet (general direction only, not precise location). The Crown is the bodyguard's blessing, the watch-Priest's working, the petition for the parishioner who must stand vigil through the night.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per Chalice. At Chalice 3+, see through illusion of Novice rank. At Chalice 5+, also gain +1 Bonus Die on Initiative checks.
Word of Steadiness

blocks.text: Grace: 2 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch or self Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: A single word spoken over a hand, a blade, or the recipient's heart. The target gains +1 Bonus Die on their next attack roll, ability check, or Resistance check before the end of your next turn. The miracle is small and ubiquitous: it is the petition cast over the gambler about to throw the dice, the swordsman about to swing, the singer about to take the stage.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per 2 Chalice (Chalice 2: +2; Chalice 4: +3). At Chalice 4+, the bonus applies to up to 3 rolls during the duration.
Touch of Resolve

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: A laying-on of hands that grants brief immunity to Fear. For the duration, the target cannot be Frightened by any source, magical or mundane. The miracle does not prevent the target from feeling fear — only from being incapacitated by it. The warrior with Touch of Resolve still hears the dragon's roar; they simply do not turn and run.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 minute to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the target also gains +1 Bonus Die to all attack rolls and ability checks while in the immediate presence of a feared source.
Anointed Blade

blocks.text: Grace: 5 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch (weapon) Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You bless a weapon with a small measure of divine radiance. For the duration, the weapon deals an additional 1d6 Lucent Damage on each successful hit. The weapon counts as magical for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction or resistance based on magical weapon requirement. The miracle is the working a battle-Priest casts on the company's blades before they ride to engagement.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d6 Lucent Damage per 2 Chalice (Chalice 2: 2d6; Chalice 4: 3d6; Chalice 6: 4d6). At Chalice 4+, the duration extends to 10 minutes.
Mantle of Service

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: A blessing of supporting strength on all allies within Close range. Each ally gains +5 to one Ability Score of their choice (Strength, Stamina, Speed, Memory, Reason, or Soul) for the duration. Each ally may choose a different score. The blessing's score reflects whatever quality the petitioner asks be reinforced — a battle for Strength, a parley for Soul, an investigation for Reason.
Chalice Scaling. Add +2 to the chosen score per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, each ally may choose two scores to enhance simultaneously.
Radiant Mark

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You name a target within Medium range as unworthy of mercy. They make Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, a luminous mark appears above their forehead, visible to all observers. For the duration, all attacks against the marked target gain +1 Bonus Die to attack rolls, the target's Stealth and concealment effects fail entirely (they cannot Hide while marked), and the mark cannot be removed by any working short of Touch of Purity or a Chalice-equivalent in any school.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to attacks against the marked target per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Strike of the Faithful

blocks.text: Grace: 5 Casting Time: Rider on basic melee or ranged attack Range: Self Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction (rider). You channel the radiance of your covenant into the triggering attack. The attack deals an additional 1d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Lucent Damage. If the target is an undead, demon, eldritch, or other creature whose existence directly opposes your covenant, double this damage. The miracle is the Priest's combat working, attached to any blow the Priest delivers.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 Lucent Damage per Chalice.
Witness of Truth

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You attune to the divine register of intentional falsehood. For the duration, you know whether the touched creature's next statement is true, false, or deliberately misleading. The target makes Resistance (Resolve); on failure, the miracle resolves as described. On success, the target becomes aware of the working but the miracle continues regardless.

Witness of Truth is the divine cousin of Magistrate's Inquiry. The mechanical effect is similar; the source is different. Where Magistrate's Inquiry reads the field's response to falsehood, Witness of Truth invokes the petitioner's covenant to bear witness. In settings where covenants have institutional weight, Witness of Truth's findings are admissible in court while Magistrate's Inquiry's are merely indicative.
Chalice Scaling. Add 10 minutes to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Sanctified Water

blocks.text: Grace: 4 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Permanent Resistance: None

blocks.html: You consecrate up to one flask of pure water (about 10 ounces). The water becomes Sanctified Water — useful as a thrown weapon against undead and eldritch creatures (1d8 Lucent Damage; treats Cover as half-strength against the target), as a cleansing agent for wounds (allies who wash a wound in Sanctified Water gain +1 Bonus Die on their next healing magic received from it), and as a sanctifying substance for further workings (other Priests' rituals that require holy water consume Sanctified Water as their material component).

Sanctified Water is the staple consumable of the Priest's stores. A working Priest produces several flasks per week, distributes them to allies, and consumes them in the work as they arise. The miracle is reliable, modest, and one of the most-used in any Priest's career.
Chalice Scaling. Produce 1 additional flask per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the damage from thrown Sanctified Water increases to 2d8.
Hallowed Voice

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: 1 mile Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You speak a short message of up to 25 words to a fellow petitioner of your covenant within 1 mile. The recipient hears you as if you were speaking softly directly to them. The miracle works only with fellow petitioners — fellow Priests, devout parishioners, those bound to the same covenant. It does not work for general communication with strangers or with those who reject your faith.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 mile to the maximum range per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the recipient may whisper a reply of equal length.
Mark of Chalice

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 week Resistance: None

blocks.html: You inscribe a sigil of your covenant on an object — a weapon, a piece of armor, a ring, a coin, a doorpost. The mark is visible only to fellow petitioners of your covenant. For the duration, any time you pray to your covenant within 100 feet of the marked object, you gain +1 Bonus Die on the petition. The marked object also serves as an emergency contact point: you may cast Hallowed Voice through it as if it were a fellow petitioner (allowing communication to anyone who can perceive the mark and listens for your voice).
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 week to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 3+, also gain +1 Bonus Die on miracles cast within 100 feet of the marked object.
Lantern of Faith

blocks.text: Grace: 3 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self or touched object Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A radiance of pale gold-white light surrounds you or a touched object. The light illuminates clearly to 30 feet and dimly to 60 feet. The light is harmful to undead and certain eldritch creatures within 10 feet of its source: such creatures take 1 Lucent Damage at the start of each of their turns within range, and suffer 1 Challenge on attacks against creatures illuminated by the Lantern.
Chalice Scaling. Add 5 feet to the bright/dim ranges and +1 Lucent Damage to affected creatures per Chalice.
Greater Mending

blocks.text: Grace: 8 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You restore 4d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Health to a touched living creature. The miracle knits substantial wounds — broken skin sealed, broken bone reduced, lost blood restored. It does not regrow lost limbs (Knit the Body for that) or cure systemic ailments like Disease or Curse (Hand of the Restoring Light or higher).
Chalice Scaling. Add +2d8 healing per Chalice.
Hand of the Restoring Light

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: Your hand glows with cleansing radiance. The target is cured of one Disease, one Poison, one Curse, one Possession, or one Petrification effect (target's choice if multiple apply). The miracle does not heal damage already dealt — it removes the source. Most working Priests learn the Hand by their third year of service; the petition is among the most-requested by parishioners.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 3+, remove two conditions in a single casting. At Chalice 5+, three. At Chalice 7+, all conditions of those types from the target.
Word of Restoration

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You speak a phrase and a single Condition affecting the target is removed: Stunned, Dazed, Frightened, Charmed, Blinded, Deafened, Sickened, Slowed, or any other condition of equivalent rank. The miracle does not remove physical damage or restore lost Health; it answers the working that imposed the Condition and undoes the working.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 3+, remove two conditions in a single casting. At Chalice 5+, three.
Pulse of Sanctified Life

blocks.text: Grace: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (15-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: A radiant pulse of healing extends from you in a 15-foot radius. All allies in the area (and yourself, if you choose) recover 2d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Health. The miracle is the formation-restorer: a Priest in the middle of a battered group casts the Pulse and the group is half-healed in a single Action.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 healing per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the radius extends to 20 feet.
Voice of Vitality

blocks.text: Grace: 8 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You call out to an ally within Medium range and they recover 2d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Health. The miracle is the Priest's ranged healing — the petition that reaches the wounded comrade across the corridor, the friend across the battlefield, the parishioner who has fallen and is too far to touch.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 healing per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the range extends to Long.
Knit the Body

blocks.text: Grace: 14 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You petition your covenant for the restoration of significant tissue. The target regenerates one minor missing body part — a finger, a toe, an ear, an eye, a small portion of a face. The regeneration is complete and functional; the regrown tissue is indistinguishable from the original.

Knit the Body cannot restore the dead, cannot restore lost limbs at the Novice rank (that requires Greater Healing or Knit the Bone at Expert), and cannot regrow internal organs lost more than a day ago. It is the miracle most often requested by survivors of significant trauma — the lost eye, the maimed hand. Working Priests perform the miracle once or twice per month in any reasonably populated parish.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 4+, the miracle can also restore a single major lost organ (intact lung, kidney, or similar). At Chalice 6+, an entire limb.
Sanctuary Bell

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: A bell-toned blessing of refused violence. While you Concentrate, any hostile creature attempting to attack you must succeed Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll or be unable to complete the attack — they hesitate, redirect, or simply choose another target. The protection ends if you attack a creature or cast a hostile working. Allies may attack normally; the protection is one-way.

Sanctuary Bell is the miracle a Priest casts when the Priest's death is not the answer to the situation. The healer at the wounded's side, the witness about to speak truth, the diplomat trying to broker peace — all of them sometimes need to remain alive without being able to fight. The miracle answers that need. Its limitations are real: a Priest under Sanctuary Bell can do nothing offensive without ending the working immediately.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 minute to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the protection's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Greater Ward

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A substantial blessing of protection. The target gains +2 Bonus Dice on Resistance checks, +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions, and immunity to Curses of Basic and Novice rank for the duration. The miracle is the working a Priest casts on the ally going into a place the Priest cannot follow — the assassin's preferred target, the heir traveling alone, the soldier into the breach.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, also immune to Curses of Expert rank. At Chalice 6+, of Master rank.
Shield of Faith

blocks.text: Grace: 8 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Close Duration: 1 round Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction. When an ally within Close range is targeted by an attack, you intercede. The ally gains +3 Bonus Dice on the Defense reaction to that attack and on any further Defense reactions until the start of your next turn. The miracle is the bodyguard-Priest's working, the petition that turns the killing blow into the deflected one.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the protection also extends to one additional ally within Close range for the same duration.
Sanctuary Step

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: Reaction Range: Self Duration: 1 round Resistance: None

blocks.html: Reaction. Triggered when you are targeted by an attack. You are translated briefly out of harm's way — incorporeal to all forms of attack for one round, immune to all damage, all conditions, and all spells. You may not act offensively during the round, but you may continue to move, heal allies, and cast non-hostile workings normally. The miracle is the healer's emergency exit: the working that lets a vulnerable Priest survive the moment that should have killed them.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 4+, the miracle may be triggered twice per encounter. At Chalice 6+, three times.
Anointing of Battle

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 encounter Resistance: None

blocks.html: You anoint a weapon and prepare it for the fight ahead. For the next encounter (or until the next Long Rest, whichever comes first), the weapon deals an additional 1d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice Lucent Damage on each successful hit, automatically, with no further Mana cost. Against undead and creatures of opposed-covenant nature, the damage is doubled. The weapon also counts as magical for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction.

Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 Lucent Damage to the weapon's strikes per 2 Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the bonus damage type may be changed to whichever element best matches the situation when the encounter begins.
Mantle of Courage

blocks.text: Grace: 14 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close (30-foot radius) Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A blessing of collective resolve. All allies within Close range when you cast (and any who enter the range during the duration) gain immunity to Fear effects, +1 Bonus Die on attack rolls against frightening creatures, and the ability to act normally even when reduced to half their Health. The miracle is the working a Priest casts before a great battle, when the army must hold firm and the captain has no other answer.
Chalice Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, also immune to Despair and Demoralization effects. At Chalice 6+, allies gain temporary Health equal to 5 per Chalice at casting.
Crown of the Just

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (15-foot radius) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: While you Concentrate, allies within 15 feet of you gain +1 Bonus Die on weapon attack rolls, +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions, and a small radiance that illuminates 5 feet around them. The miracle is the formation-Priest's working — the petition that turns a squad of soldiers into a coordinated fighting force.
Chalice Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius and +1 Bonus Die per 2 Chalice (Chalice 2: +2 dice on attacks and Defense; Chalice 6: +4 dice).
Word of Truth

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close (20-foot radius) Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: All allies within Close range gain immunity to Charm effects of Basic, Novice, and Expert rank for the duration, immunity to magical illusion of Basic and Novice rank, and +1 Bonus Die on Resistance (Resolve) checks against any deception. The miracle is the working that protects a delegation from deception during a negotiation, an army from infiltration, a parish from charmed corruption.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per 2 Chalice. At Chalice 5+, also immune to Charm effects of Master rank.
Banishing Word

blocks.text: Grace: 14 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: Permanent (banishment) Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You name an eldritch creature within Close range and command its return to its home plane. The target makes Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge if it is not native to the plane you stand on; on failure, it is banished to its home plane and cannot return for at least 30 days. Native-plane creatures resist at +1 Bonus Die.

Banishing Word is the miracle that ends the demon-summoning gone wrong, the eldritch incursion, the visit from beyond. The petition does not destroy the target — it returns them to where they belong. The 30-day banishment gives the parish time to ward, prepare, and seek the help of more experienced exorcists if needed.

Chalice Scaling. Add +1 month to the banishment duration per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 2 Challenges. At Chalice 7+, banishment is permanent unless the target is summoned again by deliberate working.
Greater Smite

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self (20-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: Radiance bursts from you. All creatures within 20 feet whose existence directly opposes your covenant (undead, demons, eldritch entities for most covenants) must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll or take 4d8 + your Soul Bonus Dice Lucent Damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Allies and neutral creatures are unaffected.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 Lucent Damage per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, the radius extends to 25 feet. At Chalice 6+, the damage applies to all hostile creatures, not only those opposed to your covenant.
Mark of Judgment

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You name a target within Medium range as deserving of focused attention. They make Resistance (Resolve) VS your casting roll; on failure, all allies attacking the marked target gain +2 Bonus Dice on attack rolls against them, and the target's Defense reactions against allies suffer 1 Challenge. The mark cannot be hidden, suppressed, or removed except by a working of Expert rank or higher.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 round to the lingering effect per Chalice (the mark continues for X rounds after the duration ends). At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Voice of Unmaking

blocks.text: Grace: 18 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Close Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You speak a word that severs the binding holding an undead creature to its undeath. Choose a single undead creature within Close range. It makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge; on failure, it takes 8d10 Lucent Damage. If reduced to 0 Health by this miracle, the creature is permanently destroyed — its remains cannot be reanimated, its spirit cannot be bound again, and the creature passes on to whatever judgment its covenant has prepared.

Voice of Unmaking is the exorcist's signature working. Lesser undead are usually destroyed in a single casting. Greater undead, named undead, undead of significant power resist; the miracle is one of the few working answers to such threats, and a Priest who reaches Novice Faith and knows it is an asset to any region troubled by the restless dead.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d10 Lucent Damage per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 2 Challenges.
Sense the Unfaithful

blocks.text: Grace: 6 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (60-foot radius) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: While you Concentrate, you sense the presence of creatures within 60 feet whose intentions are actively hostile to your covenant — those planning violence against you or yours, those engaged in worship of opposed covenants, those carrying cursed objects, those harboring concealed malice. You learn the general direction of each such creature but not their precise location.

Chalice Scaling. Add 30 feet to the radius per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, also learn the precise location of each detected creature. At Chalice 6+, also learn the nature of their hostility (the specific opposition or malice).
Voice of the Departed

blocks.text: Grace: 14 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch (corpse) Duration: 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You petition a recently-dead spirit (deceased within the past 10 days) to answer your questions before its passage is final. The spirit may answer up to three questions truthfully to the best of its memory in life. The spirit is not compelled — it answers because the petition is honored, not because the petitioner has authority. A spirit may refuse to answer if doing so would betray the dead's covenant or living loved ones.

Voice of the Departed is the divine equivalent of Speak with Dead. The mechanics are similar; the source is different. Where Incantare's Speak with Dead reads the field's lingering impression, Voice of the Departed reaches the spirit itself, briefly, before passage completes. Some traditions consider the Incantare version disrespectful and use only the Miracles version. Others consider them equivalent.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 additional question per 2 Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the lookback extends to 30 days.
Sign of Inquiry

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 minute Resistance: Resolve

blocks.html: You touch a sapient creature and ask one yes/no question through your covenant. The target makes Resistance (Resolve); on failure, they answer truthfully (verbally or by clear gesture). On success, they are aware of the working but answer however they choose. The miracle is more limited than Witness of Truth — it asks one question, gets one answer, and ends — but it is also more reliable, since the target's answer is compelled if the Resistance fails.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 additional question per 2 Chalice (each evaluated against a fresh Resistance check).
Sanctify Ground

blocks.text: Grace: 12 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Self (20-foot radius) Duration: 24 hours Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A 20-foot radius around you becomes sanctified ground for the duration. Undead, demons, and creatures opposed to your covenant suffer 1 Challenge on all rolls within the area. They cannot enter willingly without first succeeding Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge — those who fail are pushed to the boundary. Healing magic of any kind cast within the area is +1 Bonus Die effective.

Sanctify Ground is the working a Priest performs over a temporary camp, a battle field-position, a sickroom, a place of mourning. The protection is not absolute — a determined enemy can push through — but it is real, and the boundary is visible to those who can see Aura: a soft golden shimmer at the perimeter of the sanctified area.
Chalice Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius per Chalice. At Chalice 4+, also gain +1 Bonus Die on attack rolls against opposed-covenant creatures within the area.
Holy Anointing

blocks.text: Grace: 10 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 month Resistance: None

blocks.html: You anoint a willing creature with sacred oil and bless them with persistent divine favor. For the duration, the anointed creature gains +1 Bonus Die on Resistance (Soul) checks, +1 Bonus Die on the casting rolls of any miracles they themselves cast, and a small persistent radiance that warns of approaching undead within 30 feet (the anointed creature's skin briefly chills and tightens when undead approach within range).

Holy Anointing is the long-term blessing — the petition cast over a champion before they ride to war that will last months, over the cleric apprentice beginning their service, over the chosen heir of a covenant. It cannot be cast on a creature who already has Holy Anointing active. Most working Priests carry the oils for this ritual at all times.

Chalice Scaling. Add 1 month to the duration per 2 Chalice. At Chalice 4+, also gain +1 Bonus Die on any Resistance check. At Chalice 6+, the duration becomes permanent until consciously broken by the recipient.
Greater Healing

blocks.text: Grace: 18 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You restore 8d10 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Health to a touched living creature. The miracle also removes one Wounded condition and one ongoing Bleeding effect. Lost limbs are regrown over the following 24 hours (the target sees the limb beginning to regenerate within 1 hour and the regeneration completes by the next dawn).
Chalice Scaling. Add +2d10 healing per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the limb regeneration completes in 10 minutes rather than 24 hours.
Restoration of Spirit

blocks.text: Grace: 22 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You remove from a touched creature all Curses, Diseases, Possessions, Petrifications, and Exhaustions affecting them. You also restore any temporarily reduced Ability Scores. The miracle is the deep-healing working — the petition cast over the parishioner whose long sickness has finally yielded, over the survivor of demonic possession, over the cursed heir whose family has spent decades trying to lift the working.

Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 5+, the miracle also restores permanent Ability Score reductions caused by magical means within the last year. At Chalice 7+, restoration extends to any permanent reduction caused by magic at any time.
Mass Mending
