blocks.text: Grace: 22 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (30-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: A wave of radiant restoration extends from you in a 30-foot radius. All allies in the area (including yourself) recover 4d10 + your Soul Bonus Dice in Health and have one Wounded or Bleeding condition removed. The miracle is the major-action healing — the working that turns a battered, half-defeated party back into a fighting force in a single round.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d10 healing per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the radius extends to 40 feet.
Sanctified Sanctuary

blocks.text: Grace: 25 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Self (60-foot radius) Duration: 8 hours Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You consecrate a 60-foot radius as a Sanctified Sanctuary. For the duration, no hostile creature can enter the area without first succeeding Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 2 Challenges. Failed entries push the creature back to the boundary. Within the Sanctuary, allies recover 1d4 Health at the start of each of their turns and gain +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions. The Sanctuary's boundary is visible to all observers as a faint pale-gold ring around the area.
Chalice Scaling. Add 30 feet to the radius and +1 Bonus Die to allies' Defense per Chalice.
Aegis of the Last Warden

blocks.text: Grace: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Touch Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: A dramatic personal blessing. The target gains immunity to one chosen damage type (Fire, Cold, Lightning, Force, Aetheric, etc.), +5 Bonus Dice on Defense reactions, +50 temporary Health, and immunity to Fear and Charm for the duration. The miracle is the Priest's working cast over a Champion about to face a known and dangerous opponent — the petition that says 'we cannot lose this person to this fight.'

Chalice Scaling. Add +1 chosen damage type immunity per 2 Chalice. At Chalice 5+, also gain immunity to one chosen Condition type for the duration. At Chalice 7+, immunity to all damage types is granted simultaneously.
Smite of Heaven

blocks.text: Grace: 22 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Long Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A pillar of radiance descends from above onto a target within Long range. The target makes Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll; on failure, they take 12d8 Lucent Damage. Targets opposed to your covenant double this damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

Smite of Heaven is the Priest's siege-breaker. The pillar is visible to any observer in line of sight for miles — a column of pale-white light descending from the sky to a single point. After the strike resolves, the column dissipates and only the ground bears witness: the impact point is scorched in the shape of a circle, the scorching often remaining visible for years afterward.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d8 Lucent Damage per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the target's Resistance is at 1 Challenge.
Voice of the Final Judgment

blocks.text: Grace: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (30-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: You speak a word that judges the unworthy. All creatures opposed to your covenant within 30 feet must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge or take 6d10 Lucent Damage and be Stunned for 1 round. Creatures who succeed take half damage and are not Stunned. Allies, neutrals, and worthy creatures are unaffected.
Chalice Scaling. Add 5 feet to the radius and +1d10 Lucent Damage per Chalice.
Unmaking Light

blocks.text: Grace: 28 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Medium (30-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A field of devastating radiance fills a 30-foot radius at a point within Medium range. All undead creatures in the area must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 2 Challenges or take 10d10 Lucent Damage; on success, they take half damage. Undead reduced to 0 Health by Unmaking Light are permanently destroyed (cannot be reanimated, cannot return).

Unmaking Light is the working that ends necropolis. A Priest of significant rank entering a tomb of restless dead can clear three or four chambers in a single casting; the working scales hard enough that a Master Priest can purge a small army of undead in two or three uses of it. The Priest's reputation in the surrounding region is forged largely by what they do with this working.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1d10 damage per Chalice. At Chalice 6+, the miracle also affects demons and eldritch creatures (not only undead) within the radius.
Mantle of Authority

blocks.text: Grace: 25 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (30-foot radius) Duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes Resistance: None

blocks.html: You raise a halo of authority visible to all observers. All allies within 30 feet gain +2 Bonus Dice on attack rolls, +1 Bonus Die on Defense reactions, immunity to Fear effects of Expert rank or lower, and +1 Bonus Die on ability checks of all kinds. The miracle is the working that turns a Priest into a battlefield commander — the petition that elevates the immediate allies into a coordinated force.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 Bonus Die per 2 Chalice to the listed bonuses. At Chalice 6+, the radius extends to 60 feet.
Halo of the Host

blocks.text: Grace: 20 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Close Duration: 1 minute Resistance: None

blocks.html: You bless up to one ally per Chalice within Close range with a circlet of pale radiance visible above their heads. Each haloed ally gains +1 Bonus Die on all rolls, +5 to their Mana maximum (if they have a Mana pool), +1 Bonus Die on Resistance checks, and may add +1d6 Lucent Damage to any weapon attack they make during the duration.

Halo of the Host is the Priest's pre-battle blessing on the war-party. The blessings are visible as the company moves out, and the morale effect is significant; an army that watches its Priest visibly halo its champions before the fight watches its champions march out with greater confidence.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 additional ally affected per Chalice. At Chalice 5+, the duration extends to 10 minutes.
Crown of the Sovereign

blocks.text: Grace: 30 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: 1 day Resistance: None

blocks.html: An apex personal blessing. The target gains +2 to all Ability Scores, +2 Bonus Dice on all rolls, immunity to Fear, immunity to Charm, immunity to a chosen damage type, +20 maximum Health (and is healed to full), and a regenerative blessing (recover 1d6 Health per round in combat, 1d10 per minute out of combat). The miracle is the working cast over the chosen Champion before they face a god-touched threat. The Priest who casts it has invested an enormous amount of Chalice; the working should not be repeated more than once per Long Rest.
Chalice Scaling. Add +1 to chosen Ability Score scaling per Chalice. At Chalice 6+, the target also gains an additional Action per turn for the duration.
Witness of the Last

blocks.text: Grace: 18 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Self Duration: Variable Resistance: None

blocks.html: You petition your covenant for guidance about an event within the next 24 hours. The covenant responds with a vision: a brief glimpse of one significant moment that will occur within that window, shown from the perspective of an observer present at the moment. The vision is fragmentary and silent; you see what occurs, but not necessarily what causes it or what follows. The Witness of the Last cannot be petitioned about events caused by the petitioner themselves (the petitioner's own actions are not revealed in this way).

Witness of the Last is the working that lets a Priest navigate the immediate future. Most Priests cast it before great events — battles, council meetings, journeys into dangerous country — and use the vision to inform their actions. The miracle does not show the future as fixed; it shows what will occur if no significant intervention shifts the path. A Priest's actions can change the outcome — and that, in many traditions, is the point of the working.
The Final Mending

blocks.text: Grace: 50 Casting Time: 1 hour (Ritual) Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: You petition your covenant to restore a creature dead for no more than 7 days to life, provided their soul has not been claimed, destroyed, or barred from return. The body need not be intact — even ash or a single recognizable token suffices. The restored creature returns to full Health, full Mana, full Vigor, and full Chalice, and is purged of all Conditions, Diseases, Poisons, Curses, and Possessions. Restored creatures regrow any missing organs or limbs. Aging is not restored; a creature who died of natural age may be restored, but will continue to age normally from the moment of return.

The Final Mending is the working that defines a Master Priest in their region. It is petitioned for the loss that the covenant agrees must not stand — the heir cut down by treachery, the parish's child taken by accident, the comrade who fell in service. Not every petition is granted. The Priest who casts the Mending knows, before the petition is made, that the covenant may refuse — that the soul may have already passed beyond reach, that the dead may have chosen rest. When the petition is granted, the moment is the kind that becomes folklore.

Chalice Scaling. The miracle's component cost (typically rare alchemical materials worth 25,000 silver) reduces by 5,000 silver per Chalice (minimum 5,000 silver). At Chalice 7+, no material components are required.
Hand of the Last Warden

blocks.text: Grace: 50 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: 1 hour Resistance: None

blocks.html: A reality-bending personal blessing. The target gains complete immunity to all damage of all types, all Conditions, all spells of Expert rank or lower, and reduced effects from spells of Master rank (half damage, half duration, half effect). The target also gains 1 additional Action per turn for the duration and may move twice their normal Speed without spending Movement. The miracle is the Priest's apex protection — the working cast over the Champion who must walk into the lair of a god-touched threat and emerge alive.
Chalice Scaling. Add 30 minutes to the duration per Chalice. At Chalice 6+, also gain complete immunity to Master spells (rather than reduced effects).
Voice of the Eternal

blocks.text: Grace: 60 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Self (300-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Soul

blocks.html: You open your mouth and the covenant speaks through you. All creatures within 300 feet who can hear you must succeed Resistance (Soul) VS your casting roll at 1 Challenge or be subjected to one of the following effects (chosen by you at casting): instantly Stunned for 3 rounds; instantly Frightened of you for 1 hour; struck dumb (unable to speak) for 24 hours; rendered Charmed by you for 1 hour; or commanded to perform one simple immediate action (drop your weapon, fall to your knees, leave this place).

Voice of the Eternal is the miracle that ends speeches, breaks negotiations, halts armies. The Priest channeling it does not speak in their own voice for the duration of the casting — observers describe their voice as multiplied, layered, resonating as if many speakers spoke simultaneously through the Priest's mouth. The working is overwhelming, and the witnesses to it often describe it as the moment they understood what their tradition meant when it described divine speech.
Light of the Host

blocks.text: Grace: 55 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Self (60-foot radius) Duration: Instant Resistance: Stamina

blocks.html: A radiance from a higher source descends through you in a 60-foot radius. All allies in the area are healed to full Health, gain immunity to one chosen damage type for 1 hour, and have all Conditions removed. All hostile creatures in the area whose existence opposes your covenant must succeed Resistance (Stamina) VS your casting roll at 2 Challenges or be permanently destroyed (undead) or banished to their home plane for 100 years (other opposed creatures). Creatures who succeed take 8d12 Lucent Damage and are Stunned for 2 rounds.
Chalice Scaling. Add 10 feet to the radius and +1d12 Lucent Damage per Chalice.
Crown of the Saint

blocks.text: Grace: 80 Casting Time: 3 Actions Range: Self Duration: Concentration up to 1 minute

blocks.html: You enter a state of transcendent Chalice. While in the Crown, you gain immunity to all damage and all Conditions; +5 Bonus Dice on all rolls; one additional Action per turn; the ability to cast any miracle of Expert rank or lower without paying its Mana cost; and a radiant halo visible to all observers that illuminates a 60-foot radius. You may also speak to your covenant directly during the duration, asking questions and receiving answers as if in personal conversation.

The Crown of the Saint is the miracle that approaches godhood. The Priest who wears the Crown is not, briefly, a Priest at all; they are a vessel for the covenant's full attention. The state cannot be maintained beyond one minute regardless of available Chalice, and after the working ends, the Priest is Fatigued (3) for three full Long Rests and may not cast any miracle above Basic rank during that recovery.
The Last Petition

blocks.text: Grace: 100 Casting Time: 10 minutes (Ritual) Range: Variable Duration: Variable Resistance: Special

blocks.html: You petition your covenant for a one-time intervention in the immediate situation. The petition must be specific and within the scope of your covenant's interests. Examples include: ending a plague that has gripped your parish; granting victory in a specific battle; preventing a specific death; revealing the truth of a critical mystery; banishing a specific named demon permanently; calming a natural disaster; reversing a specific recent event. The GM is the final arbiter of whether the petition is granted, partially granted, or refused.

Each casting of The Last Petition has a 50% chance of imposing 1 year of withdrawn covenant: during this period, the Priest's Chalice is halved, their miracles of Expert and Master rank cannot be cast, and their covenant does not respond to ordinary prayers. The miracle is rarely cast — most Master Priests will use it once in their career, perhaps twice. It is the working that defines the legends Priests are remembered for, and the cost is part of why those legends are remembered.
Chalice Scaling. At Chalice 6+, the withdrawn-covenant chance reduces to 33%. At Chalice 8+, to 20%.
The Greater Mending

blocks.text: Grace: 70 Casting Time: 1 Action Range: Touch Duration: Instant Resistance: None

blocks.html: A working of overwhelming healing. The target is restored to full Health, full Mana, full Vigor, and full Chalice simultaneously. All Conditions affecting them are removed (Wounded, Bleeding, Fatigued, Stunned, Charmed, Frightened, Diseased, Poisoned, Cursed — all of them, regardless of rank or source). The miracle is the apex restoration: the working that takes a dying, broken, cursed, possessed comrade and returns them whole.
Chalice Scaling. Add 1 additional target healed simultaneously per 2 Chalice (Chalice 2: 2 targets within Touch; Chalice 6: 4 targets).
The Final Light

blocks.text: Grace: 80 Casting Time: 2 Actions Range: Special (line of sight, any distance) Duration: Permanent Resistance: None

blocks.html: You speak the name of an evil and cast a working that will, in time, undo it. Name an active source of evil in the world that you have personally witnessed or directly identified: a specific cult, a specific demon's binding, a specific cursed object, a specific plague's source, a specific tyrant's reign. The covenant accepts the petition. Over the course of the next year, the named evil will be undone — by the petitioner's continued actions, by the consequences the working sets in motion, by the alignment of circumstances and other agents the covenant arranges.

The Final Light is not a guarantee. The named evil may resist, may relocate, may transform. But the covenant has committed: the working is active, and over the year, every action the Priest takes against the named evil is amplified, every coincidence aligns in their favor, every chance encounter that could help them does. At year's end, the named evil is either undone or has demonstrated that it is beyond the petitioner's covenant's power to undo — in which case the covenant must seek other answers, and the Priest learns the limits of their tradition.

Performances are special actions fueled by Entertain checks and Mana. A performance takes 1 turn to initiate and requires upkeep each turn. Unless otherwise noted, performances affect targets within hearing or sight range (GM discretion). Maintaining a performance beyond its initial effect requires spending 1 action each turn.
Distract (2 Mana): Force a target within 20 ft to focus on you. They suffer -5 Speed until the start of their next turn.
Encourage (2 Mana): An ally who can hear you gains +1 Bonus Die to their next action roll.
Demoralize (2 Mana): A hostile creature makes a Resistance check VS your Entertain roll or suffers 1 Challenge on their next roll.
Soothing Tune (2 Mana): An ally regains +1d6 Vigor or reduces Stress/Fear conditions by 1 step.
Cruel Mockery (8 Mana): When you Demoralize, you may inflict -1 Bonus Die (instead of a Challenge) on all target rolls until end of your next turn.
Beguile (8 Mana): When you Distract, you may instead impose the Dazzled condition. Target resists each turn with Resistance VS your Entertain.
Rally (12 Mana): Encourage up to 3 allies; each gains +1 Bonus Die on either attack or defense rolls until the end of your next turn.
Inspiring Verse (8 Mana): Choose one ally; until your next turn they gain advantage on Initiative and may reroll Speed checks.
Cloud the Mind (12 Mana): As part of a Deception check, implant a short-lived falsehood (invisibility, illusory threat, etc.) until end of your next turn. Target resists with Resistance (Willpower).
Glamour (8 Mana): With a Social (Charm) check, you Enthrall a target for up to 1 hour, so long as you don’t break the effect. When it ends, they recall events as dreamlike haze.
Song of Clarity (12 Mana): Allies within 30 ft may reroll any failed Resistance check vs Fear/Charm/Confusion once during the effect.
Dance of Distraction (10 Mana): You move and perform at once; enemies targeting you suffer 1 Challenge on attack rolls until your next turn.
Word of Command (15 Mana): As an Intimidation check, force a target to obey a simple one-turn command (attack, flee, drop an item). Resisted by Soul or GM discretion.
Virtuoso’s Anthem (20 Mana): All allies within 30 ft gain +2 Bonus Dice to their next action. Lasts 1 turn.
Withering Mockery (15 Mana): Affected enemies lose 2d6 Mana immediately and suffer 1 Challenge on all actions for 1 turn.
Chorus of Triumph (20 Mana): Allies within hearing range immediately regain +2d6 Health, +2d6 Vigor, and +1 Bonus Die to their next roll.

Mana:2

You establish a simple beat tied to your heart rate. Allies within hearing gain +5 Speed until your next turn.

Mana:2

You strike a piercing chord. One creature within 30 ft suffers 1 Challenge to Reactions until your next turn.

Mana:2

You create a stable musical flow. One ally may ignore 1 Challenge die applied to their next action.

Mana:8

You amplify vibrations in the air. Allies may reroll failed Sound-based rolls (echolocation, tracking, etc.) for 1 minute.

Mana:8

A slow, hypnotic progression distracts one enemy. They must pass a Resistance (Soul) check or lose their Reaction for the round.

Mana:8

An ally’s next action creates a musical echo. If it hits or succeeds, another ally within 30 ft may immediately take 1 Free Action.

Mana:12

You perform an accelerating rhythm synced to pain. Allies regain 1d6 Vigor each time they take damage this turn.

Mana:10

A discordant note assaults a target’s inner ear. They suffer 2 Challenge on Balance, Precision, or Ranged attacks until end of turn.

Mana:12

You lock a target’s movement to your melody. On a failed Resistance, they must mimic your movement or suffer -10 Speed.

Mana:20

You play a death-hymn that fuels violence. All allies within range gain +1 Bonus Die and +1d6 damage on their next successful attack.

Mana:18

While performed, all allies within 30 ft resist mental control or Fear with +2 Bonus Dice.

Mana:20

A crescendo detonates in psychic space. Enemies within 20 ft must pass Resistance (Soul) or suffer 2d6 psychic damage and become Dazed.

Mana:2

You call out precise tactical advice. One ally may reposition 5 feet without provoking a Reaction.

Mana:2

You insult an enemy’s technique. They suffer 1 Challenge to their next Melee or Technique action.

Mana:2

Your voice rallies the weak. Allies recovering from a condition reduce its severity by one step.

Mana:8

You tell a paradox or riddle aloud. One enemy within 30 ft must succeed a Resistance (Soul) check or lose their Action next turn.

Mana:8

You rally up to 3 allies, who may immediately reduce Fear or Stress by 1 level and gain +1 Bonus Die to their next Resistance roll.

Mana:8

You call out an enemy by name, invoking past wrongs. The named enemy must focus all actions on you or suffer 2 Challenge on non-you targets.

Mana:12

You tell a tale that plays on guilt. One enemy takes 3d6 psychic damage and cannot willingly attack until the start of your next turn (Resistance applies).

Mana:10

You mark an ally or enemy with a verbal geas. Their next roll gains either +2 Bonus Dice or 2 Challenge Dice (your choice, GM decides if target accepts).

Mana:12

You empower a group with divine presence. Allies ignore the next penalty or debuff they would receive this round.

Mana:20

You proclaim sentence. One enemy of your choosing suffers 5d6 fire or Lucent Damage (your choice), Resistance (Soul) to halve.

Mana:18

You expose the hidden with forceful rhetoric. All invisible or disguised creatures within 60 ft must make a Soul Resistance or be revealed and suffer 1 Challenge to all rolls.

Mana:20

While maintained, all allies regain 1 Mana per round and gain +1 Bonus Die to all Leadership or Social rolls.

Flourish Step

Mana:2

You perform a distracting flourish. Enemies targeting you with ranged attacks suffer 1 Challenge on the roll until your next turn.

Baiting Spin

Mana:2

You step into threat range and back, drawing attention. One enemy must make a Resistance (Soul) check or waste their Reaction.

Trick Landing

Mana:2

You recover from an impact or throw with Chalice. Gain +10 Speed for 1 turn and land on your feet.

Phantom Waltz

Mana:8

You create the illusion of multiple bodies through movement. Until your next turn, enemies targeting you must choose randomly between your illusions.

Disarming Feint

Mana:8

You fake a stumble to throw off a weapon-user. Target must make a Resistance (Precision) check or drop a held item.

Whirlwind Draw

Mana:8

You leap into motion and draw a weapon as you go. Immediately gain +1 Bonus Die to your next Melee roll and move 10 ft without triggering Reactions.

Echo Step

Mana:12

You step in such a way that your past and future selves blur. Until your next turn, you may use Defense twice against a single attack.

Chainflow Tumble

Mana:10

You flow through multiple enemies, striking or disturbing each. Move up to 30 ft and make an Entertain vs Resistance check on each enemy passed. On failure, they are Dazed.

Momentum Seal

Mana:12

You end your movement in a locked stance. Until your next turn, you gain +2 Bonus Dice to Defense but cannot move.

Dead Air Vault

Mana:20

You leap off dreamlike momentum, ignoring gravity. You may leap 60 ft in any direction without fall damage. While airborne, attacks from below suffer 2 Challenge.

Violence Waltz

Mana:20

A flurry of movement and strikes. Choose up to 3 targets within 20 ft — make a single Melee roll against each with +1 Bonus Die. You may move between each attack.

Dance of Endings

Mana:18

A deadly finale. You strike the ground or your chest; all enemies within 15 ft must succeed Resistance (Soul) or fall prone and suffer 3d6 damage as fate tightens around them.

Enchanting allows you to bind magical runes into physical items, granting them powerful effects; known as enchantments. This process draws on your mastery of the Occult, requiring knowledge of ritual components, access to rune materials, and the ability to channel Mana into the object.
Enchantments are built from three magic Circles, each representing a different aspect of the magical effect:
Force Circle. Defines the strength and scale of the enchantment. This sets the enchantments rank, base VS, and Mana Cost.
Concept Circle. Defines the nature or school of the effect, whether it is elemental, spiritual, eldritch, etc.
Structure Circle. Defines define how and where the enchantment takes effect.
All three circles must be inscribed correctly during the enchantment ritual.
Enchanting is a complicated process requiring hours of work a deep focus.
Prepare the Item. You must have the item to be enchanted and the three core components that meet or exceed the quality of the item you are enchanting.
Perform the Ritual. Spend a number of hours equal to your final VS and pay the enchantment’s Mana cost.
Make an Occult Check. The base Victory Sum (VS) is determined by the specific circles in your enchantment. The base is set by the force circle then your specific
On Success, the enchantment is bound into the item.
On Failure, the item is not enchanted, but the components and Mana are expended.
An item’s Quality Rank determines how many enchantments it can hold at once:
✴ Curses are negative enchantments and can be applied even to low-quality items. Some dark rituals and forbidden runes may only produce cursed effects.
You may take Chances on the Occult roll to increase the effective rank of your item with regards to the number of enchantments, however.
Each circle is its own discipline, and falls into the rank of novice to master – there are no basic enchanting circles.
The foundation of most enchantments is its force. These are closely guarded enchantments of each school.

Each Concept Circle defines the effect or thematic energy that powers the enchantment. Learning a Concept Circle allows you to craft, identify, and fully utilize enchantments that draw on that source.
Concepts set the core effect values and increase the base Mana cost based on the Rank:

rows: Teleportation, displacement, range extension, dimensional seals
Radiance, divine favor, warding, repelling undead or corruption
Toxins, virulence, afflictions, degeneration over time
Luck manipulation, probability shifts, triggering conditional effects
Sonic force, voice amplification, confusion, speech-based effects
Illumination, healing, clarity, bane against darkness
Shock, paralysis, overload, sudden burst effects
Death effects, necrotic energy, weakening vitality
Solar light, holy fire, searing truth
Delay, speed alteration, repetition, echo actions
Cold damage, slow, freeze, elemental fortitude
Physical resilience, health, enhancement of the flesh
Mana storage, spell boosting, arcane resistance
Healing, energy transference, protection of the soul

blocks.html: These Circles define how an enchantment activates or takes effect. A character must learn each Structure Circle individually as its own discipline to use it in enchanting or identify/enhance its effects.
Structure Circles increase the target enchanting VS based on the Rank of the structure.

rows: The effect is always active while the item is equipped or held. Ideal for constant bonuses, resistances, or passive buffs.
Triggers when the enchanted item successfully hits a target. Common for damage, debuffs, or Conditions.
Triggers when the user is attacked or makes a Defense reaction. Often applies resistance, deflection, or counter-effects.
The user must spend 1 action to activate the effect. Duration is often 1–3 turns. Good for tactical abilities or situational effects.
The enchantment holds a number of limited uses that recharge daily or through rituals. Stronger effects, but limited in frequency.
Can be triggered outside your turn in response to a trigger (e.g., damage taken, spell cast, ally downed). Rare and powerful.
Creates a persistent area of effect around the user or item. Best for buffs, zones, or radiating deterrents.
Modifies or enhances another action (e.g., spells, skills, invocations). Often boosts action rolls or applies bonus dice.
Automatically triggers in response to a specific condition (e.g., when reduced to 0 Vigor, when surrounded). High utility, low control.
Holds a spell or ritual that can be released by the wielder or under a defined condition. Extremely potent and flexible.

Scrolls are one-use magical scripts that store a single spell, ritual, or invocation for instant casting. They allow a non-caster or untrained individual to release controlled magic once.

items: Requirements: You must know the spell or ritual you wish to inscribe.
Base VS: 25 + (10 × Spell Rank).
Mana Cost: Equal to the spell’s casting cost × 2 (infused into the scroll).
Materials: Alchemical ink, arcane parchment, and a reagent or crystal focus matching the spell’s Concept Circle.
Time: 1 hour per 10 VS.
Occult Check: Roll Occult vs the total VS.

blocks.html: Success: The scroll is successfully crafted and holds one use.
Failure: The materials and Mana are consumed, and the spell misbinds (GM discretion — minor magical backlash or wasted reagent).
Reading a scroll takes 1 Action.
The spell activates immediately, using the creator’s original Occult result as its roll value.
Scrolls ignore skill requirements, but not resource costs (e.g., reagents or sacrifices).
Once activated, the scroll burns away, leaving a faint magical residue.

Grimoires are permanent books, tablets, or codices that store magical knowledge. They can be used repeatedly for casting, study, or teaching. Unlike scrolls, they contain structured bindings of power that can replenish over time.

items: Requirements: You must know all Circles used in the spell or ritual being inscribed.
Base VS: 40 + (10 × Spell Rank).
Mana Cost: Equal to the spell’s casting cost × 3, infused during inscription.
Materials: A bound tome, crystalline core, or engraved medium worth at least 500 × the spell’s Rank in silver.
Time: 1 day per 10 VS.
Occult Check: Roll Occult vs the total VS.

blocks.html: Success: The Grimoire is created and holds a permanent, stable record of the spell.
Failure: The materials and Mana are consumed; the incomplete text becomes inert.
You may cast directly from the Grimoire once per hour, using its stored Occult value or your own, whichever is higher.
A Grimoire can also be studied to learn the contained spell as a Discipline, requiring an Occult check VS the spell’s crafting VS and the appropriate time investment.
You must still meet all Concept and Structure Circle prerequisites to learn or replicate the spell.

Every cultivator's path is long. The First Star is the work of years; the Third is the work of a decade; the cultivation of a true Mystery may take a lifetime of patient attention to small currents others have never noticed. Advancement in Radiant Age is the slow, recorded accumulation of who you have become — and the world remembers.

Throughout your adventures, you advance in several ways: gaining experience points, learning new Disciplines, gathering treasure and equipment, expanding your cultivated engines, and accomplishing the Titles and Deeds that mark your legend. Some growth is incremental — the small skill-point gain at each level. Some is transformative — the engine grant that opens a new tier of capability, the Deed that brings a lasting Perk, the multiclass commitment that opens a second path. This chapter explains all of it, including the rules for combining classes, the framework for Titles and Deeds, and the specific honors and accomplishments that mark cultivators across Radiant Age.

You gain Experience Points (EXP) by overcoming challenges, completing objectives, and engaging meaningfully with the world of Radiant Age. EXP accumulates and is converted into Levels at the thresholds shown in the table below. Each Level grants you skill points, resource increases, and the opportunity to deepen your cultivated engines.
Each level you gain produces the following automatic bonuses, before any other talent or class effects apply:

When you achieve a new level, you automatically gain 1 skill point to spend on a new skill bonus, skill talent, general talent, or Elite Tree purchase. So long as you meet the prerequisites, you may purchase any talent available to your class or available through the Core Skill Trees. This represents the small, incremental growth we all make as we gain experience and lived practice.

Each level grants 1d10 additional Health. Health is the only resource that cannot be increased with talents — only through gaining experience and levels. It represents the accumulation of physical and spiritual fortitude that comes from surviving difficult things.

Each level grants +6 Durability and +4 each to Vigor and Mana, as baseline. Your Foundation Talent (chosen at character creation) adds additional scaling — Steel Mantle adds substantial Durability per level, Arcane Reservoir adds substantial Mana per level, and so on. Heritage features and General Talents may also add to these baselines.

There are no formal limits to what level you can achieve; the system supports continued growth beyond Level 50. However, the cultivation of mortal beings tends to plateau around recognizable thresholds:

items: Levels 1-13 (Ordinary Cultivation). The cultivator is in the long apprenticeship — the period during which their first cultivation engine matures and they purchase the talents of their Ordinary Class tree.

Levels 14-25 (Heroic Cultivation). The cultivator has crossed the Heroic threshold and begun deepening into one or more specialty classes. By Level 25, the cultivator has typically completed a full Heroic class and stands as a recognized senior practitioner.

Levels 26-50 (Epic Cultivation). Most mortals will never reach this tier. The cultivator's craft is now legendary in scope — capable of changing the political landscape of regions, redefining schools of practice, and accomplishing things ordinary cultivators consider impossible.

Levels 50+ (Legendary Cultivation). Ancient beings, founders of traditions, the named heroes whose stories are still told centuries after their deaths. Few mortals reach this tier; those who do are functionally beyond ordinary mortal limitations.

blocks.html: Most mortal creatures will plateau around Level 25 to 30 before age and accumulated injury catch up with them. Ancient and powerful creatures who have spent lifetimes amassing power might reach Level 100 or more. The longest-lived beings of the Realms of Ouroboros — certain Ceith elders, divine archons, eldritch survivors — might have achieved Level 250 or higher.

A path begun is not a path closed. The cultivator who has walked their first class to its end may, with deliberate effort, begin another — and the combined craft may grow into something neither path could produce alone.

Radiant Age supports the deliberate cultivation of more than one class over the course of your career. Where many cultivators commit to a single Ordinary class and follow it into one Heroic specialty, others choose to build broader: an Armsman who has also studied Mage discipline; a Mage who has cultivated a Mystery; a Rogue whose Edge has been refined by Spiritualist work into something stranger and more specific. Multiclassing is the formal recognition of these broader paths, and the rules below explain how characters acquire and develop a second (or third) class.
You may add additional classes provided you meet the skill and ability score requirements of that class:

items: Level 2 or higher. You must have reached at least Level 2 before you may add an additional class. However, most classes have such requirements that you may need to be level 5 or higher to add a new class.

Meet the new class's ability score requirements. Advanced elite classes have specific requirements before you may add them, including ability score and skill prerequisites. To add an additional ordinary class you muts have at least 15 in one of two ability scores, as determined based on the class:

Skill Ranks. To add a new class you must possess the requisite skills and talents. When adding an Ordinary class you must posses that class’s starting Skills at novice rank. When adding elite classes, you must meet that class’s listed skill requirements.

Talents and Deeds. Many of the most potent elite classes have specific talent or deed requirements. Some can be earned simply by progressing in an ordinary class, suct as the knights 2-Star requirement. But others, such as the Dragon Mage’s Arcane Overload deed requirement must be earned.

blocks.html: When you commit to a new class, you gain the following benefits through the Class Training general talent:

items: The new class's starting talent, the class-specific Rank 1 starting talent of the Elite skill tree from your new class.
Access to the new class's Elite Tree, which you may purchase into using skill points from this level forward.
You do NOT gain a second Foundation Talent. Foundation Talents are once-per-character commitments made at first level.
You do NOT gain the new class's starting Heritage skill duplications or History benefits. Those belonged to your original character creation.

blocks.html: Once you have added one or more new classes, you continue to gain 1 skill point per level as normal. You may spend these skill points on either your original class's Elite Tree, your new class's Elite Tree, the Core Skill Trees, or General Talents — whatever you choose, in any combination.

You cannot, for example, purchase Rank 3 talents in your new Mage class until you have purchased the Rank 1 and Rank 2 prerequisites. The new class begins at Rank 1 in its Elite Tree, regardless of your character's actual Level.

Your character’s level and their classes are separate. You don’t have 2 levels of Armsman and 3 levels of mage. Rather you are simply level 5, with access to the Armsman and Mage elite talent trees. Your level is a general descriptor of your overall power, where as your class list represents the catalogue of talents you may purchase.

Levels measure the years of work; Deeds and Titles measure the moments that change you. The Star you ignite is the work of practice; the Deed you accomplish is the work of being alive at the right moment, ready when the world asked something of you.

Titles and Deeds reflect your renown and accomplishments. They influence how NPCs see you, unlock social opportunities, and add depth to your roleplaying. Beyond the narrative weight, they often grant mechanical Perks — small but lasting bonuses that the character carries for the rest of their career.

Titles are accomplishments that announce your ability and powers to the world. Several can be earned through roleplay alone, and some emerge naturally from cultivation progression (reaching Master rank in a skill, for example). Deeds, by contrast, can only be accomplished through your daring exploits — they require sustained effort, narrative significance, and the alignment of risk, mastery, and circumstance that the system can recognize as exceptional.

There are many Titles and Deeds that are well-defined, presented in this chapter as examples and starting points. The GM can always decide that some incredible action you have taken qualifies as a Deed of an existing or new design. Titles and Deeds are not always good things — dramatic failures or choices with catastrophic consequences can earn you Titles and Deeds too, which may haunt you the rest of your days. When you accomplish a Deed, you earn an associated Perk: a special bonus, knowledge, ability, or other lasting boon that becomes part of your character.

Titles are symbols of your journey and identity. As your character grows, you may earn Titles through narrative milestones, hard-fought accomplishments, or mastering elite talents. Unlike Deeds, which directly enhance your character's mechanical power, Titles reflect who your character has become. Some are earned by completing one-step story arcs, slaying a legendary foe, solving a diplomatic crisis, or surviving a near-death experience. Others emerge from deep mastery — granted upon unlocking a Rank 4 or 5 elite talent, or upon reaching a Master rank in a Core Skill.

Most Titles are incomplete and open for customization based on what you accomplished and your character's personality. You may combine your Titles to create something unique to you — the wandering ascetic who is both "Saint's Hand" and "Of Stone" might use the combined Title "Saint's Hand of Stone" in some contexts; the dark cultivator who has earned both "Devil-Touched" and "Wyrdspeaker" might be known as "The Wyrd-Touched." The exact combination is yours to author.

When you accomplish something truly extraordinary — something that has taken multiple moments to achieve — you may have accomplished a Deed. No Deed is easy. It could take several chapters of effort, sometimes spanning sessions, to fulfill a single Deed's Steps. Few characters accomplish more than five or six Deeds throughout their entire career, and many cultivators reach their natural plateau having accomplished only one or two.

When you accomplish a Deed, it becomes a permanent part of your legend and story. It's the GM's job to award Deeds when their Steps are completed, when the conditions are met, or when the GM judges that you as a player have accomplished something extraordinary. Deeds are not a checklist to be ground through; they are recognition that you and your character have done something the world will remember.

All Deeds are exceptional accomplishments, but not all Deeds are equal. They are broken down into Titles, Ordinary Deeds, Heroic Deeds, and Epic Deeds. Each requires a number of Steps necessary to accomplish, depending on the scope of the Perk granted for the accomplishment.
Steps are intentionally rare and difficult; most characters complete only a handful per tier of play. A Step is always one clear event, resolved at the table and marked immediately when it occurs.
A single Step for a Deed can be any one of the following:
These Steps are resolved through the dice system and are objective and unambiguous.

Accomplish a Check While Suffering 3 Challenges. The character succeeds on a check where their Bonus Dice are reduced by three ranks due to stacked Challenges. The GM must explicitly confirm the Challenges before the roll. Failure does not count, regardless of narrative outcome.

Accomplish a Check After Taking 3 Chances. The character voluntarily takes three Chances related to the same task, scene, or objective. After the third Chance is taken, the qualifying check must be attempted and succeeded. The Chances must meaningfully increase risk; cosmetic Chances do not qualify.

Have 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a Single Check. During one roll, three separate Bonus Dice explode (roll max, reroll, add). This may occur on an Action roll, Effect roll, or Crafting roll. Only one Step may be earned per roll, regardless of how many additional dice Burst.
These Steps represent long-term investment and are deterministic — they happen at a specific identifiable moment in the character's progression.
Skill Mastery. A specific skill line is increased to Rank 5 (Master). The skill must be relevant to the Deed being pursued. Each skill may only generate Step credit once per Deed.
Talent Suite. A selection of 3 seemingly unrelated talents are learned. These must connect to the Deed's final outcome or meaning.

Engine Threshold. Reaching a significant cultivation milestone — completing an Ordinary Class engine progression (3 Stars, 3 Circles, etc.), entering a Heroic class, or completing the Heroic class's full tree. The threshold must relate to the Deed's theme.
These Steps are granted by the GM but must be concrete and earned.

Major Objective. Completion of a major story objective, dungeon, boss encounter, expedition, or crisis. Typically the culmination of multiple scenes or sessions. The objective must be clearly relevant to the Deed's theme.

Unique Challenge. A singular, high-impact action tailored to the character. A unique challenge is personal, symbolic, or unprecedented. May overlap with a Major Objective but must highlight the individual character's role. Examples: holding a collapsing bridge alone so others may escape; parleying with an entity that cannot normally be reasoned with; using an untested artifact with catastrophic risk.
To prevent inflation and confusion, the following never qualify as Steps:

items: Ordinary successes, even if impressive.
Multiple similar actions in the same scene (only one Step max per scene).
Routine combat victories.
Passive bonuses, buffs, or preparation alone.
GM fiat without a clear triggering event.

blocks.html: If it cannot be clearly pointed to, written down, and agreed upon at the table, it is not a Step.
Once each Step is completed, the Deed is accomplished. This event is much like gaining a level — the character has a kind of epiphany and finds they have suddenly developed the Perk.
Deed Perks are the mechanical rewards granted when a character completes a Deed. The tier of the Deed determines both the number of Steps required and the maximum power of the Perk it may grant.
Titles represent a character becoming known for something specific or learning a narrow optimization through experience. Choose one:

items: Reduce the resource cost of one specific Reaction by 1.
Reduce the resource cost of one specific Technique or one Spell School by 1.
Reduce the resource cost of one specific Talent by 2.
Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with one specific spell or technique.
Add 1 Charge to all rolls with one Academic or Practical skill.
Learn one new spell or technique the character otherwise qualifies for.

blocks.html: Ordinary Deeds reflect consistent heroics and earned competence. They should noticeably improve performance without redefining the character's build or progression. Choose one:

items: Reduce the cost of all techniques or all spells by 1.
Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with all techniques or spells from one school.
Add 1 Charge to all rolls with one Combat or Elite skill.
Add 1 Charge to effect rolls or crafting results once per turn.
Open access to one General Talent List.
Increase Guard by 2.
Increase Ward by 2.
Increase Combat Movement Speed by 5 feet.
Improve Initiative by +1 Bonus Die.
Increase Mana or Vigor recovery by 1d4 during the recovery phase.
Increase elemental magic damage by 1 rank (single element).
Add 1 Charge to damage rolls against one specific enemy type.

blocks.html: Heroic Deeds define who the character is in the world. These perks are designed to shape play patterns and reinforce character identity. Choose one:

items: Add 1 Charge to all checks under one Ability Score.
Increase one Ability Score by 1d4.
Increase Health recovery by 1d6 during rest.
Increase Mana recovery by 1d10 during rest.
Increase one specific weapon type's damage by 1 rank.
Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with weapons and techniques against one enemy type.
Gain Heroic Durability against one damage type.
Improve an existing Talent's effect by 1 rank.
Unlock a Heroic Class entry path that the character would otherwise not qualify for (with GM approval).

blocks.html: Epic Deeds represent moments that reshape legend, fate, or the campaign itself. An Epic Perk may bend or override normal assumptions of play. Choose one:

items: Gain Immunity to one specific Condition (fear, sickened, petrified, etc.).
Gain one additional Action per turn.
Permanently increase Health recovery beyond normal limits.
Permanently increase Durability by a large fixed amount (e.g., +4d6).
Add 1 universal Charge to a core skill that applies unconditionally.
Gain a repeatable Epic benefit tied to defeating extreme-risk entities.
Unlock an Epic Class entry path.
Gain the benefits of two Ordinary Perks, or one Ordinary and two Title Perks.

blocks.html: Deeds are not "extra talents." They are earned transformations that reflect what the character has survived, mastered, or changed in the world. If a Title or Deed does not feel like a story worth retelling, it should not grant a powerful Perk. GMs and players should hold each other to this standard — the Deed is the story, not the bonus.

These basic Titles are found among the heroes and villains of Radiant Age. While not everyone earns a Title, those who do gain some renown. The list is not exhaustive — GMs are encouraged to create new Titles appropriate to their campaigns and characters.
Condition. Master the art of hitting precise shots at long range.
Step. Successfully make 10 Called Shots using Aim.
Perk. You are known as "Deadeye." Once per scene, if you are hidden or undetected, your first Aim-based attack gains 1 Charge.
Condition. Endure brutal environments without pause.
Step. Survive 3 Critical Challenge results on Athletics checks.
Perk. You are called "Stonefoot." You gain 1 Bonus Die on Athletics checks involving balance or moving through hazardous terrain.
Condition. Notice what others miss when it matters.
Step. Roll a Burst on an Awareness check that uncovers a hidden threat or trap.
Perk. You are known as "Farseeing." When ambushed, you gain +1 Initiative and may make a free Awareness check.
Condition. Calm or control a creature in distress.
Step. Succeed a VS 30 Beast Handling check with a wild or hostile creature.
Perk. You bear the title "Beastwhisper." You gain 1 Charge when using Beast Handling to soothe or lead wounded animals.
Condition. Craft something remarkable or symbolic.
Step. Craft a Masterwork item using Crafting with a Charged roll.
Perk. Known as "Forgehand," you may ignore 1 Challenge per crafting session if using a personal forge or trusted tools.
Condition. Deflect or evade attacks with inhuman precision.
Step. Deflect or Dodge 5 attacks in a single encounter.
Perk. You have earned the name "Mirrorblade." If you successfully Deflect an attack, your next Defense reaction this round gains 1 Charge.
Condition. Sway a powerful or hostile audience.
Step. Succeed a Social check VS 40 to change the mind of a hostile or neutral figure.
Perk. Known as "Silver Voice," you gain 1 Bonus Die on Social checks during formal negotiations or courtly events.
Condition. Unlock a piece of hidden lore or unravel a great puzzle.
Step. Use Studies or Occult to reveal a hidden truth, secret location, or ancient name.
Perk. You are called "Seeker." You gain 1 Bonus Die on Studies or Occult checks involving forgotten or forbidden knowledge.
Condition. Endure poison, sickness, or magical affliction without flinching.
Step. Pass 3 Resistance Reactions in a single day.
Perk. You have earned the name "Irongut." You recover 1 extra Vigor when succeeding at a Resistance check against illness, poison, or fatigue.
Condition. Steal something valuable or hidden under pressure.
Step. Successfully use Legerdemain in a VS 35 action during a high-risk encounter.
Perk. Known as "Ghosthand," when stealing or planting an object unnoticed, you may take 1 Chance without raising suspicion.
Condition. Stabilize the dying with skill alone.
Step. Succeed a VS 40 Medicine check to save a creature at 0 Health.
Perk. You are remembered as "Field Surgeon." You gain 1 Bonus Die on your first Medicine check in any scene where a creature is dying.
Condition. Use Menace to end a fight before it begins.
Step. Force an enemy to surrender or retreat using Menace in a contested roll.
Perk. Called "Iron Howl," you gain 1 Charge when using Menace to intimidate a foe of lesser rank or tier than you.
Condition. Commune with an unknown magical or spiritual entity.
Step. Use Occult in a VS 35 interaction with a being or force of the unknown.
Perk. Named "Wyrdspeaker," once per session you may reroll a single Occult check involving spirits, curses, or rituals.
Condition. Uncover or protect ancient or sensitive knowledge.
Step. Successfully use Studies to verify or translate a unique ancient script or relic.
Perk. Known as "Lorebinder," when referencing rare books or tomes, you gain 1 Bonus Die on Studies checks.
Condition. Track or survive an extended time in the wilderness.
Step. Use Survival to track a beast or enemy for three days or more.
Perk. With the name "Warden of the Wild," you may ignore 1 Challenge to Survival when navigating hostile terrain.
Condition. Defend yourself with your fists alone against a weapon-wielding foe.
Step. Win a combat encounter using only Unarmed attacks against armed enemies.
Perk. You have earned the name "Ironroot." Once per session, your first Unarmed attack gains 1 Charge if your hands are empty.
Condition. Leap into danger without a plan.
Step. Initiate a combat, investigation, or mission without preparing or equipping.
Perk. You are known as "Lucky Rookie." Once per session, reroll a failed Awareness or Athletics check when acting impulsively.
Condition. Successfully cast or craft a complex arcane effect without guidance.
Step. Learn a Lost or Forbidden Spell.
Perk. You bear the title "Magus." You gain 1 Bonus Die on your first roll involving arcane theory or arcane working each day.
Condition. Spare an enemy or show compassion when others would not.
Step. Forgo an execution or lethal blow in a critical moment.
Perk. Named "Merciful," you gain 1 Charge on Social checks involving forgiveness, healing, or reconciliation.
Condition. Lead a team through crisis, ensuring all survive.
Step. Complete a mission where all allies are wounded but none die.
Perk. You are "Commander." When issuing tactical instructions, your team receives a Charge on their next action roll.
Condition. Bring a powerful or corrupt figure to trial.
Step. Use Social, Menace, or Studies to expose a public injustice.
Perk. You are "The Just." You gain 1 Bonus Die to Social or Menace when appealing to law or righteous cause.
Condition. Defeat a skilled enemy in single combat without trickery.
Step. Win a duel against an enemy of higher level or risk without any Charges or ally assistance.
Perk. Called "The Bloodied," you gain 1 Charge on your first Light Weapons or Heavy Weapons checks made on your turn during 1-on-1 combat.
Condition. Complete a major scene while isolated from allies.
Step. Resolve a crisis or defeat a major foe while separated and unaided.
Perk. As "The Steel," once per day, you gain 1 Bonus Die when acting while alone.
Condition. Overcome a fate that should have killed you.
Step. Survive a Critical Hit or a scene that should have been fatal.
Perk. You are "The Immortal." The first time you drop to 0 Health each session, roll 1d6. On a 5-6, you recover 1 Health instead.
Condition. Stand your ground against overwhelming odds.
Step. Choose not to retreat in a combat encounter outnumbered 2:1 or worse.
Perk. As "The Courageous," your first Defense Reaction in a battle gains 1 Charge if you are outnumbered.
Condition. Choose personal sacrifice over gain.
Step. Give up a reward, position, or item to help someone else.
Perk. Named "The Generous," once per session, when refusing a reward, you may regain 1d10 Mana or Vigor.
Condition. Deal the final blow to an enemy resistant or immune to your normal damage type.
Step. Defeat an enemy using a suboptimal or inferior weapon.
Perk. As "Reaper," once per session, your first damage roll ignores Armor Rank.
Condition. Resist corruption, charm, or magical compulsion.
Step. Pass a VS 40 Resistance or Resolve check against mental control.
Perk. You are "Of Stone." You gain 1 Bonus Die on Resistance checks to maintain self-control.
Condition. Accept or use forbidden power for personal gain.
Step. Use a cursed item, dark cultivation, or taboo knowledge to overcome a major obstacle or enemy.
Perk. Titled "Infernal," you gain +1 Bonus Die on Occult checks related to forbidden powers and infernal beings.
Condition. Witness or survive a divine-level event or apocalyptic working.
Step. Remain conscious after taking Mythic-rank effect damage.
Perk. As "Heavenfall," you gain +1 Bonus Die on all Resistance checks against divine effects.
Condition. Complete a task that fulfills a prophecy or long-term goal.
Step. Resolve a major character arc or named Vision.
Perk. Bearing the name "Starbound," you gain 1 Bonus Die on your first Occult or Studies action each day if it furthers a personal or destined ideal.
Condition. Conceal your identity, intentions, or feelings in a major scene.
Step. Use Social or Legerdemain to pass through an event with your true goal hidden.
Perk. Called "Silver," you may ignore 1 Challenge on deception-related checks once per session.
Condition. Inspire others during a moment of utter hopelessness.
Step. Use Social or Entertain to lift morale or rally the party during a desperate encounter.
Perk. As "Sunforged," when making rallying speeches, your first Social check per day gains +1 Bonus Die.
Condition. Choose the fate of another and carry out the decision.
Step. Make and execute a significant moral judgment in-game.
Perk. Titled "Judge," you gain 1 Bonus Die on Social or Menace when speaking for others' fates.
Condition. Refuse a title, rank, or reward that grants power.
Step. Publicly decline an offered position or accolade.
Perk. You are "The Crownless." You gain 1 Charge on Reputation checks when dealing with rebels, commoners, or dissidents.
Condition. Complete a personal journey that changes who you are.
Step. Resolve a major bond, flaw, or origin arc.
Perk. Known as "The Ark." Once per campaign milestone, gain 1 Bonus Die on any roll directly tied to your completed journey.
Condition. Awaken your first Aura Star.
Step. Complete the cultivation needed to ignite your first Aura Star (Armsman Rank 1 talent, or equivalent).
Perk. Recognized as "Awakened," you gain +1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks involving aura, spiritual, or magical effects.
Condition. Inscribe your first Mana Circle within your heart.
Step. Complete the cultivation needed to inscribe your first Mana Circle (Mage Rank 1 talent, or equivalent).
Perk. Recognized as "Inscribed," you may once per scene, reduce the casting time of a Basic-rank spell by 1 Action (minimum 1 Action).
Condition. Move through a hostile region without trace.
Step. Travel through a guarded area or hostile territory using Stealth without being detected over the course of an entire scene or journey.
Perk. Called "Quietfoot," you may ignore 1 Challenge to Stealth checks when moving alone or with one other person.
Condition. Refine your cultivation through deliberate practice.
Step. Successfully meditate or train uninterrupted for 8 hours, then make a successful Resistance (Resolve) check VS 30 to advance a Talent's effect.
Perk. Recognized as "The Cultivator," you gain +1 Bonus Die to all checks related to deepening or maintaining your cultivation engines.
Condition. Form a binding agreement with a spiritual entity, sect, or named being.
Step. Successfully negotiate a Contract or formal Pact through Social, Occult, or Mystery-based interaction VS 35.
Perk. Named "Pact-Sworn," you gain 1 Charge on Social checks with parties whose terms you have honored.
Condition. Carry the weight of an oath, contract, or bond that was broken.
Step. Break a significant Contract, Pact, or sworn oath, then continue forward despite the consequence.
Perk. Known as "Severed," you gain 1 Bonus Die on Resistance checks against magical compulsion, but suffer 1 Challenge to Social checks with parties who know of your broken vow.
Condition. Lift a curse or hex placed on another.
Step. Successfully remove a Heroic-tier curse, hex, or magical compulsion from another creature using Occult or Miracle.
Perk. Called "Hexbreaker," you gain 1 Charge on Occult or Medicine checks to remove or treat magical afflictions.
Condition. Restore hope or sanity to someone who had lost it.
Step. Successfully use Social, Medicine, or Performance to restore a creature suffering Despair, Madness, or similar mental affliction.
Perk. Known as "Soulbright," your presence reduces by 1 Challenge any Resistance check made against fear, despair, or mental compulsion within 10 feet of you.
Condition. Glimpse the future and act on what you saw.
Step. Use Occult, Awareness, or Wyrd Witness Mystery to perceive a near-future event and successfully act to change its outcome.
Perk. Bearing the name "Wyrd-Touched," once per scene you may take 1 Chance on an action roll without suffering the normal Challenge.
Condition. Formally join and serve a recognized sect, school, or tradition.
Step. Complete the formal initiation rites and serve the sect through one major narrative arc.

Perk. Recognized as "Sect-Sworn," you gain +1 Bonus Die to Social checks when dealing with members of your sect and allied factions; the sect provides shelter and minor resources in regions where it has presence.
Condition. Break formal vows of sect or tradition and survive the consequences.
Step. Survive being formally cast out of a sect or breaking a formal oath publicly.
Perk. Known as "Forsworn," you may not return to your former sect, but you gain +1 Bonus Die to Social checks with rival or hostile sects to your former allegiance.
Condition. Cross difficult terrain that defeated others.
Step. Successfully complete a multi-day journey through hostile mountains, deep forest, deserts, or extreme cold using Survival.
Perk. Called "Mountain Walker," you gain 1 Charge on Survival checks involving terrain and weather, and you ignore 1 Challenge to long-distance travel.
Condition. Form a Contract or Bond with a named spirit, beast, or eldritch entity.
Step. Successfully establish a formal Contract, Bond, or pact through Mystery, Occult, or related cultivation.
Perk. Titled "Maker of Spirits," you gain +1 Bonus Die to Occult and Awareness checks involving spirits, the unseen, and related phenomena.
Condition. Successfully cultivate a second class.
Step. Multiclass into a second Ordinary Class and complete its Rank 1 starting talents.
Perk. Recognized as "Of Two Paths," you gain 1 Bonus Die to one Crafting or Studies check related to the integration of your two classes once per session.
Condition. Maintain a binding silence over an extended period.
Step. Hold a formal vow of silence for an entire session of play without breaking it under significant pressure.
Perk. Called "Of Silence," once per session you may immediately succeed a Resistance check against a verbal compulsion, deception, or magical command that requires you to respond verbally.
Condition. Travel and survive on the open road.
Step. Complete a trans-regional journey through three or more major settlements as the party's principal navigator.
Perk. Known as "Roadwise," you gain 1 Bonus Die to Survival, Awareness, and Social checks in unfamiliar settlements and along travel routes.
Condition. Visibly represent a cause through formal rite or trial.
Step. Successfully fulfill a sworn duty visible to all parties — carrying a banner through battle, defending a sacred ground, or escorting a relic.
Perk. Titled "Standard Bearer," allies within 30 feet of you gain +1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks against fear and despair.
Condition. Master a weapon to its highest practiced expression.
Step. Reach Master rank in any Combat skill (Light Weapons, Heavy Weapons, Ranged Weapons, Unarmed, or Defense).
Perk. Called "Adept of Steel," you may add 1 Charge to your first weapon attack each scene with your specialty weapon.
Condition. Master an academic discipline to its highest practiced expression.
Step. Reach Master rank in any Academic skill (Studies, Awareness, Memory, Reason, or Occult).
Perk. Called "Adept of Mind," you gain 1 Bonus Die to all rolls related to your mastered academic field.
Condition. Master a spiritual or social discipline to its highest practiced expression.
Step. Reach Master rank in any Social or Esoteric skill (Social, Entertain, Beast Handling, or Medicine).
Perk. Called "Adept of Spirit," you gain 1 Bonus Die to all rolls related to your mastered field.
Condition. Master a physical discipline to its highest practiced expression.
Step. Reach Master rank in any Practical skill (Athletics, Stealth, Survival, Crafting, Legerdemain, or Piloting).
Perk. Called "Adept of Body," you gain 1 Bonus Die to all rolls related to your mastered field.
Condition. Carry a memory or knowledge that should not have been carried.
Step. Successfully retain knowledge of a Forbidden or Mythic-tier event or working that others around you have forgotten or are unaware of.
Perk. Known as "Burdened," you gain 1 Bonus Die to Resistance checks against forgetting, amnesia, or mind-altering effects.
Condition. Personally witness an event of legendary significance.
Step. Be present as a witness for the completion of an Epic Deed by another character, or witness a confirmed legendary event in the campaign's world.
Perk. Titled "Witness," you may once per session add 1 Bonus Die to a recall, narration, or testimony check about your witnessed event.

Below are Deeds that every character is capable of accomplishing, alone or as part of a party of adventurers. Each Deed is presented with its required Steps and a chosen Perk for completion. GMs and players may negotiate the specific Perk from the appropriate Perk list when the Deed is completed.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Master the art of defense.
Step 1. Successfully Dodge an attack with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Defense check.
Perk. Reduce the resource cost of all Dodge reactions by 1.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate proficiency in handling animals.
Step 1. Successfully calm a wild animal with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Ride or train a difficult mount after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all rolls with one Combat or Elite skill of your choice.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Excel in training, controlling, or partnering with beasts.
Step 1. Increase Beast Handling to Expert rank.
Step 2. Calm a creature with a Risk of 18 or higher after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Beast Handling check.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls against Beasts. Unlocks access to the Tamer Heroic class if other prerequisites are met.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Refuse to accept your fate.
Step 1. Succeed on any action roll against a VS 50 with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Fail an action with a VS less than 35 after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Roll a 1 on your action dice, but still succeed on the action roll.
Perk. Unlock the "Fate's Threads" General Talent line, or gain access to the Wyrd Witness Mystery if not already possessed.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show extraordinary diplomatic skills.
Step 1. Resolve a major conflict through diplomacy with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Form an alliance after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Social check.
Perk. Increase your Empathy Ability Score by 1d4.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Master the art of ranged combat.
Step 1. Increase Ranged Weapons to Master rank.
Step 2. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Aim check.
Step 3. Hit a hidden target from long range with 3 Challenges.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with all techniques or spells from one school you have mastered.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Strike fear into the hearts of enemies.
Step 1. Intimidate a powerful enemy with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Learn the Social Talent Terrify.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Menace check.
Perk. Gain Immunity to the Frightened condition.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Provide crucial medical aid.
Step 1. Successfully treat an injury while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Use medicinal items to recover at least 20 Durability with a single action.
Perk. Increase recovery of your patient's Health by 1d6 during rest.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show mastery with heavy weapons.
Step 1. Increase Heavy Weapons to Master rank.
Step 2. Break through a defense after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Heavy Weapons check.
Perk. Increase your chosen specific type of heavy weapon's damage by 1 rank when wielding it.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show extraordinary physical prowess.
Step 1. Perform a feat of strength with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Complete a marathon-distance journey after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Athletics check.
Perk. Increase Strength, Stamina, or Agility Ability Score by 1d4.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Notice crucial details.
Step 1. Successfully spot a hidden object or enemy with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Make a successful investigation check after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all Awareness checks.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate academic prowess.
Step 1. Successfully complete a research project with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Learn the Able Learner Talent.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all Studies and Memory checks involving research, recall, or learning.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show mastery of seamanship and sailing.
Step 1. Successfully complete a maritime route with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Perform a daring sea route after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Piloting (Ship) check.
Perk. Add +1 Bonus Die to all Piloting and Navigation checks at sea.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show mastery in unarmed combat.
Step 1. Increase Unarmed to Master rank.
Step 2. Perform a flawless combination of three techniques after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Unarmed check.
Perk. Increase your Strength Ability Score by 1d4.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Demonstrate unparalleled skill in alchemy.
Step 1. Successfully create an alchemical item of Heroic quality.
Step 2. Learn the Runecrafting Talent.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Occult crafting check.
Perk. Add 1d10 to Mana Recovery during a rest.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Demonstrate exceptional craftsmanship.
Step 1. Purchase the Crafting Artisan talent.
Step 2. Craft a Legendary-quality item after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Crafting check.
Perk. Increase the quality of all items you craft under your specialty by 1 rank.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Achieve widespread fame through your performances.
Step 1. Perform for a large audience with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Use a performance to defuse a tense situation after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Entertain check.
Perk. Improve Initiative by +1 Bonus Die.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Demonstrate extraordinary medical skill.
Step 1. Increase Medicine to Master rank.
Step 2. Cure a rare affliction after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Medicine check.
Perk. Increase Resolve Ability Score by 1d4.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Uncover hidden truths.
Step 1. Increase Awareness to Master rank.
Step 2. Successfully Investigate a crime scene or event after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Awareness check.
Perk. Add 1 Charge with all Reason-based skill checks.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Master the use of elemental and arcane magic.
Step 1. Increase Occult to Master rank.
Step 2. Defeat an opponent with a single elemental spell after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Occult casting check.
Perk. Increase the damage of one chosen element's spells by 1 rank when casting those spells.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Achieve legendary status as a thief.
Step 1. Steal a Legendary-quality item.
Step 2. Infiltrate a secure location after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Increase Legerdemain to Master rank.
Perk. Add 1 Charge on all Legerdemain checks in dim light or darkness.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Demonstrate unmatched survival skills.
Step 1. Survive extreme conditions of heat or cold with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Track a dangerous predator after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Survival check.
Perk. Gain Heroic Durability against either fire or cold damage.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Achieve mastery with swords.
Step 1. Increase Light Weapons to Master rank.
Step 2. Perform a flawless attack after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Light Weapons check.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with all swordsmanship techniques.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Prove you are a navigator of great skill.
Step 1. Successfully navigate a difficult course using Piloting or Survival with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Increase Piloting or Survival with celestial navigation to Expert.
Perk. Improve Initiative by +1 Bonus Die when piloting or navigating.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Excel in physical activities.
Step 1. Successfully make an Athletics check with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Complete a climb or swim check after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Increase combat Movement Speed by 5 feet.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Become attuned to the arcane.
Step 1. Successfully cast a spell with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Cast a complex spell after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Reduce the Mana cost of Incantare Spells by 1 (minimum 1).
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Craft useful potions and elixirs.
Step 1. Successfully craft a Heroic-quality potion.
Step 2. Create an alchemical item after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to potion and elixir crafting results.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate strength with heavy weapons.
Step 1. Successfully hit with a Power Attack while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Defeat an enemy in a single blow with heavy weapons after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all damage Bonus Dice with Power Attack with heavy weapons.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate skill in unarmed combat.
Step 1. Successfully land an unarmed strike while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Defeat an opponent with a single unarmed attack after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with one martial arts Technique of your choice.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Show dexterity with light weapons.
Step 1. Successfully hit an enemy with a light weapon while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Defeat an enemy with a single stroke after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with one Light weapon of your choice.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Achieve recognition for academic contributions.
Step 1. Learn the Academic Authority Talent.
Step 2. Solve an academic mystery or present groundbreaking research after taking 3 Chances.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Studies check.
Perk. Increase your Memory Ability Score by 1d4.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Perform feats of stealth and thievery.
Step 1. Successfully pickpocket or lockpick with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Evade detection after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all Sneak Attack checks.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate precision with ranged weapons.
Step 1. Successfully hit a target from long range while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Make a successful ranged attack after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to attack rolls with one specific ranged weapon or technique.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Demonstrate persuasive abilities.
Step 1. Successfully persuade or deceive someone after taking 3 Chances.
Step 2. Use seduction or manipulation in combat successfully with 3 Challenges.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all Social skill rolls where you can speak.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Create or repair items with precision.
Step 1. Repair a broken item with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Successfully craft a useful item of Heroic quality.
Perk. You gain 1 Charge when making Crafting checks within your specialty.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Captivate an audience with your performance.
Step 1. Successfully perform a piece of music or storytelling with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Distract an enemy after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Add 1 Charge to all Entertain skill checks with your specialty.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Show unparalleled defensive skills.
Step 1. Block or deflect a powerful attack against an opponent who has taken 3 Chances.
Step 2. Successfully defend against an attack while suffering 3 Challenges.
Step 3. Purchase the Defense Invictus talent.
Perk. Increase Guard by 2.
Ordinary Deed
Condition. Show proficiency in survival skills.
Step 1. Successfully navigate a dangerous terrain with 3 Challenges.
Step 2. Forage for food and water after taking 3 Chances.
Perk. Increase recovery of Vigor by 1d4 during the recovery phase.

The following Deeds are specifically tied to cultivation engine progression, elite class commitments, and the major milestones that mark a character's transition between tiers. These Deeds reward the cultivator who has truly committed to their path — not merely accumulated levels, but genuinely accomplished the transformative work that the system recognizes as worthy of mythical legend.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Cultivate Aura Stars to a meaningful threshold and prove their potency in combat.
Step 1. Reach 3 Aura Stars (Armsman Rank 5 talent, or equivalent through other paths).
Step 2. Defeat an enemy of equal or higher level using an Aura-empowered attack in a single decisive blow.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single attack roll using an Aura Technique.
Perk. Gain Heroic Durability against one damage type of your choosing, and your Aura Star count counts as 1 higher for the purposes of all Heroic Class prerequisites and Aura-scaling effects.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Cultivate Mana Circles to a meaningful threshold and prove their potency in casting.
Step 1. Reach 3 Mana Circles (Mage Rank 5 talent, or equivalent through other paths).
Step 2. Successfully cast an Expert-rank Spell in combat against an enemy of equal or higher level.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single casting check.
Perk. Your Mana Circle count counts as 1 higher for the purposes of all Heroic Class prerequisites. Increase your Mana recovery by 1d10 during rest.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Cultivate Edge Dice to their full Ordinary expression and prove their tactical reach.
Step 1. Reach 5 Edge Dice at d10 (Rogue Rank 5 talent, or equivalent through other paths).
Step 2. Successfully complete a scene using Sharp Strike, Sharp Move, and Sharp Defense in coordinated tactical play within a single combat.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on a single Edge Action of any type.
Perk. Gain 1 additional Edge Die to your starting pool (now 6 d10 dice at maximum), and your Edge Dice count counts as 1 higher for Heroic class prerequisites.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Reach the apex of an Ordinary-tier Mystery cultivation.
Step 1. Cultivate a Mystery to Perfect tier (Spiritualist Rank 5 capstone, or equivalent through long-arc cultivation).
Step 2. Use the Mystery's Perfect-tier ability to substantially shape the outcome of a major narrative event.
Step 3. Achieve 3 Bonus Dice Burst on an Occult or Resistance check involving your Perfect Mystery.
Perk. Your Mystery's Perfect-tier ability becomes usable an additional time per scene, and you gain Heroic-tier recognition among other Spiritualists and divine practitioners.
Heroic Deed
Condition. Cross the Heroic threshold and commit fully to your chosen specialty.
Step 1. Enter a Heroic Class for the first time, taking its Rank 1 entry talent.
Step 2. Purchase 3 talents within your Heroic Class's Elite Tree.
Step 3. Use your Heroic Class's signature ability to substantially shape the outcome of a major encounter or narrative event.
Perk. Improve an existing Talent's effect within your Heroic Class by 1 rank, or unlock a single Rank 4 talent in your Heroic Class without requiring its normal Rank 3 prerequisite (taken once).
Heroic Deed
